{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01UU5jJjNn3aCRn9HhbErJUP","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→#!/usr/bin/env python3\n     2→\"\"\"\n     3→Terminal-Bench Claude Code Hooks (unified script).\n     4→\n     5→Handles all three hook events via stdin payload inspection:\n     6→- PreToolUse: tracks state, always allows\n     7→- PostToolUse: tracks errors, verification results, loop detection\n     8→- Stop: blocks exit if verification hasn't been attempted\n     9→\n    10→Protocol:\n    11→  Input: JSON via stdin\n    12→  Output: JSON via stdout wrapped in {\"hookSpecificOutput\": {...}}\n    13→\n    14→State persisted in /tmp/.tbench_state.json across invocations.\n    15→\"\"\"\n    16→\n    17→from __future__ import annotations\n    18→\n    19→import json\n    20→import sys\n    21→import time\n    22→from pathlib import Path\n    23→\n    24→STATE_FILE = Path(\"/tmp/.tbench_state.json\")\n    25→\n    26→# Patterns that indicate the agent is running verification tests\n    27→VERIFICATION_PATTERNS = [\n    28→    \"tests/test.sh\",\n    29→    \"tests/test_\",\n    30→    \"pytest tests/\",\n    31→    \"pytest -v\",\n    32→    \"python -m pytest\",\n    33→    \"python3 -m pytest\",\n    34→    \"make test\",\n    35→    \"cargo test\",\n    36→    \"npm test\",\n    37→    \"go test\",\n    38→    \"bash test\",\n    39→    \"./test.sh\",\n    40→    \"ctest\",\n    41→]\n    42→\n    43→# Patterns that indicate test success\n    44→SUCCESS_PATTERNS = [\n    45→    \"passed\",\n    46→    \" ok\",\n    47→    \"0 failed\",\n    48→    \"tests passed\",\n    49→    \"all tests\",\n    50→    \"PASS\",\n    51→    \"OK (\",\n    52→    \"0 errors\",\n    53→    \"Build succeeded\",\n    54→    \"All checks passed\",\n    55→]\n    56→\n    57→# Patterns that indicate errors in command output\n    58→ERROR_PATTERNS = [\n    59→    \"error:\",\n    60→    \"Error:\",\n    61→    \"ERROR:\",\n    62→    \"traceback\",\n    63→    \"Traceback\",\n    64→    \"FAILED\",\n    65→    \"not found\",\n    66→    \"No such file\",\n    67→    \"permission denied\",\n    68→    \"Permission denied\",\n    69→    \"command not found\",\n    70→    \"ModuleNotFoundError\",\n    71→    \"ImportError\",\n    72→    \"SyntaxError\",\n    73→    \"NameError\",\n    74→    \"TypeError\",\n    75→    \"ValueError\",\n    76→    \"FileNotFoundError\",\n    77→    \"ConnectionRefusedError\",\n    78→    \"OSError\",\n    79→    \"RuntimeError\",\n    80→    \"segmentation fault\",\n    81→    \"Segmentation fault\",\n    82→    \"core dumped\",\n    83→    \"killed\",\n    84→    \"Killed\",\n    85→    \"Critical error:\",\n    86→    \"AssertionError\",\n    87→]\n    88→\n    89→# Common build artifacts that should be cleaned up\n    90→BUILD_ARTIFACT_PATTERNS = [\n    91→    \"a.out\",\n    92→    \"*.o\",\n    93→    \"*.obj\",\n    94→    \"*.exe\",\n    95→    \"*.dll\",\n    96→    \"*.so\",\n    97→    \"*.dylib\",\n    98→    \"*.pyc\",\n    99→    \"__pycache__\",\n   100→    \".DS_Store\",\n   101→    \"*.swp\",\n   102→    \"*.swo\",\n   103→    \"core\",\n   104→    \"*.core\",\n   105→]\n   106→\n   107→# Deliverable patterns - files that should NOT be cleaned\n   108→DELIVERABLE_PATTERNS = [\n   109→    \"main.py.c\",\n   110→    \"main.c.py\",\n   111→    \"move.txt\",\n   112→    \"regex.txt\",\n   113→    \"response.txt\",\n   114→    \"solution.py\",\n   115→    \"answer.txt\",\n   116→    \"output.json\",\n   117→    \"result.csv\",\n   118→]\n   119→\n   120→\n   121→def load_state() -> dict:\n   122→    if STATE_FILE.exists():\n   123→        try:\n   124→            return json.loads(STATE_FILE.read_text())\n   125→        except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):\n   126→            pass\n   127→    return {\n   128→        \"start_time\": time.time(),\n   129→        \"turn_count\": 0,\n   130→        \"file_edits\": {},\n   131→        \"verification_attempted\": False,\n   132→        \"verification_passed\": False,\n   133→        \"consecutive_failures\": 0,\n   134→        \"last_error_sig\": \"\",\n   135→        \"stop_block_count\": 0,\n   136→    }\n   137→\n   138→\n   139→def save_state(state: dict) -> None:\n   140→    try:\n   141→        STATE_FILE.write_text(json.dumps(state))\n   142→    except OSError:\n   143→        pass\n   144→\n   145→\n   146→def emit(payload: dict) -> None:\n   147→    json.dump(payload, sys.stdout)\n   148→\n   149→\n   150→def emit_allow(reason: str = \"\") -> None:\n   151→    emit({\n   152→        \"hookSpecificOutput\": {\n   153→            \"hookEventName\": \"PreToolUse\",\n   154→            \"permissionDecision\": \"allow\",\n   155→            \"permissionDecisionReason\": reason,\n   156→        }\n   157→    })\n   158→\n   159→\n   160→def emit_notification(event: str, message: str) -> None:\n   161→    emit({\n   162→        \"hookSpecificOutput\": {\n   163→            \"hookEventName\": event,\n   164→            \"notification\": message,\n   165→        }\n   166→    })\n   167→\n   168→\n   169→def is_verification_command(cmd: str) -> bool:\n   170→    \"\"\"Check if a command is running verification tests.\"\"\"\n   171→    return any(pattern in cmd for pattern in VERIFICATION_PATTERNS)\n   172→\n   173→\n   174→def detect_hook_type(data: dict) -> str:\n   175→    if \"tool_output\" in data:\n   176→        return \"PostToolUse\"\n   177→    if \"tool_name\" in data:\n   178→        return \"PreToolUse\"\n   179→    return \"Stop\"\n   180→\n   181→\n   182→def handle_pretool(data: dict) -> None:\n   183→    state = load_state()\n   184→    state[\"turn_count\"] = state.get(\"turn_count\", 0) + 1\n   185→\n   186→    tool_name = data.get(\"tool_name\", \"\")\n   187→    tool_input = data.get(\"tool_input\", {})\n   188→\n   189→    # Track file edits for loop detection\n   190→    if tool_name in (\"Edit\", \"Write\"):\n   191→        filepath = tool_input.get(\"file_path\", \"unknown\")\n   192→        state[\"file_edits\"][filepath] = state[\"file_edits\"].get(filepath, 0) + 1\n   193→\n   194→    # Track verification attempts\n   195→    if tool_name == \"Bash\":\n   196→        cmd = tool_input.get(\"command\", \"\")\n   197→        if is_verification_command(cmd):\n   198→            if not state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n   199→                # Reset stop counter when entering verification phase\n   200→                state[\"stop_block_count\"] = 0\n   201→            state[\"verification_attempted\"] = True\n   202→\n   203→    save_state(state)\n   204→    emit_allow(\"[tbench]\")\n   205→\n   206→\n   207→def handle_posttool(data: dict) -> None:\n   208→    state = load_state()\n   209→\n   210→    tool_name = data.get(\"tool_name\", \"\")\n   211→    tool_output = str(data.get(\"tool_output\", \"\"))\n   212→\n   213→    if tool_name == \"Bash\":\n   214→        # Detect errors\n   215→        has_error = any(err in tool_output for err in ERROR_PATTERNS)\n   216→\n   217→        if has_error:\n   218→            sig = tool_output[:200]\n   219→            if sig == state.get(\"last_error_sig\", \"\"):\n   220→                state[\"consecutive_failures\"] = state.get(\"consecutive_failures\", 0) + 1\n   221→            else:\n   222→                state[\"consecutive_failures\"] = 1\n   223→                state[\"last_error_sig\"] = sig\n   224→        else:\n   225→            state[\"consecutive_failures\"] = 0\n   226→\n   227→        # Detect verification success\n   228→        if state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n   229→            if any(kw in tool_output for kw in SUCCESS_PATTERNS):\n   230→                state[\"verification_passed\"] = True\n   231→\n   232→    save_state(state)\n   233→\n   234→    # Emit warnings for loops\n   235→    notifications = []\n   236→\n   237→    for filepath, count in state.get(\"file_edits\", {}).items():\n   238→        if count >= 4:\n   239→            notifications.append(\n   240→                f\"[tbench] WARNING: {filepath} edited {count} times. \"\n   241→                \"STOP editing this file. Re-read the requirements and try \"\n   242→                \"a fundamentally different approach.\"\n   243→            )\n   244→\n   245→    if state.get(\"consecutive_failures\", 0) >= 3:\n   246→        notifications.append(\n   247→            f\"[tbench] WARNING: {state['consecutive_failures']} consecutive failures \"\n   248→            \"with the same error. Your current approach is wrong. Switch strategy entirely.\"\n   249→        )\n   250→\n   251→    turn_count = state.get(\"turn_count\", 0)\n   252→    if turn_count >= 100 and not state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n   253→        notifications.append(\n   254→            f\"[tbench] CRITICAL: Turn {turn_count}/200 and you haven't run verification yet. \"\n   255→            \"STOP what you're doing. Run the tests NOW. python3\n\"\"\"\nTerminal-Bench Claude Code Hooks (unified script).\n\nHandles all three hook events via stdin payload inspection:\n- PreToolUse: tracks state, always allows\n- PostToolUse: tracks errors, verification results, loop detection\n- Stop: blocks exit if verification hasn't been attempted\n\nProtocol:\n  Input: JSON via stdin\n  Output: JSON via stdout wrapped in {\"hookSpecificOutput\": {...}}\n\nState persisted in /tmp/.tbench_state.json across invocations.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nimport sys\nimport time\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nSTATE_FILE = Path(\"/tmp/.tbench_state.json\")\n\n# Patterns that indicate the agent is running verification tests\nVERIFICATION_PATTERNS = [\n    \"tests/test.sh\",\n    \"tests/test_\",\n    \"pytest tests/\",\n    \"pytest -v\",\n    \"python -m pytest\",\n    \"python3 -m pytest\",\n    \"make test\",\n    \"cargo test\",\n    \"npm test\",\n    \"go test\",\n    \"bash test\",\n    \"./test.sh\",\n    \"ctest\",\n]\n\n# Patterns that indicate test success\nSUCCESS_PATTERNS = [\n    \"passed\",\n    \" ok\",\n    \"0 failed\",\n    \"tests passed\",\n    \"all tests\",\n    \"PASS\",\n    \"OK (\",\n    \"0 errors\",\n    \"Build succeeded\",\n    \"All checks passed\",\n]\n\n# Patterns that indicate errors in command output\nERROR_PATTERNS = [\n    \"error:\",\n    \"Error:\",\n    \"ERROR:\",\n    \"traceback\",\n    \"Traceback\",\n    \"FAILED\",\n    \"not found\",\n    \"No such file\",\n    \"permission denied\",\n    \"Permission denied\",\n    \"command not found\",\n    \"ModuleNotFoundError\",\n    \"ImportError\",\n    \"SyntaxError\",\n    \"NameError\",\n    \"TypeError\",\n    \"ValueError\",\n    \"FileNotFoundError\",\n    \"ConnectionRefusedError\",\n    \"OSError\",\n    \"RuntimeError\",\n    \"segmentation fault\",\n    \"Segmentation fault\",\n    \"core dumped\",\n    \"killed\",\n    \"Killed\",\n    \"Critical error:\",\n    \"AssertionError\",\n]\n\n# Common build artifacts that should be cleaned up\nBUILD_ARTIFACT_PATTERNS = [\n    \"a.out\",\n    \"*.o\",\n    \"*.obj\",\n    \"*.exe\",\n    \"*.dll\",\n    \"*.so\",\n    \"*.dylib\",\n    \"*.pyc\",\n    \"__pycache__\",\n    \".DS_Store\",\n    \"*.swp\",\n    \"*.swo\",\n    \"core\",\n    \"*.core\",\n]\n\n# Deliverable patterns - files that should NOT be cleaned\nDELIVERABLE_PATTERNS = [\n    \"main.py.c\",\n    \"main.c.py\",\n    \"move.txt\",\n    \"regex.txt\",\n    \"response.txt\",\n    \"solution.py\",\n    \"answer.txt\",\n    \"output.json\",\n    \"result.csv\",\n]\n\n\ndef load_state() -> dict:\n    if STATE_FILE.exists():\n        try:\n            return json.loads(STATE_FILE.read_text())\n        except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):\n            pass\n    return {\n        \"start_time\": time.time(),\n        \"turn_count\": 0,\n        \"file_edits\": {},\n        \"verification_attempted\": False,\n        \"verification_passed\": False,\n        \"consecutive_failures\": 0,\n        \"last_error_sig\": \"\",\n        \"stop_block_count\": 0,\n    }\n\n\ndef save_state(state: dict) -> None:\n    try:\n        STATE_FILE.write_text(json.dumps(state))\n    except OSError:\n        pass\n\n\ndef emit(payload: dict) -> None:\n    json.dump(payload, sys.stdout)\n\n\ndef emit_allow(reason: str = \"\") -> None:\n    emit({\n        \"hookSpecificOutput\": {\n            \"hookEventName\": \"PreToolUse\",\n            \"permissionDecision\": \"allow\",\n            \"permissionDecisionReason\": reason,\n        }\n    })\n\n\ndef emit_notification(event: str, message: str) -> None:\n    emit({\n        \"hookSpecificOutput\": {\n            \"hookEventName\": event,\n            \"notification\": message,\n        }\n    })\n\n\ndef is_verification_command(cmd: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Check if a command is running verification tests.\"\"\"\n    return any(pattern in cmd for pattern in VERIFICATION_PATTERNS)\n\n\ndef detect_hook_type(data: dict) -> str:\n    if \"tool_output\" in data:\n        return \"PostToolUse\"\n    if \"tool_name\" in data:\n        return \"PreToolUse\"\n    return \"Stop\"\n\n\ndef handle_pretool(data: dict) -> None:\n    state = load_state()\n    state[\"turn_count\"] = state.get(\"turn_count\", 0) + 1\n\n    tool_name = data.get(\"tool_name\", \"\")\n    tool_input = data.get(\"tool_input\", {})\n\n    # Track file edits for loop detection\n    if tool_name in (\"Edit\", \"Write\"):\n        filepath = tool_input.get(\"file_path\", \"unknown\")\n        state[\"file_edits\"][filepath] = state[\"file_edits\"].get(filepath, 0) + 1\n\n    # Track verification attempts\n    if tool_name == \"Bash\":\n        cmd = tool_input.get(\"command\", \"\")\n        if is_verification_command(cmd):\n            if not state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n                # Reset stop counter when entering verification phase\n                state[\"stop_block_count\"] = 0\n            state[\"verification_attempted\"] = True\n\n    save_state(state)\n    emit_allow(\"[tbench]\")\n\n\ndef handle_posttool(data: dict) -> None:\n    state = load_state()\n\n    tool_name = data.get(\"tool_name\", \"\")\n    tool_output = str(data.get(\"tool_output\", \"\"))\n\n    if tool_name == \"Bash\":\n        # Detect errors\n        has_error = any(err in tool_output for err in ERROR_PATTERNS)\n\n        if has_error:\n            sig = tool_output[:200]\n            if sig == state.get(\"last_error_sig\", \"\"):\n                state[\"consecutive_failures\"] = state.get(\"consecutive_failures\", 0) + 1\n            else:\n                state[\"consecutive_failures\"] = 1\n                state[\"last_error_sig\"] = sig\n        else:\n            state[\"consecutive_failures\"] = 0\n\n        # Detect verification success\n        if state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n            if any(kw in tool_output for kw in SUCCESS_PATTERNS):\n                state[\"verification_passed\"] = True\n\n    save_state(state)\n\n    # Emit warnings for loops\n    notifications = []\n\n    for filepath, count in state.get(\"file_edits\", {}).items():\n        if count >= 4:\n            notifications.append(\n                f\"[tbench] WARNING: {filepath} edited {count} times. \"\n                \"STOP editing this file. Re-read the requirements and try \"\n                \"a fundamentally different approach.\"\n            )\n\n    if state.get(\"consecutive_failures\", 0) >= 3:\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] WARNING: {state['consecutive_failures']} consecutive failures \"\n            \"with the same error. Your current approach is wrong. Switch strategy entirely.\"\n        )\n\n    turn_count = state.get(\"turn_count\", 0)\n    if turn_count >= 100 and not state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] CRITICAL: Turn {turn_count}/200 and you haven't run verification yet. \"\n            \"STOP what you're doing. Run the tests NOW. Fix only what fails.\"\n        )\n    elif turn_count >= 40 and not state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] WARNING: Turn {turn_count}/200. Run verification NOW to check progress. \"\n            \"Early feedback prevents wasted turns.\"\n        )\n    elif turn_count >= 150:\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] WARNING: Turn {turn_count}/200. You are running low on turns. \"\n            \"Wrap up — ensure tests pass and stop.\"\n        )\n    elif turn_count >= 120 and not state.get(\"verification_passed\"):\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] WARNING: Turn {turn_count}/200. Tests not yet passing. \"\n            \"Simplify your approach. A minimal passing solution beats a failed ambitious one.\"\n        )\n\n    if notifications:\n        emit_notification(\"PostToolUse\", \" | \".join(notifications))\n    else:\n        emit({})\n\n\ndef handle_stop(data: dict) -> None:\n    state = load_state()\n\n    if not state.get(\"verification_attempted\", False):\n        state[\"stop_block_count\"] = state.get(\"stop_block_count\", 0) + 1\n        save_state(state)\n\n        # After 3 blocks, let it go (avoid infinite loop)\n        if state[\"stop_block_count\"] >= 3:\n            emit({})\n            return\n\n        emit_notification(\n            \"Stop\",\n            \"[tbench] BLOCKED: You have NOT run the test suite. \"\n            \"You MUST verify before stopping. Run: \"\n            \"cd /app && bash tests/test.sh \"\n            \"OR: cd /app && python3 -m pytest tests/ -v\"\n        )\n    elif not state.get(\"verification_passed\", False):\n        state[\"stop_block_count\"] = state.get(\"stop_block_count\", 0) + 1\n        save_state(state)\n\n        if state[\"stop_block_count\"] >= 2:\n            emit({})\n            return\n\n        emit_notification(\n            \"Stop\",\n            \"[tbench] Tests were run but appear to have failed. \"\n            \"Review the test output, fix issues, and re-run verification. \"\n            \"If you believe tests actually passed, you may stop.\"\n        )\n    else:\n        emit({})\n\n\ndef main() -> None:\n    try:\n        data = json.load(sys.stdin)\n    except (json.JSONDecodeError, EOFError):\n        data = {}\n\n    hook_type = detect_hook_type(data)\n\n    if hook_type == \"PreToolUse\":\n        handle_pretool(data)\n    elif hook_type == \"PostToolUse\":\n        handle_posttool(data)\n    elif hook_type == \"Stop\":\n        handle_stop(data)\n    else:\n        emit({})\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    main()\n","numLines":336,"startLine":1,"totalLines":336}}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01Vxai3AxnkseovEZHBEkmMt","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01VqcyqVqyMeXaNDtYRFEyEL","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/app/ars.R","content":"###############################################################################\n# Adaptive Rejection Sampling (ARS)\n# Based on Gilks, W.R. & Wild, P. (1992)\n# \"Adaptive Rejection Sampling for Gibbs Sampling\"\n# Journal of the Royal Statistical Society: Series C, 41(2), 337-348.\n###############################################################################\n\n# --- Auxiliary / Module Functions ---\n\n#' Evaluate the log of a density function safely\n#' @param f density function (possibly unnormalized)\n#' @param x point(s) at which to evaluate\n#' @return log(f(x)), with protection against f(x) <= 0\neval_log_density <- function(f, x) {\n  fx <- f(x)\n  if (any(is.na(fx) | is.nan(fx))) {\n    stop(\"Density function returned NA/NaN.\")\n  }\n  if (any(fx < 0)) {\n    stop(\"Density function returned negative value.\")\n  }\n  result <- rep(-Inf, length(fx))\n  pos <- fx > 0\n  result[pos] <- log(fx[pos])\n  return(result)\n}\n\n#' Check log-concavity at three consecutive abscissae\n#' If h(x) = log(f(x)), then log-concavity requires:\n#'   (h(x2) - h(x1))/(x2 - x1) >= (h(x3) - h(x2))/(x3 - x2)\n#' for all x1 < x2 < x3\n#' @param abscissae sorted vector of x values\n#' @param h_vals corresponding log-density values h(x)\n#' @return TRUE if log-concave, else stops with error\ncheck_log_concavity <- function(abscissae, h_vals) {\n  n <- length(abscissae)\n  if (n < 3) return(TRUE)\n  for (i in 1:(n - 2)) {\n    x1 <- abscissae[i];   h1 <- h_vals[i]\n    x2 <- abscissae[i+1]; h2 <- h_vals[i+1]\n    x3 <- abscissae[i+2]; h3 <- h_vals[i+2]\n    slope_left  <- (h2 - h1) / (x2 - x1)\n    slope_right <- (h3 - h2) / (x3 - x2)\n    if (slope_right > slope_left + 1e-10) {\n      stop(paste0(\n        \"Log-concavity violated at x = \", x2,\n        \". Left slope = \", round(slope_left, 6),\n        \", Right slope = \", round(slope_right, 6),\n        \". The density may not be log-concave.\"\n      ))\n    }\n  }\n  return(TRUE)\n}\n\n#' Compute the slopes between consecutive abscissae on the log-density\n#' @param abscissae sorted x values\n#' @param h_vals corresponding h(x)\n#' @return vector of slopes of length (n-1)\ncompute_slopes <- function(abscissae, h_vals) {\n  n <- length(abscissae)\n  slopes <- (h_vals[2:n] - h_vals[1:(n-1)]) / (abscissae[2:n] - abscissae[1:(n-1)])\n  return(slopes)\n}\n\n#' Compute the upper hull (piecewise-linear envelope)\n#' The upper hull at each abscissa j uses the tangent lines from adjacent segments.\n#' For j=1: use slope from segment (x1, x2)\n#' For j=k (last): use slope from segment (x_{k-1}, x_k)\n#' For interior j: the tangent from left segment and right segment\n#'\n#' Returns the intersection points z between consecutive tangent lines,\n#' plus the slopes and intercepts of each tangent segment.\n#'\n#' @param abscissae sorted x values\n#' @param h_vals corresponding h(x)\n#' @param lb lower bound of domain\n#' @param ub upper bound of domain\n#' @return list with z (intersection points), slopes, intercepts for upper hull\ncompute_upper_hull <- function(abscissae, h_vals, lb, ub) {\n  k <- length(abscissae)\n\n  # Slopes of secant lines between consecutive abscissae\n  secant_slopes <- compute_slopes(abscissae, h_vals)\n\n  # Each tangent line j: u_j(x) = h_j + s_j * (x - x_j)\n  # where s_j is the slope assigned to abscissa j\n  # For the upper hull in ARS:\n  #   - segment j uses the line through (x_j, h_j) and (x_{j+1}, h_{j+1})\n  #   i.e., slope = secant_slope[j], intercept at x_j\n  # There are k-1 segments. The hull extends to lb on the left and ub on the right.\n\n  # The upper hull consists of k-1 line segments connecting consecutive points.\n  # But in ARS, the upper hull is defined by tangent lines at each abscissa.\n  # For log-concave densities, the tangent at x_j has slope between\n  # the left secant and right secant.\n\n  # Actually, in Gilks & Wild (1992), the upper hull u_k(x) is the\n  # minimum of the tangent lines. With only function values (no derivatives),\n  # they use the secant lines. Specifically:\n  #\n  # The upper hull is piecewise linear with pieces defined by:\n  #   piece j (for j = 1, ..., k-1): line through (x_j, h_j) and (x_{j+1}, h_{j+1})\n  #\n  # The intersection z_j of piece j and piece j+1 gives the breakpoints.\n\n  # For j = 1,...,k-1, line j has:\n  #   slope_j = (h_{j+1} - h_j) / (x_{j+1} - x_j)\n  #   u_j(x) = h_j + slope_j * (x - x_j)\n  #\n  # Intersection of line j and line j+1:\n  #   h_j + slope_j * (z - x_j) = h_{j+1} + slope_{j+1} * (z - x_{j+1})\n  #   z = (h_{j+1} - h_j + slope_j * x_j - slope_{j+1} * x_{j+1}) / (slope_j - slope_{j+1})\n\n  if (k < 2) stop(\"Need at least 2 abscissae for upper hull.\")\n\n  # k-1 segments\n  seg_slopes <- secant_slopes\n  seg_intercepts <- h_vals[1:(k-1)] - seg_slopes * abscissae[1:(k-1)]\n  # u_j(x) = seg_intercepts[j] + seg_slopes[j] * x\n\n  # Intersection points z between consecutive segments\n  # z[j] is where segment j meets segment j+1 (for j = 1,...,k-2)\n  z_inner <- numeric(0)\n  if (k > 2) {\n    z_inner <- (seg_intercepts[2:(k-1)] - seg_intercepts[1:(k-2)]) /\n               (seg_slopes[1:(k-2)] - seg_slopes[2:(k-1)])\n  }\n\n  # Full z vector including domain bounds\n  z <- c(lb, z_inner, ub)\n\n  return(list(\n    z = z,\n    seg_slopes = seg_slopes,\n    seg_intercepts = seg_intercepts\n  ))\n}\n\n#' Evaluate the upper hull at a point x\n#' @param x point to evaluate\n#' @param hull list from compute_upper_hull\n#' @return u(x), the upper hull value\neval_upper_hull <- function(x, hull) {\n  z <- hull$z\n  seg_slopes <- hull$seg_slopes\n  seg_intercepts <- hull$seg_intercepts\n  n_seg <- length(seg_slopes)\n\n  # Find which segment x falls in\n  # Segment j covers [z[j], z[j+1]]\n  seg_idx <- findInterval(x, z, rightmost.closed = TRUE)\n  seg_idx <- pmax(1, pmin(seg_idx, n_seg))\n\n  return(seg_intercepts[seg_idx] + seg_slopes[seg_idx] * x)\n}\n\n#' Evaluate the lower hull (piecewise linear through abscissae) at a point x\n#' @param x point to evaluate\n#' @param abscissae sorted x values\n#' @param h_vals corresponding h(x) values\n#' @return l(x), the lower hull value. -Inf if outside the range of abscissae.\neval_lower_hull <- function(x, abscissae, h_vals) {\n  k <- length(abscissae)\n  if (x <= abscissae[1] || x >= abscissae[k]) {\n    return(-Inf)\n  }\n  # Find which interval x falls in\n  j <- findInterval(x, abscissae)\n  j <- pmax(1, pmin(j, k - 1))\n\n  # Linear interpolation\n  t <- (x - abscissae[j]) / (abscissae[j+1] - abscissae[j])\n  return(h_vals[j] * (1 - t) + h_vals[j+1] * t)\n}\n\n#' Sample from the upper hull (piecewise-exponential distribution)\n#' The upper hull is a piecewise linear function on log scale, which corresponds\n#' to a piecewise exponential distribution.\n#' @param n number of samples to draw\n#' @param hull list from compute_upper_hull\n#' @return vector of n samples from the upper hull distribution\nsample_upper_hull <- function(n, hull) {\n  z <- hull$z\n  seg_slopes <- hull$seg_slopes\n  seg_intercepts <- hull$seg_intercepts\n  n_seg <- length(seg_slopes)\n\n  # For each segment j on [z_j, z_{j+1}]:\n  # The unnormalized density is exp(a_j + b_j * x) where a_j = seg_intercepts[j], b_j = seg_slopes[j]\n  # The integral is:\n  #   if b_j != 0: exp(a_j) * (exp(b_j * z_{j+1}) - exp(b_j * z_j)) / b_j\n  #   if b_j == 0: exp(a_j) * (z_{j+1} - z_j)\n\n  # Compute area under each segment\n  areas <- numeric(n_seg)\n  for (j in 1:n_seg) {\n    lo <- z[j]\n    hi <- z[j + 1]\n    if (!is.finite(lo)) lo <- z[j + 1] - 10  # fallback for -Inf\n    if (!is.finite(hi)) hi <- z[j] + 10       # fallback for +Inf\n    a <- seg_intercepts[j]\n    b <- seg_slopes[j]\n\n    if (abs(b) < 1e-15) {\n      areas[j] <- exp(a) * (hi - lo)\n    } else {\n      # Integral of exp(a + b*x) from lo to hi\n      # = exp(a)/b * (exp(b*hi) - exp(b*lo))\n      # Use log-sum-exp for numerical stability\n      log_hi <- a + b * hi\n      log_lo <- a + b * lo\n      if (b > 0) {\n        areas[j] <- exp(log_hi) * (1 - exp(log_lo - log_hi)) / b\n      } else {\n        areas[j] <- exp(log_lo) * (1 - exp(log_hi - log_lo)) / (-b)\n      }\n    }\n    if (is.na(areas[j]) || !is.finite(areas[j]) || areas[j] < 0) {\n      areas[j] <- 0\n    }\n  }\n\n  total_area <- sum(areas)\n  if (total_area <= 0 || !is.finite(total_area)) {\n    stop(\"Upper hull has zero or infinite area. Check domain bounds.\")\n  }\n  probs <- areas / total_area\n\n  # Sample segments according to their areas\n  seg_choices <- sample.int(n_seg, size = n, replace = TRUE, prob = probs)\n\n  # For each chosen segment, sample from the truncated exponential\n  samples <- numeric(n)\n  for (i in 1:n) {\n    j <- seg_choices[i]\n    lo <- z[j]\n    hi <- z[j + 1]\n    if (!is.finite(lo)) lo <- z[j + 1] - 10\n    if (!is.finite(hi)) hi <- z[j] + 10\n    b <- seg_slopes[j]\n\n    if (abs(b) < 1e-15) {\n      # Uniform on [lo, hi]\n      samples[i] <- runif(1, lo, hi)\n    } else {\n      # Inverse CDF of truncated exponential\n      # CDF: F(x) = (exp(b*x) - exp(b*lo)) / (exp(b*hi) - exp(b*lo))\n      u <- runif(1)\n      # x = log(exp(b*lo) + u * (exp(b*hi) - exp(b*lo))) / b\n      # Numerically stable version:\n      log_lo <- b * lo\n      log_hi <- b * hi\n      if (b > 0) {\n        samples[i] <- (log_lo + log(1 + u * (exp(log_hi - log_lo) - 1))) / b\n      } else {\n        samples[i] <- (log_hi + log(1 + (1 - u) * (exp(log_lo - log_hi) - 1))) / b\n      }\n    }\n  }\n  return(samples)\n}\n\n#' Choose initial abscissae for the ARS algorithm\n#' @param lb lower bound\n#' @param ub upper bound\n#' @param f density function\n#' @param n_init number of initial points (default 5)\n#' @return vector of initial abscissae\nchoose_initial_abscissae <- function(lb, ub, f, n_init = 5) {\n  # Handle infinite bounds\n  if (is.finite(lb) && is.finite(ub)) {\n    # Both finite: space evenly\n    pts <- seq(lb + (ub - lb) * 0.05, ub - (ub - lb) * 0.05, length.out = n_init)\n  } else if (is.finite(lb) && !is.finite(ub)) {\n    pts <- lb + (1:n_init) * 1.0\n  } else if (!is.finite(lb) && is.finite(ub)) {\n    pts <- ub - rev((1:n_init) * 1.0)\n  } else {\n    # Both infinite: center around 0\n    pts <- seq(-2, 2, length.out = n_init)\n  }\n\n  # Verify all points have positive density\n  vals <- f(pts)\n  if (any(is.na(vals) | vals <= 0)) {\n    # Try to find good points by searching\n    good <- which(vals > 0 & !is.na(vals))\n    if (length(good) < 2) {\n      stop(\"Cannot find initial abscissae with positive density. Check domain bounds.\")\n    }\n    pts <- pts[good]\n  }\n\n  return(pts)\n}\n\n#' Validate inputs to the ars function\n#' @param f density function\n#' @param n number of samples\n#' @param lb lower bound\n#' @param ub upper bound\nvalidate_inputs <- function(f, n, lb, ub) {\n  if (!is.function(f)) {\n    stop(\"'f' must be a function.\")\n  }\n  if (!is.numeric(n) || length(n) != 1 || n < 1 || n != as.integer(n)) {\n    stop(\"'n' must be a positive integer.\")\n  }\n  if (!is.numeric(lb) || length(lb) != 1) {\n    stop(\"'lb' must be a single numeric value.\")\n  }\n  if (!is.numeric(ub) || length(ub) != 1) {\n    stop(\"'ub' must be a single numeric value.\")\n  }\n  if (lb >= ub) {\n    stop(\"Lower bound must be strictly less than upper bound.\")\n  }\n}\n\n# --- Main ARS Function ---\n\n#' Adaptive Rejection Sampling\n#'\n#' @param f density function (possibly unnormalized), must be log-concave.\n#'          Should accept a vector and return a vector of density values.\n#' @param support optional 2-element vector c(lb, ub) for the domain\n#' @param n number of samples to generate (default 100)\n#' @param lb lower bound of the domain (default -Inf)\n#' @param ub upper bound of the domain (default Inf)\n#' @param ... additional arguments (currently unused)\n#' @return vector of n samples from the target distribution\nars <- function(f, support = NULL, n = 100, lb = -Inf, ub = Inf, ...) {\n  # Handle the support vector argument (alternative call pattern)\n  if (!is.null(support) && is.numeric(support) && length(support) == 2) {\n    lb <- support[1]\n    ub <- support[2]\n  }\n\n  # Validate inputs\n  validate_inputs(f, n, lb, ub)\n\n  # Choose initial abscissae\n  abscissae <- choose_initial_abscissae(lb, ub, f)\n  h_vals <- eval_log_density(f, abscissae)\n\n  # Check initial log-concavity\n  check_log_concavity(abscissae, h_vals)\n\n  # Main sampling loop\n  samples <- numeric(n)\n  count <- 0\n  max_iter <- n * 100  # safety limit to avoid infinite loops\n  iter <- 0\n\n  while (count < n && iter < max_iter) {\n    iter <- iter + 1\n\n    # Build upper hull\n    hull <- compute_upper_hull(abscissae, h_vals, lb, ub)\n\n    # Sample from upper hull\n    x_star <- sample_upper_hull(1, hull)\n\n    # Squeeze test\n    u <- runif(1)\n    u_val <- eval_upper_hull(x_star, hull)\n    l_val <- eval_lower_hull(x_star, abscissae, h_vals)\n\n    if (u <= exp(l_val - u_val)) {\n      # Accept by squeeze test (no density evaluation needed)\n      count <- count + 1\n      samples[count] <- x_star\n    } else {\n      # Need to evaluate the true log-density\n      h_star <- eval_log_density(f, x_star)\n\n      if (u <= exp(h_star - u_val)) {\n        # Accept by rejection test\n        count <- count + 1\n        samples[count] <- x_star\n      }\n\n      # Update the abscissae with the new point\n      insert_pos <- findInterval(x_star, abscissae) + 1\n      abscissae <- append(abscissae, x_star, after = insert_pos - 1)\n      h_vals <- append(h_vals, h_star, after = insert_pos - 1)\n\n      # Re-check log-concavity with new point\n      check_log_concavity(abscissae, h_vals)\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (count < n) {\n    warning(paste(\"Only generated\", count, \"out of\", n, \"requested samples.\"))\n  }\n\n  return(samples)\n}\n\n# --- Test Function ---\n\n#' Run formal tests of the ARS implementation\n#' Compares samples against known distributions using statistical tests\ntest <- function() {\n  cat(\"============================================================\\n\")\n  cat(\"     Adaptive Rejection Sampling (ARS) - Test Suite\\n\")\n  cat(\"============================================================\\n\\n\")\n\n  pass_count <- 0\n  fail_count <- 0\n\n  run_test <- function(name, expr) {\n    result <- tryCatch(expr, error = function(e) {\n      cat(paste0(\"  Error: \", e$message, \"\\n\"))\n      return(FALSE)\n    })\n    if (isTRUE(result)) {\n      cat(paste0(name, \": PASS\\n\\n\"))\n      pass_count <<- pass_count + 1\n    } else {\n      cat(paste0(name, \": FAIL\\n\\n\"))\n      fail_count <<- fail_count + 1\n    }\n  }\n\n  # ---- Input Validation Tests ----\n  cat(\"--- Input Validation Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_negative_n\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that negative n is rejected...\\n\")\n    err <- tryCatch({ ars(dnorm, n = -5); FALSE },\n                    error = function(e) grepl(\"positive integer\", e$message))\n    err\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_zero_n\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that n=0 is rejected...\\n\")\n    err <- tryCatch({ ars(dnorm, n = 0); FALSE },\n                    error = function(e) grepl(\"positive integer\", e$message))\n    err\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_non_integer_n\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that non-integer n is rejected...\\n\")\n    err <- tryCatch({ ars(dnorm, n = 3.5); FALSE },\n                    error = function(e) grepl(\"positive integer\", e$message))\n    err\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_invalid_bounds\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that lb >= ub is rejected...\\n\")\n    err <- tryCatch({ ars(dnorm, n = 10, lb = 5, ub = 2); FALSE },\n                    error = function(e) grepl(\"strictly less\", e$message))\n    err\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_non_function_f\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that non-function f is rejected...\\n\")\n    err <- tryCatch({ ars(\"not_a_function\", n = 10); FALSE },\n                    error = function(e) grepl(\"must be a function\", e$message))\n    err\n  })\n\n  # ---- Log-Concavity Tests ----\n  cat(\"--- Log-Concavity Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_bimodal_rejection\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing rejection of bimodal (non-log-concave) density...\\n\")\n    bimodal <- function(x) 0.5 * dnorm(x, -3, 0.5) + 0.5 * dnorm(x, 3, 0.5)\n    err <- tryCatch({ ars(bimodal, n = 1000, lb = -10, ub = 10); FALSE },\n                    error = function(e) grepl(\"[Ll]og-concav\", e$message))\n    err\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_cauchy_rejection\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing rejection of Cauchy (non-log-concave on wide domain)...\\n\")\n    # Cauchy is NOT log-concave: log(1/(1+x^2)) = -log(1+x^2) has\n    # second derivative (x^2 - 1)/(1+x^2)^2, which is positive for |x|>1\n    # However, Cauchy IS log-concave. Let me use t-distribution with df=1.\n    # Actually, Cauchy = t(df=1). log f(x) = -log(1+x^2) (up to constant)\n    # d^2/dx^2 [-log(1+x^2)] = (2x^2-2)/(1+x^2)^2 which changes sign.\n    # So Cauchy IS log-concave only near the center.\n    # On a wide enough domain with enough initial points, we should catch it.\n    # Actually, the second derivative of -log(1+x^2) is (2(x^2-1))/(1+x^2)^2\n    # which is negative for |x| < 1 and positive for |x| > 1.\n    # So Cauchy is NOT log-concave. Good.\n    err <- tryCatch({\n      ars(dcauchy, n = 1000, lb = -100, ub = 100)\n      FALSE\n    }, error = function(e) grepl(\"[Ll]og-concav\", e$message))\n    err\n  })\n\n  # ---- Sampling Tests (Standard Normal) ----\n  cat(\"--- Standard Normal Distribution Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_normal_sampling\", {\n    cat(\"  Sampling 5000 points from N(0,1)...\\n\")\n    set.seed(42)\n    samples <- ars(n = 5000, f = dnorm, lb = -Inf, ub = Inf)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(mean(samples), 4), \" (expected ~0)\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(sd(samples), 4), \" (expected ~1)\\n\"))\n\n    # Save samples\n    writeLines(as.character(samples), \"/app/normal_samples.txt\")\n    cat(\"  Saved samples to /app/normal_samples.txt\\n\")\n\n    # KS test against N(0,1)\n    ks <- ks.test(samples, \"pnorm\")\n    cat(paste0(\"  KS test p-value: \", round(ks$p.value, 4), \"\\n\"))\n\n    # Check: mean close to 0, sd close to 1, KS p-value > 0.01\n    abs(mean(samples)) < 0.15 && abs(sd(samples) - 1) < 0.15 && ks$p.value > 0.01\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_normal_bounded\", {\n    cat(\"  Sampling 2000 points from N(0,1) on [-5, 5]...\\n\")\n    set.seed(123)\n    samples <- ars(dnorm, c(-5, 5), n = 2000)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(mean(samples), 4), \"\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(sd(samples), 4), \"\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Min: \", round(min(samples), 4), \", Max: \", round(max(samples), 4), \"\\n\"))\n\n    # All samples should be within bounds\n    all(samples >= -5 & samples <= 5) && abs(mean(samples)) < 0.2\n  })\n\n  # ---- Sampling Tests (Exponential) ----\n  cat(\"--- Exponential Distribution Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_exponential_sampling\", {\n    cat(\"  Sampling 5000 points from Exp(1) on [0, 100]...\\n\")\n    set.seed(99)\n    samples <- ars(dexp, c(0.001, 100), n = 5000)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(mean(samples), 4), \" (expected ~1)\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(sd(samples), 4), \" (expected ~1)\\n\"))\n\n    # Save samples\n    writeLines(as.character(samples), \"/app/exponential_samples.txt\")\n    cat(\"  Saved samples to /app/exponential_samples.txt\\n\")\n\n    # KS test\n    ks <- ks.test(samples, \"pexp\")\n    cat(paste0(\"  KS test p-value: \", round(ks$p.value, 4), \"\\n\"))\n\n    abs(mean(samples) - 1) < 0.15 && ks$p.value > 0.01\n  })\n\n  # ---- Sampling Tests (Gamma) ----\n  cat(\"--- Gamma Distribution Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_gamma_sampling\", {\n    cat(\"  Sampling 3000 points from Gamma(3, 1) on [0.01, 30]...\\n\")\n    set.seed(77)\n    gamma_dens <- function(x) dgamma(x, shape = 3, rate = 1)\n    samples <- ars(gamma_dens, c(0.01, 30), n = 3000)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(mean(samples), 4), \" (expected ~3)\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(sd(samples), 4), \" (expected ~1.73)\\n\"))\n\n    ks <- ks.test(samples, \"pgamma\", shape = 3, rate = 1)\n    cat(paste0(\"  KS test p-value: \", round(ks$p.value, 4), \"\\n\"))\n\n    abs(mean(samples) - 3) < 0.3 && ks$p.value > 0.01\n  })\n\n  # ---- Sampling Tests (Beta) ----\n  cat(\"--- Beta Distribution Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_beta_sampling\", {\n    cat(\"  Sampling 3000 points from Beta(2, 5) on [0.01, 0.99]...\\n\")\n    set.seed(55)\n    beta_dens <- function(x) dbeta(x, shape1 = 2, shape2 = 5)\n    samples <- ars(beta_dens, c(0.01, 0.99), n = 3000)\n    expected_mean <- 2 / (2 + 5)\n    expected_sd <- sqrt(2 * 5 / ((2 + 5)^2 * (2 + 5 + 1)))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(mean(samples), 4),\n               \" (expected ~\", round(expected_mean, 4), \")\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(sd(samples), 4),\n               \" (expected ~\", round(expected_sd, 4), \")\\n\"))\n\n    ks <- ks.test(samples, \"pbeta\", shape1 = 2, shape2 = 5)\n    cat(paste0(\"  KS test p-value: \", round(ks$p.value, 4), \"\\n\"))\n\n    abs(mean(samples) - expected_mean) < 0.05 && ks$p.value > 0.01\n  })\n\n  # ---- Sampling Tests (Log-Normal via unnormalized density) ----\n  cat(\"--- Unnormalized Density Test ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_unnormalized_density\", {\n    cat(\"  Sampling from unnormalized Gaussian: f(x) = exp(-x^2/2)...\\n\")\n    set.seed(33)\n    unnorm_gauss <- function(x) exp(-x^2 / 2)\n    samples <- ars(unnorm_gauss, n = 3000, lb = -Inf, ub = Inf)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(mean(samples), 4), \" (expected ~0)\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(sd(samples), 4), \" (expected ~1)\\n\"))\n\n    ks <- ks.test(samples, \"pnorm\")\n    cat(paste0(\"  KS test p-value: \", round(ks$p.value, 4), \"\\n\"))\n\n    abs(mean(samples)) < 0.15 && abs(sd(samples) - 1) < 0.15 && ks$p.value > 0.01\n  })\n\n  # ---- Module-Level Tests ----\n  cat(\"--- Module-Level Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_eval_log_density\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing eval_log_density with dnorm...\\n\")\n    result <- eval_log_density(dnorm, 0)\n    expected <- log(dnorm(0))\n    cat(paste0(\"  log(dnorm(0)) = \", round(result, 6), \" (expected \", round(expected, 6), \")\\n\"))\n    abs(result - expected) < 1e-10\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_check_log_concavity_valid\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing log-concavity check on concave data...\\n\")\n    # h(x) = -x^2 is concave\n    x <- c(-2, -1, 0, 1, 2)\n    h <- -x^2\n    result <- tryCatch({ check_log_concavity(x, h); TRUE },\n                       error = function(e) FALSE)\n    result\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_check_log_concavity_invalid\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing log-concavity check on convex data...\\n\")\n    # h(x) = x^2 is convex (NOT concave)\n    x <- c(-2, -1, 0, 1, 2)\n    h <- x^2\n    result <- tryCatch({ check_log_concavity(x, h); FALSE },\n                       error = function(e) grepl(\"[Ll]og-concav\", e$message))\n    result\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_upper_hull_above_density\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that upper hull is above log-density at test points...\\n\")\n    set.seed(11)\n    x_abs <- c(-3, -1, 0, 1, 3)\n    h_abs <- eval_log_density(dnorm, x_abs)\n    hull <- compute_upper_hull(x_abs, h_abs, -Inf, Inf)\n\n    # Check at many random points\n    test_pts <- seq(-2.5, 2.5, by = 0.1)\n    h_test <- eval_log_density(dnorm, test_pts)\n    u_test <- eval_upper_hull(test_pts, hull)\n\n    all_above <- all(u_test >= h_test - 1e-10)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Upper hull >= log-density at all \", length(test_pts), \" test points: \", all_above, \"\\n\"))\n    all_above\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_lower_hull_below_density\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that lower hull is below log-density at test points...\\n\")\n    x_abs <- c(-3, -1, 0, 1, 3)\n    h_abs <- eval_log_density(dnorm, x_abs)\n\n    test_pts <- seq(-2.5, 2.5, by = 0.1)\n    h_test <- eval_log_density(dnorm, test_pts)\n    l_test <- sapply(test_pts, function(x) eval_lower_hull(x, x_abs, h_abs))\n\n    all_below <- all(l_test <= h_test + 1e-10)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Lower hull <= log-density at all \", length(test_pts), \" test points: \", all_below, \"\\n\"))\n    all_below\n  })\n\n  # ---- Summary ----\n  cat(\"============================================================\\n\")\n  cat(paste0(\"  TOTAL: \", pass_count + fail_count, \" tests, \",\n             pass_count, \" passed, \", fail_count, \" failed\\n\"))\n  cat(\"============================================================\\n\")\n\n  invisible(list(passed = pass_count, failed = fail_count))\n}\n\n# Run tests if this script is executed directly\nif (sys.nframe() == 0) {\n  test()\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":102,"cache_read_input_tokens":27920,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":102,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"78903e02-0918-48fe-a339-296475e92493","uuid":"03973fd2-b9f2-44e9-9dc4-3af3a2c51bbb"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01VqcyqVqyMeXaNDtYRFEyEL","type":"tool_result","content":"File created successfully at: /app/ars.R"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"78903e02-0918-48fe-a339-296475e92493","uuid":"94f83298-8c19-4ab5-929a-789329a68658","tool_use_result":{"type":"create","filePath":"/app/ars.R","content":"###############################################################################\n# Adaptive Rejection Sampling (ARS)\n# Based on Gilks, W.R. & Wild, P. (1992)\n# \"Adaptive Rejection Sampling for Gibbs Sampling\"\n# Journal of the Royal Statistical Society: Series C, 41(2), 337-348.\n###############################################################################\n\n# --- Auxiliary / Module Functions ---\n\n#' Evaluate the log of a density function safely\n#' @param f density function (possibly unnormalized)\n#' @param x point(s) at which to evaluate\n#' @return log(f(x)), with protection against f(x) <= 0\neval_log_density <- function(f, x) {\n  fx <- f(x)\n  if (any(is.na(fx) | is.nan(fx))) {\n    stop(\"Density function returned NA/NaN.\")\n  }\n  if (any(fx < 0)) {\n    stop(\"Density function returned negative value.\")\n  }\n  result <- rep(-Inf, length(fx))\n  pos <- fx > 0\n  result[pos] <- log(fx[pos])\n  return(result)\n}\n\n#' Check log-concavity at three consecutive abscissae\n#' If h(x) = log(f(x)), then log-concavity requires:\n#'   (h(x2) - h(x1))/(x2 - x1) >= (h(x3) - h(x2))/(x3 - x2)\n#' for all x1 < x2 < x3\n#' @param abscissae sorted vector of x values\n#' @param h_vals corresponding log-density values h(x)\n#' @return TRUE if log-concave, else stops with error\ncheck_log_concavity <- function(abscissae, h_vals) {\n  n <- length(abscissae)\n  if (n < 3) return(TRUE)\n  for (i in 1:(n - 2)) {\n    x1 <- abscissae[i];   h1 <- h_vals[i]\n    x2 <- abscissae[i+1]; h2 <- h_vals[i+1]\n    x3 <- abscissae[i+2]; h3 <- h_vals[i+2]\n    slope_left  <- (h2 - h1) / (x2 - x1)\n    slope_right <- (h3 - h2) / (x3 - x2)\n    if (slope_right > slope_left + 1e-10) {\n      stop(paste0(\n        \"Log-concavity violated at x = \", x2,\n        \". Left slope = \", round(slope_left, 6),\n        \", Right slope = \", round(slope_right, 6),\n        \". The density may not be log-concave.\"\n      ))\n    }\n  }\n  return(TRUE)\n}\n\n#' Compute the slopes between consecutive abscissae on the log-density\n#' @param abscissae sorted x values\n#' @param h_vals corresponding h(x)\n#' @return vector of slopes of length (n-1)\ncompute_slopes <- function(abscissae, h_vals) {\n  n <- length(abscissae)\n  slopes <- (h_vals[2:n] - h_vals[1:(n-1)]) / (abscissae[2:n] - abscissae[1:(n-1)])\n  return(slopes)\n}\n\n#' Compute the upper hull (piecewise-linear envelope)\n#' The upper hull at each abscissa j uses the tangent lines from adjacent segments.\n#' For j=1: use slope from segment (x1, x2)\n#' For j=k (last): use slope from segment (x_{k-1}, x_k)\n#' For interior j: the tangent from left segment and right segment\n#'\n#' Returns the intersection points z between consecutive tangent lines,\n#' plus the slopes and intercepts of each tangent segment.\n#'\n#' @param abscissae sorted x values\n#' @param h_vals corresponding h(x)\n#' @param lb lower bound of domain\n#' @param ub upper bound of domain\n#' @return list with z (intersection points), slopes, intercepts for upper hull\ncompute_upper_hull <- function(abscissae, h_vals, lb, ub) {\n  k <- length(abscissae)\n\n  # Slopes of secant lines between consecutive abscissae\n  secant_slopes <- compute_slopes(abscissae, h_vals)\n\n  # Each tangent line j: u_j(x) = h_j + s_j * (x - x_j)\n  # where s_j is the slope assigned to abscissa j\n  # For the upper hull in ARS:\n  #   - segment j uses the line through (x_j, h_j) and (x_{j+1}, h_{j+1})\n  #   i.e., slope = secant_slope[j], intercept at x_j\n  # There are k-1 segments. The hull extends to lb on the left and ub on the right.\n\n  # The upper hull consists of k-1 line segments connecting consecutive points.\n  # But in ARS, the upper hull is defined by tangent lines at each abscissa.\n  # For log-concave densities, the tangent at x_j has slope between\n  # the left secant and right secant.\n\n  # Actually, in Gilks & Wild (1992), the upper hull u_k(x) is the\n  # minimum of the tangent lines. With only function values (no derivatives),\n  # they use the secant lines. Specifically:\n  #\n  # The upper hull is piecewise linear with pieces defined by:\n  #   piece j (for j = 1, ..., k-1): line through (x_j, h_j) and (x_{j+1}, h_{j+1})\n  #\n  # The intersection z_j of piece j and piece j+1 gives the breakpoints.\n\n  # For j = 1,...,k-1, line j has:\n  #   slope_j = (h_{j+1} - h_j) / (x_{j+1} - x_j)\n  #   u_j(x) = h_j + slope_j * (x - x_j)\n  #\n  # Intersection of line j and line j+1:\n  #   h_j + slope_j * (z - x_j) = h_{j+1} + slope_{j+1} * (z - x_{j+1})\n  #   z = (h_{j+1} - h_j + slope_j * x_j - slope_{j+1} * x_{j+1}) / (slope_j - slope_{j+1})\n\n  if (k < 2) stop(\"Need at least 2 abscissae for upper hull.\")\n\n  # k-1 segments\n  seg_slopes <- secant_slopes\n  seg_intercepts <- h_vals[1:(k-1)] - seg_slopes * abscissae[1:(k-1)]\n  # u_j(x) = seg_intercepts[j] + seg_slopes[j] * x\n\n  # Intersection points z between consecutive segments\n  # z[j] is where segment j meets segment j+1 (for j = 1,...,k-2)\n  z_inner <- numeric(0)\n  if (k > 2) {\n    z_inner <- (seg_intercepts[2:(k-1)] - seg_intercepts[1:(k-2)]) /\n               (seg_slopes[1:(k-2)] - seg_slopes[2:(k-1)])\n  }\n\n  # Full z vector including domain bounds\n  z <- c(lb, z_inner, ub)\n\n  return(list(\n    z = z,\n    seg_slopes = seg_slopes,\n    seg_intercepts = seg_intercepts\n  ))\n}\n\n#' Evaluate the upper hull at a point x\n#' @param x point to evaluate\n#' @param hull list from compute_upper_hull\n#' @return u(x), the upper hull value\neval_upper_hull <- function(x, hull) {\n  z <- hull$z\n  seg_slopes <- hull$seg_slopes\n  seg_intercepts <- hull$seg_intercepts\n  n_seg <- length(seg_slopes)\n\n  # Find which segment x falls in\n  # Segment j covers [z[j], z[j+1]]\n  seg_idx <- findInterval(x, z, rightmost.closed = TRUE)\n  seg_idx <- pmax(1, pmin(seg_idx, n_seg))\n\n  return(seg_intercepts[seg_idx] + seg_slopes[seg_idx] * x)\n}\n\n#' Evaluate the lower hull (piecewise linear through abscissae) at a point x\n#' @param x point to evaluate\n#' @param abscissae sorted x values\n#' @param h_vals corresponding h(x) values\n#' @return l(x), the lower hull value. -Inf if outside the range of abscissae.\neval_lower_hull <- function(x, abscissae, h_vals) {\n  k <- length(abscissae)\n  if (x <= abscissae[1] || x >= abscissae[k]) {\n    return(-Inf)\n  }\n  # Find which interval x falls in\n  j <- findInterval(x, abscissae)\n  j <- pmax(1, pmin(j, k - 1))\n\n  # Linear interpolation\n  t <- (x - abscissae[j]) / (abscissae[j+1] - abscissae[j])\n  return(h_vals[j] * (1 - t) + h_vals[j+1] * t)\n}\n\n#' Sample from the upper hull (piecewise-exponential distribution)\n#' The upper hull is a piecewise linear function on log scale, which corresponds\n#' to a piecewise exponential distribution.\n#' @param n number of samples to draw\n#' @param hull list from compute_upper_hull\n#' @return vector of n samples from the upper hull distribution\nsample_upper_hull <- function(n, hull) {\n  z <- hull$z\n  seg_slopes <- hull$seg_slopes\n  seg_intercepts <- hull$seg_intercepts\n  n_seg <- length(seg_slopes)\n\n  # For each segment j on [z_j, z_{j+1}]:\n  # The unnormalized density is exp(a_j + b_j * x) where a_j = seg_intercepts[j], b_j = seg_slopes[j]\n  # The integral is:\n  #   if b_j != 0: exp(a_j) * (exp(b_j * z_{j+1}) - exp(b_j * z_j)) / b_j\n  #   if b_j == 0: exp(a_j) * (z_{j+1} - z_j)\n\n  # Compute area under each segment\n  areas <- numeric(n_seg)\n  for (j in 1:n_seg) {\n    lo <- z[j]\n    hi <- z[j + 1]\n    if (!is.finite(lo)) lo <- z[j + 1] - 10  # fallback for -Inf\n    if (!is.finite(hi)) hi <- z[j] + 10       # fallback for +Inf\n    a <- seg_intercepts[j]\n    b <- seg_slopes[j]\n\n    if (abs(b) < 1e-15) {\n      areas[j] <- exp(a) * (hi - lo)\n    } else {\n      # Integral of exp(a + b*x) from lo to hi\n      # = exp(a)/b * (exp(b*hi) - exp(b*lo))\n      # Use log-sum-exp for numerical stability\n      log_hi <- a + b * hi\n      log_lo <- a + b * lo\n      if (b > 0) {\n        areas[j] <- exp(log_hi) * (1 - exp(log_lo - log_hi)) / b\n      } else {\n        areas[j] <- exp(log_lo) * (1 - exp(log_hi - log_lo)) / (-b)\n      }\n    }\n    if (is.na(areas[j]) || !is.finite(areas[j]) || areas[j] < 0) {\n      areas[j] <- 0\n    }\n  }\n\n  total_area <- sum(areas)\n  if (total_area <= 0 || !is.finite(total_area)) {\n    stop(\"Upper hull has zero or infinite area. Check domain bounds.\")\n  }\n  probs <- areas / total_area\n\n  # Sample segments according to their areas\n  seg_choices <- sample.int(n_seg, size = n, replace = TRUE, prob = probs)\n\n  # For each chosen segment, sample from the truncated exponential\n  samples <- numeric(n)\n  for (i in 1:n) {\n    j <- seg_choices[i]\n    lo <- z[j]\n    hi <- z[j + 1]\n    if (!is.finite(lo)) lo <- z[j + 1] - 10\n    if (!is.finite(hi)) hi <- z[j] + 10\n    b <- seg_slopes[j]\n\n    if (abs(b) < 1e-15) {\n      # Uniform on [lo, hi]\n      samples[i] <- runif(1, lo, hi)\n    } else {\n      # Inverse CDF of truncated exponential\n      # CDF: F(x) = (exp(b*x) - exp(b*lo)) / (exp(b*hi) - exp(b*lo))\n      u <- runif(1)\n      # x = log(exp(b*lo) + u * (exp(b*hi) - exp(b*lo))) / b\n      # Numerically stable version:\n      log_lo <- b * lo\n      log_hi <- b * hi\n      if (b > 0) {\n        samples[i] <- (log_lo + log(1 + u * (exp(log_hi - log_lo) - 1))) / b\n      } else {\n        samples[i] <- (log_hi + log(1 + (1 - u) * (exp(log_lo - log_hi) - 1))) / b\n      }\n    }\n  }\n  return(samples)\n}\n\n#' Choose initial abscissae for the ARS algorithm\n#' @param lb lower bound\n#' @param ub upper bound\n#' @param f density function\n#' @param n_init number of initial points (default 5)\n#' @return vector of initial abscissae\nchoose_initial_abscissae <- function(lb, ub, f, n_init = 5) {\n  # Handle infinite bounds\n  if (is.finite(lb) && is.finite(ub)) {\n    # Both finite: space evenly\n    pts <- seq(lb + (ub - lb) * 0.05, ub - (ub - lb) * 0.05, length.out = n_init)\n  } else if (is.finite(lb) && !is.finite(ub)) {\n    pts <- lb + (1:n_init) * 1.0\n  } else if (!is.finite(lb) && is.finite(ub)) {\n    pts <- ub - rev((1:n_init) * 1.0)\n  } else {\n    # Both infinite: center around 0\n    pts <- seq(-2, 2, length.out = n_init)\n  }\n\n  # Verify all points have positive density\n  vals <- f(pts)\n  if (any(is.na(vals) | vals <= 0)) {\n    # Try to find good points by searching\n    good <- which(vals > 0 & !is.na(vals))\n    if (length(good) < 2) {\n      stop(\"Cannot find initial abscissae with positive density. Check domain bounds.\")\n    }\n    pts <- pts[good]\n  }\n\n  return(pts)\n}\n\n#' Validate inputs to the ars function\n#' @param f density function\n#' @param n number of samples\n#' @param lb lower bound\n#' @param ub upper bound\nvalidate_inputs <- function(f, n, lb, ub) {\n  if (!is.function(f)) {\n    stop(\"'f' must be a function.\")\n  }\n  if (!is.numeric(n) || length(n) != 1 || n < 1 || n != as.integer(n)) {\n    stop(\"'n' must be a positive integer.\")\n  }\n  if (!is.numeric(lb) || length(lb) != 1) {\n    stop(\"'lb' must be a single numeric value.\")\n  }\n  if (!is.numeric(ub) || length(ub) != 1) {\n    stop(\"'ub' must be a single numeric value.\")\n  }\n  if (lb >= ub) {\n    stop(\"Lower bound must be strictly less than upper bound.\")\n  }\n}\n\n# --- Main ARS Function ---\n\n#' Adaptive Rejection Sampling\n#'\n#' @param f density function (possibly unnormalized), must be log-concave.\n#'          Should accept a vector and return a vector of density values.\n#' @param support optional 2-element vector c(lb, ub) for the domain\n#' @param n number of samples to generate (default 100)\n#' @param lb lower bound of the domain (default -Inf)\n#' @param ub upper bound of the domain (default Inf)\n#' @param ... additional arguments (currently unused)\n#' @return vector of n samples from the target distribution\nars <- function(f, support = NULL, n = 100, lb = -Inf, ub = Inf, ...) {\n  # Handle the support vector argument (alternative call pattern)\n  if (!is.null(support) && is.numeric(support) && length(support) == 2) {\n    lb <- support[1]\n    ub <- support[2]\n  }\n\n  # Validate inputs\n  validate_inputs(f, n, lb, ub)\n\n  # Choose initial abscissae\n  abscissae <- choose_initial_abscissae(lb, ub, f)\n  h_vals <- eval_log_density(f, abscissae)\n\n  # Check initial log-concavity\n  check_log_concavity(abscissae, h_vals)\n\n  # Main sampling loop\n  samples <- numeric(n)\n  count <- 0\n  max_iter <- n * 100  # safety limit to avoid infinite loops\n  iter <- 0\n\n  while (count < n && iter < max_iter) {\n    iter <- iter + 1\n\n    # Build upper hull\n    hull <- compute_upper_hull(abscissae, h_vals, lb, ub)\n\n    # Sample from upper hull\n    x_star <- sample_upper_hull(1, hull)\n\n    # Squeeze test\n    u <- runif(1)\n    u_val <- eval_upper_hull(x_star, hull)\n    l_val <- eval_lower_hull(x_star, abscissae, h_vals)\n\n    if (u <= exp(l_val - u_val)) {\n      # Accept by squeeze test (no density evaluation needed)\n      count <- count + 1\n      samples[count] <- x_star\n    } else {\n      # Need to evaluate the true log-density\n      h_star <- eval_log_density(f, x_star)\n\n      if (u <= exp(h_star - u_val)) {\n        # Accept by rejection test\n        count <- count + 1\n        samples[count] <- x_star\n      }\n\n      # Update the abscissae with the new point\n      insert_pos <- findInterval(x_star, abscissae) + 1\n      abscissae <- append(abscissae, x_star, after = insert_pos - 1)\n      h_vals <- append(h_vals, h_star, after = insert_pos - 1)\n\n      # Re-check log-concavity with new point\n      check_log_concavity(abscissae, h_vals)\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (count < n) {\n    warning(paste(\"Only generated\", count, \"out of\", n, \"requested samples.\"))\n  }\n\n  return(samples)\n}\n\n# --- Test Function ---\n\n#' Run formal tests of the ARS implementation\n#' Compares samples against known distributions using statistical tests\ntest <- function() {\n  cat(\"============================================================\\n\")\n  cat(\"     Adaptive Rejection Sampling (ARS) - Test Suite\\n\")\n  cat(\"============================================================\\n\\n\")\n\n  pass_count <- 0\n  fail_count <- 0\n\n  run_test <- function(name, expr) {\n    result <- tryCatch(expr, error = function(e) {\n      cat(paste0(\"  Error: \", e$message, \"\\n\"))\n      return(FALSE)\n    })\n    if (isTRUE(result)) {\n      cat(paste0(name, \": PASS\\n\\n\"))\n      pass_count <<- pass_count + 1\n    } else {\n      cat(paste0(name, \": FAIL\\n\\n\"))\n      fail_count <<- fail_count + 1\n    }\n  }\n\n  # ---- Input Validation Tests ----\n  cat(\"--- Input Validation Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_negative_n\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that negative n is rejected...\\n\")\n    err <- tryCatch({ ars(dnorm, n = -5); FALSE },\n                    error = function(e) grepl(\"positive integer\", e$message))\n    err\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_zero_n\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that n=0 is rejected...\\n\")\n    err <- tryCatch({ ars(dnorm, n = 0); FALSE },\n                    error = function(e) grepl(\"positive integer\", e$message))\n    err\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_non_integer_n\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that non-integer n is rejected...\\n\")\n    err <- tryCatch({ ars(dnorm, n = 3.5); FALSE },\n                    error = function(e) grepl(\"positive integer\", e$message))\n    err\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_invalid_bounds\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that lb >= ub is rejected...\\n\")\n    err <- tryCatch({ ars(dnorm, n = 10, lb = 5, ub = 2); FALSE },\n                    error = function(e) grepl(\"strictly less\", e$message))\n    err\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_non_function_f\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that non-function f is rejected...\\n\")\n    err <- tryCatch({ ars(\"not_a_function\", n = 10); FALSE },\n                    error = function(e) grepl(\"must be a function\", e$message))\n    err\n  })\n\n  # ---- Log-Concavity Tests ----\n  cat(\"--- Log-Concavity Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_bimodal_rejection\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing rejection of bimodal (non-log-concave) density...\\n\")\n    bimodal <- function(x) 0.5 * dnorm(x, -3, 0.5) + 0.5 * dnorm(x, 3, 0.5)\n    err <- tryCatch({ ars(bimodal, n = 1000, lb = -10, ub = 10); FALSE },\n                    error = function(e) grepl(\"[Ll]og-concav\", e$message))\n    err\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_cauchy_rejection\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing rejection of Cauchy (non-log-concave on wide domain)...\\n\")\n    # Cauchy is NOT log-concave: log(1/(1+x^2)) = -log(1+x^2) has\n    # second derivative (x^2 - 1)/(1+x^2)^2, which is positive for |x|>1\n    # However, Cauchy IS log-concave. Let me use t-distribution with df=1.\n    # Actually, Cauchy = t(df=1). log f(x) = -log(1+x^2) (up to constant)\n    # d^2/dx^2 [-log(1+x^2)] = (2x^2-2)/(1+x^2)^2 which changes sign.\n    # So Cauchy IS log-concave only near the center.\n    # On a wide enough domain with enough initial points, we should catch it.\n    # Actually, the second derivative of -log(1+x^2) is (2(x^2-1))/(1+x^2)^2\n    # which is negative for |x| < 1 and positive for |x| > 1.\n    # So Cauchy is NOT log-concave. Good.\n    err <- tryCatch({\n      ars(dcauchy, n = 1000, lb = -100, ub = 100)\n      FALSE\n    }, error = function(e) grepl(\"[Ll]og-concav\", e$message))\n    err\n  })\n\n  # ---- Sampling Tests (Standard Normal) ----\n  cat(\"--- Standard Normal Distribution Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_normal_sampling\", {\n    cat(\"  Sampling 5000 points from N(0,1)...\\n\")\n    set.seed(42)\n    samples <- ars(n = 5000, f = dnorm, lb = -Inf, ub = Inf)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(mean(samples), 4), \" (expected ~0)\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(sd(samples), 4), \" (expected ~1)\\n\"))\n\n    # Save samples\n    writeLines(as.character(samples), \"/app/normal_samples.txt\")\n    cat(\"  Saved samples to /app/normal_samples.txt\\n\")\n\n    # KS test against N(0,1)\n    ks <- ks.test(samples, \"pnorm\")\n    cat(paste0(\"  KS test p-value: \", round(ks$p.value, 4), \"\\n\"))\n\n    # Check: mean close to 0, sd close to 1, KS p-value > 0.01\n    abs(mean(samples)) < 0.15 && abs(sd(samples) - 1) < 0.15 && ks$p.value > 0.01\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_normal_bounded\", {\n    cat(\"  Sampling 2000 points from N(0,1) on [-5, 5]...\\n\")\n    set.seed(123)\n    samples <- ars(dnorm, c(-5, 5), n = 2000)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(mean(samples), 4), \"\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(sd(samples), 4), \"\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Min: \", round(min(samples), 4), \", Max: \", round(max(samples), 4), \"\\n\"))\n\n    # All samples should be within bounds\n    all(samples >= -5 & samples <= 5) && abs(mean(samples)) < 0.2\n  })\n\n  # ---- Sampling Tests (Exponential) ----\n  cat(\"--- Exponential Distribution Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_exponential_sampling\", {\n    cat(\"  Sampling 5000 points from Exp(1) on [0, 100]...\\n\")\n    set.seed(99)\n    samples <- ars(dexp, c(0.001, 100), n = 5000)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(mean(samples), 4), \" (expected ~1)\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(sd(samples), 4), \" (expected ~1)\\n\"))\n\n    # Save samples\n    writeLines(as.character(samples), \"/app/exponential_samples.txt\")\n    cat(\"  Saved samples to /app/exponential_samples.txt\\n\")\n\n    # KS test\n    ks <- ks.test(samples, \"pexp\")\n    cat(paste0(\"  KS test p-value: \", round(ks$p.value, 4), \"\\n\"))\n\n    abs(mean(samples) - 1) < 0.15 && ks$p.value > 0.01\n  })\n\n  # ---- Sampling Tests (Gamma) ----\n  cat(\"--- Gamma Distribution Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_gamma_sampling\", {\n    cat(\"  Sampling 3000 points from Gamma(3, 1) on [0.01, 30]...\\n\")\n    set.seed(77)\n    gamma_dens <- function(x) dgamma(x, shape = 3, rate = 1)\n    samples <- ars(gamma_dens, c(0.01, 30), n = 3000)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(mean(samples), 4), \" (expected ~3)\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(sd(samples), 4), \" (expected ~1.73)\\n\"))\n\n    ks <- ks.test(samples, \"pgamma\", shape = 3, rate = 1)\n    cat(paste0(\"  KS test p-value: \", round(ks$p.value, 4), \"\\n\"))\n\n    abs(mean(samples) - 3) < 0.3 && ks$p.value > 0.01\n  })\n\n  # ---- Sampling Tests (Beta) ----\n  cat(\"--- Beta Distribution Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_beta_sampling\", {\n    cat(\"  Sampling 3000 points from Beta(2, 5) on [0.01, 0.99]...\\n\")\n    set.seed(55)\n    beta_dens <- function(x) dbeta(x, shape1 = 2, shape2 = 5)\n    samples <- ars(beta_dens, c(0.01, 0.99), n = 3000)\n    expected_mean <- 2 / (2 + 5)\n    expected_sd <- sqrt(2 * 5 / ((2 + 5)^2 * (2 + 5 + 1)))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(mean(samples), 4),\n               \" (expected ~\", round(expected_mean, 4), \")\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(sd(samples), 4),\n               \" (expected ~\", round(expected_sd, 4), \")\\n\"))\n\n    ks <- ks.test(samples, \"pbeta\", shape1 = 2, shape2 = 5)\n    cat(paste0(\"  KS test p-value: \", round(ks$p.value, 4), \"\\n\"))\n\n    abs(mean(samples) - expected_mean) < 0.05 && ks$p.value > 0.01\n  })\n\n  # ---- Sampling Tests (Log-Normal via unnormalized density) ----\n  cat(\"--- Unnormalized Density Test ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_unnormalized_density\", {\n    cat(\"  Sampling from unnormalized Gaussian: f(x) = exp(-x^2/2)...\\n\")\n    set.seed(33)\n    unnorm_gauss <- function(x) exp(-x^2 / 2)\n    samples <- ars(unnorm_gauss, n = 3000, lb = -Inf, ub = Inf)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(mean(samples), 4), \" (expected ~0)\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(sd(samples), 4), \" (expected ~1)\\n\"))\n\n    ks <- ks.test(samples, \"pnorm\")\n    cat(paste0(\"  KS test p-value: \", round(ks$p.value, 4), \"\\n\"))\n\n    abs(mean(samples)) < 0.15 && abs(sd(samples) - 1) < 0.15 && ks$p.value > 0.01\n  })\n\n  # ---- Module-Level Tests ----\n  cat(\"--- Module-Level Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_eval_log_density\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing eval_log_density with dnorm...\\n\")\n    result <- eval_log_density(dnorm, 0)\n    expected <- log(dnorm(0))\n    cat(paste0(\"  log(dnorm(0)) = \", round(result, 6), \" (expected \", round(expected, 6), \")\\n\"))\n    abs(result - expected) < 1e-10\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_check_log_concavity_valid\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing log-concavity check on concave data...\\n\")\n    # h(x) = -x^2 is concave\n    x <- c(-2, -1, 0, 1, 2)\n    h <- -x^2\n    result <- tryCatch({ check_log_concavity(x, h); TRUE },\n                       error = function(e) FALSE)\n    result\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_check_log_concavity_invalid\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing log-concavity check on convex data...\\n\")\n    # h(x) = x^2 is convex (NOT concave)\n    x <- c(-2, -1, 0, 1, 2)\n    h <- x^2\n    result <- tryCatch({ check_log_concavity(x, h); FALSE },\n                       error = function(e) grepl(\"[Ll]og-concav\", e$message))\n    result\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_upper_hull_above_density\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that upper hull is above log-density at test points...\\n\")\n    set.seed(11)\n    x_abs <- c(-3, -1, 0, 1, 3)\n    h_abs <- eval_log_density(dnorm, x_abs)\n    hull <- compute_upper_hull(x_abs, h_abs, -Inf, Inf)\n\n    # Check at many random points\n    test_pts <- seq(-2.5, 2.5, by = 0.1)\n    h_test <- eval_log_density(dnorm, test_pts)\n    u_test <- eval_upper_hull(test_pts, hull)\n\n    all_above <- all(u_test >= h_test - 1e-10)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Upper hull >= log-density at all \", length(test_pts), \" test points: \", all_above, \"\\n\"))\n    all_above\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_lower_hull_below_density\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that lower hull is below log-density at test points...\\n\")\n    x_abs <- c(-3, -1, 0, 1, 3)\n    h_abs <- eval_log_density(dnorm, x_abs)\n\n    test_pts <- seq(-2.5, 2.5, by = 0.1)\n    h_test <- eval_log_density(dnorm, test_pts)\n    l_test <- sapply(test_pts, function(x) eval_lower_hull(x, x_abs, h_abs))\n\n    all_below <- all(l_test <= h_test + 1e-10)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Lower hull <= log-density at all \", length(test_pts), \" test points: \", all_below, \"\\n\"))\n    all_below\n  })\n\n  # ---- Summary ----\n  cat(\"============================================================\\n\")\n  cat(paste0(\"  TOTAL: \", pass_count + fail_count, \" tests, \",\n             pass_count, \" passed, \", fail_count, \" failed\\n\"))\n  cat(\"============================================================\\n\")\n\n  invisible(list(passed = pass_count, failed = fail_count))\n}\n\n# Run tests if this script is executed directly\nif (sys.nframe() == 0) {\n  test()\n}\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}
