{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01GPYvq3PjsZNDmktQHPWhZH","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→###############################################################################\n     2→# Adaptive Rejection Sampling (ARS)\n     3→# Based on Gilks, W.R. & Wild, P. (1992)\n     4→# \"Adaptive Rejection Sampling for Gibbs Sampling\"\n     5→# Journal of the Royal Statistical Society: Series C, 41(2), 337-348.\n     6→#\n     7→# Derivative-free implementation using secant-based upper hull construction.\n     8→###############################################################################\n     9→\n    10→# --- Auxiliary / Module Functions ---\n    11→\n    12→#' Evaluate the log of a density function safely\n    13→#' @param f density function (possibly unnormalized)\n    14→#' @param x point(s) at which to evaluate\n    15→#' @return log(f(x)), with protection against f(x) <= 0\n    16→eval_log_density <- function(f, x) {\n    17→  fx <- f(x)\n    18→  if (any(is.na(fx) | is.nan(fx))) {\n    19→    stop(\"Density function returned NA/NaN.\")\n    20→  }\n    21→  if (any(fx < 0)) {\n    22→    stop(\"Density function returned negative value.\")\n    23→  }\n    24→  result <- rep(-Inf, length(fx))\n    25→  pos <- fx > 0\n    26→  result[pos] <- log(fx[pos])\n    27→  return(result)\n    28→}\n    29→\n    30→#' Check log-concavity at three consecutive abscissae\n    31→#' @param abscissae sorted vector of x values\n    32→#' @param h_vals corresponding log-density values h(x)\n    33→#' @return TRUE if log-concave, else stops with error\n    34→check_log_concavity <- function(abscissae, h_vals) {\n    35→  n <- length(abscissae)\n    36→  if (n < 3) return(TRUE)\n    37→  for (i in 1:(n - 2)) {\n    38→    x1 <- abscissae[i];   h1 <- h_vals[i]\n    39→    x2 <- abscissae[i+1]; h2 <- h_vals[i+1]\n    40→    x3 <- abscissae[i+2]; h3 <- h_vals[i+2]\n    41→    slope_left  <- (h2 - h1) / (x2 - x1)\n    42→    slope_right <- (h3 - h2) / (x3 - x2)\n    43→    if (slope_right > slope_left + 1e-10) {\n    44→      stop(paste0(\n    45→        \"Log-concavity violated at x = \", x2,\n    46→        \". Left slope = \", round(slope_left, 6),\n    47→        \", Right slope = \", round(slope_right, 6),\n    48→        \". The density may not be log-concave.\"\n    49→      ))\n    50→    }\n    51→  }\n    52→  return(TRUE)\n    53→}\n    54→\n    55→#' Compute the upper hull for the ARS algorithm (derivative-free version)\n    56→#'\n    57→#' For k abscissae with log-density values, the upper hull is piecewise linear\n    58→#' and lies above the log-density everywhere. It is constructed from the secant\n    59→#' slopes between consecutive abscissae. By log-concavity, secant slopes\n    60→#' decrease: s_1 > s_2 > ... > s_{k-1}.\n    61→#'\n    62→#' At each interior abscissa x_j, the upper hull has a \"tent\" with:\n    63→#'   - left arm:  slope s_j (right secant), valid for x <= x_j\n    64→#'   - right arm: slope s_{j-1} (left secant), valid for x >= x_j\n    65→#'\n    66→#' @param abscissae sorted x values (at least 3)\n    67→#' @param h_vals corresponding h(x) = log(f(x)) values\n    68→#' @param lb lower bound of domain\n    69→#' @param ub upper bound of domain\n    70→#' @return list with segment info for the piecewise linear upper hull\n    71→compute_upper_hull <- function(abscissae, h_vals, lb, ub) {\n    72→  k <- length(abscissae)\n    73→  if (k < 3) stop(\"Need at least 3 abscissae for upper hull construction.\")\n    74→\n    75→  # Secant slopes: s[j] = slope from x_j to x_{j+1}\n    76→  s <- diff(h_vals) / diff(abscissae)\n    77→\n    78→  # Build the list of line segments for the upper hull.\n    79→  # Each segment is defined by a point (px, ph) and slope m:\n    80→  #   u(x) = ph + m * (x - px) = (ph - m*px) + m*x\n    81→  #\n    82→  # Segments from left to right:\n    83→  # 1. x_1's left arm: slope s[1], through (x_1, h_1), for x from lb to z_1\n    84→  # Then for each interior point j = 2,...,k-1:\n    85→  #   left arm:  slope s[j], through (x_j, h_j)\n    86→  #   right arm: slope s[j-1], through (x_j, h_j)\n    87→  # Last: x_k's right arm: slope s[k-1], through (x_k, h_k), for x from z_{k-1} to ub\n    88→\n    89→  n_seg <- 2 * (k - 1)\n    90→  seg_px <- numeric(n_seg)\n    91→  seg_ph <- numeric(n_seg)\n    92→  seg_m  <- numeric(n_seg)\n    93→\n    94→  idx <- 1\n    95→  # First segment: x_1, slope s[1]\n    96→  seg_px[idx] <- abscissae[1]; seg_ph[idx] <- h_vals[1]; seg_m[idx] <- s[1]\n    97→  idx <- idx + 1\n    98→\n    99→  # Interior points\n   100→  for (j in 2:(k-1)) {\n   101→    # Left arm: slope s[j]\n   102→    seg_px[idx] <- abscissae[j]; seg_ph[idx] <- h_vals[j]; seg_m[idx] <- s[j]\n   103→    idx <- idx + 1\n   104→    # Right arm: slope s[j-1]\n   105→    seg_px[idx] <- abscissae[j]; seg_ph[idx] <- h_vals[j]; seg_m[idx] <- s[j-1]\n   106→    idx <- idx + 1\n   107→  }\n   108→\n   109→  # Last segment: x_k, slope s[k-1]\n   110→  seg_px[idx] <- abscissae[k]; seg_ph[idx] <- h_vals[k]; seg_m[idx] <- s[k-1]\n   111→\n   112→  # Convert to intercept form: u(x) = intercept + slope * x\n   113→  seg_int <- seg_ph - seg_m * seg_px\n   114→\n   115→  # Compute breakpoints between consecutive segments\n   116→  bp <- numeric(n_seg - 1)\n   117→  for (i in 1:(n_seg - 1)) {\n   118→    if (seg_px[i] == seg_px[i+1] && seg_ph[i] == seg_ph[i+1]) {\n   119→      # Same point (left/right arm of same tent): breakpoint is at that x\n   120→      bp[i] <- seg_px[i]\n   121→    } else {\n   122→      # Intersection of two different lines\n   123→      bp[i] <- (seg_int[i+1] - seg_int[i]) / (seg_m[i] - seg_m[i+1])\n   124→    }\n   125→  }\n   126→\n   127→  z <- c(lb, bp, ub)\n   128→\n   129→  return(list(\n   130→    z = z,\n   131→    seg_slopes = seg_m,\n   132→    seg_intercepts = seg_int,\n   133→    n_seg = n_seg\n   134→  ))\n   135→}\n   136→\n   137→#' Evaluate the upper hull at a point x\n   138→#' @param x scalar or vector of points\n   139→#' @param hull list from compute_upper_hull\n   140→#' @return u(x), the upper hull value(s)\n   141→eval_upper_hull <- function(x, hull) {\n   142→  z <- hull$z\n   143→  m <- hull$seg_slopes\n   144→  b <- hull$seg_intercepts\n   145→  n_seg <- hull$n_seg\n   146→\n   147→  seg_idx <- findInterval(x, z, rightmost.closed = TRUE)\n   148→  seg_idx <- pmax(1, pmin(seg_idx, n_seg))\n   149→\n   150→  return(b[seg_idx] + m[seg_idx] * x)\n   151→}\n   152→\n   153→#' Evaluate the lower hull (piecewise linear through abscissae) at a point x\n   154→#' @param x scalar point\n   155→#' @param abscissae sorted x values\n   156→#' @param h_vals corresponding h(x) values\n   157→#' @return l(x), the lower hull value. -Inf if outside the range of abscissae.\n   158→eval_lower_hull <- function(x, abscissae, h_vals) {\n   159→  k <- length(abscissae)\n   160→  if (x <= abscissae[1] || x >= abscissae[k]) {\n   161→    return(-Inf)\n   162→  }\n   163→  j <- findInterval(x, abscissae)\n   164→  j <- pmax(1, pmin(j, k - 1))\n   165→  t <- (x - abscissae[j]) / (abscissae[j+1] - abscissae[j])\n   166→  return(h_vals[j] * (1 - t) + h_vals[j+1] * t)\n   167→}\n   168→\n   169→#' Sample from the upper hull (piecewise-exponential distribution)\n   170→#' @param n number of samples to draw\n   171→#' @param hull list from compute_upper_hull\n   172→#' @return vector of n samples\n   173→sample_upper_hull <- function(n, hull) {\n   174→  z <- hull$z\n   175→  m <- hull$seg_slopes\n   176→  b <- hull$seg_intercepts\n   177→  n_seg <- hull$n_seg\n   178→\n   179→  # Compute area under each segment: integral of exp(b + m*x) from z[j] to z[j+1]\n   180→  areas <- numeric(n_seg)\n   181→  for (j in 1:n_seg) {\n   182→    lo <- z[j]\n   183→    hi <- z[j + 1]\n   184→\n   185→    # Handle infinite bounds: for the integral to converge, slope must be favorable\n   186→    if (!is.finite(lo)) {\n   187→      # lo = -Inf. Need m[j] > 0 for convergence\n   188→      if (m[j] <= 0) {\n   189→        areas[j] <- 0\n   190→        next\n   191→      }\n   192→      # Integral of exp(b + m*x) from -Inf to hi = exp(b + m*hi) / m\n   193→      areas[j] <- exp(b[j] + m[j] * hi) / m[j]\n   194→      next\n   195→    }\n   196→    if (!is.finite(hi)) {\n   197→      # hi = +Inf. Need m[j] < 0 for convergence\n   198→      if (m[j] >= 0) {\n   199→        areas[j] <- 0\n   200→        next\n   201→      }\n   202→      # Integral of exp(b + m*x) from lo to Inf = -exp(b + m*lo) / m\n   203→      areas[j] <- -exp(b[j] + m[j] * lo) / m[j]\n   204→      next\n   205→    }\n   206→\n   207→    if (abs(m[j]) < 1e-15) {\n   208→      areas[j] <- exp(b[j]) * (hi - lo)\n   209→    } else {\n   210→      # Integral = (exp(b + m*hi) - exp(b + m*lo)) / m\n   211→      # For numerical stability, factor out the larger value\n   212→      log_hi <- b[j] + m[j] * hi\n   213→      log_lo <- b[j] + m[j] * lo\n   214→      log_max <- max(log_hi, log_lo)\n   215→      areas[j] <- exp(log_max) * (exp(log_hi - log_max) - exp(log_lo - log_max)) / m[j]\n   216→    }\n   217→\n   218→    if (is.na(areas[j]) || !is.finite(areas[j]) || areas[j] < 0) {\n   219→      areas[j] <- 0\n   220→    }\n   221→  }\n   222→\n   223→  total_area <- sum(areas)\n   224→  if (total_area <= 0 || !is.finite(total_area)) {\n   225→    stop(\"Upper hull has zero or infinite area. Check domain bounds and initial abscissae.\")\n   226→  }\n   227→  probs <- areas / total_area\n   228→\n   229→  # Sample segments according to their areas\n   230→  seg_choices <- sample.int(n_seg, size = n, replace = TRUE, prob = probs)\n   231→\n   232→  # For each chosen segment, sample from the truncated exponential via inverse CDF\n   233→  samples <- numeric(n)\n   234→  for (i in 1:n) {\n   235→    j <- seg_choices[i]\n   236→    lo <- z[j]\n   237→    hi <- z[j + 1]\n   238→    u <- runif(1)\n   239→\n   240→    if (abs(m[j]) < 1e-15) {\n   241→      # Uniform on [lo, hi]\n   242→      if (!is.finite(lo)) lo <- hi - 20\n   243→      if (!is.finite(hi)) hi <- lo + 20\n   244→      samples[i] <- lo + u * (hi - lo)\n   245→    } else if (!is.finite(lo)) {\n   246→      # lo = -Inf, m > 0: CDF(x) = exp(m*(x-hi)) for x <= hi\n   247→      # Inverse: x = hi + log(u) / m\n   248→      samples[i] <- hi + log(u) / m[j]\n   249→    } else if (!is.finite(hi)) {\n   250→      # hi = +Inf, m < 0: CDF(x) = 1 - exp(m*(x-lo)) for x >= lo\n   251→      # Inverse: x = lo + log(1 - u) / m  ... but log(1-u) ~ log(u) in distribution\n   252→      samples[i] <- lo + log(u) / m[j]  # since m < 0, this gives x > lo\n   253→    } else {\n   254→      # Both finite: inverse CDF of truncated exponential\n   255→      log_lo <- m[j] * lo\n   256→      log_hi <- m[j] * hi\n   257→      # CDF: F(x) = (exp(m*x) - exp(m*lo)) / (exp(m*hi) - exp(m*lo))\n   258→      # Inverse: x = log(exp(m*lo) + u*(exp(m*hi) - exp(m*lo))) / m\n   259→      log_max <- max(log_lo, log_hi)\n   260→      exp_lo <- exp(log_lo - log_max)\n   261→      exp_hi <- exp(log_hi - log_max)\n   262→      samples[i] <- (log_max + log(exp_lo + u * (exp_hi - exp_lo))) / m[j]\n   263→    }\n   264→  }\n   265→  return(samples)\n   266→}\n   267→\n   268→#' Choose initial abscissae for the ARS algorithm\n   269→#' Ensures at least 3 points with positive density, and that\n   270→#' boundary slopes are compatible with infinite domains.\n   271→#' @param lb lower bound\n   272→#' @param ub upper bound\n   273→#' @param f density function\n   274→#' @param n_init number of initial points (default 5, minimum 3)\n   275→#' @return vector of initial abscissae\n   276→choose_initial_abscissae <- function(lb, ub, f, n_init = 20) {\n   277→  n_init <- max(n_init, 5)\n   278→\n   279→  # Generate candidate points\n   280→  if (is.finite(lb) && is.finite(ub)) {\n   281→    margin <- (ub - lb) * 0.02\n   282→    pts <- seq(lb + margin, ub - margin, length.out = n_init)\n   283→  } else if (is.finite(lb) && !is.finite(ub)) {\n   284→    pts <- lb + c(0.1, 0.5, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 12, 18, 25, 35, 50,\n   285→                  0.2, 0.8, 1.5, 4, 7, 10, 15, 20)[1:n_init]\n   286→  } else if (!is.finite(lb) && is.finite(ub)) {\n   287→    pts <- ub - c(0.1, 0.5, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 12, 18, 25, 35, 50,\n   288→                  0.2, 0.8, 1.5, 4, 7, 10, 15, 20)[1:n_init]\n   289→  } else {\n   290→    # Both infinite: center around 0\n   291→    pts <- c(seq(-4, 4, length.out = n_init))\n   292→  }\n   293→\n   294→  pts <- sort(unique(pts))\n   295→\n   296→  # Keep only points with positive density\n   297→  vals <- f(pts)\n   298→  good <- which(vals > 0 & !is.na(vals) & is.finite(vals))\n   299→  if (length(good) < 3) {\n   300→    stop(\"Cannot find at least 3 initial abscissae with positive density. Check domain bounds.\")\n   301→  }\n   302→  pts <- pts[good]\n   303→\n   304→  # Compute log-density\n   305→  h <- log(vals[good])\n   306→\n   307→  # For infinite left bound: need first secant slope > 0\n   308→  # For infinite right bound: need last secant slope < 0\n   309→  # Trim from each end until this is satisfied\n   310→  if (!is.finite(lb)) {\n   311→    while (length(pts) > 3) {\n   312→      s1 <- (h[2] - h[1]) / (pts[2] - pts[1])\n   313→      if (s1 > 0) break\n   314→      pts <- pts[-1]; h <- h[-1]\n   315→    }\n   316→  }\n   317→  if (!is.finite(ub)) {\n   318→    while (length(pts) > 3) {\n   319→      k <- length(pts)\n   320→      sk <- (h[k] - h[k-1]) / (pts[k] - pts[k-1])\n   321→      if (sk < 0) break\n   322→      pts <- pts[-k]; h <- h[-k]\n   323→    }\n   324→  }\n   325→\n   326→  if (length(pts) < 3) {\n   327→    stop(\"Cannot find suitable initial abscissae. The density mode may be outside the initial search range.\")\n   328→  }\n   329→\n   330→  return(pts)\n   331→}\n   332→\n   333→#' Validate inputs to the ars function\n   334→#' @param f density function\n   335→#' @param n number of samples\n   336→#' @param lb lower bound\n   337→#' @param ub upper bound\n   338→validate_inputs <- function(f, n, lb, ub) {\n   339→  if (!is.function(f)) {\n   340→    stop(\"'f' must be a function.\")\n   341→  }\n   342→  if (!is.numeric(n) || length(n) != 1 || n < 1 || n != as.integer(n)) {\n   343→    stop(\"'n' must be a positive integer.\")\n   344→  }\n   345→  if (!is.numeric(lb) || length(lb) != 1) {\n   346→    stop(\"'lb' must be a single numeric value.\")\n   347→  }\n   348→  if (!is.numeric(ub) || length(ub) != 1) {\n   349→    stop(\"'ub' must be a single numeric value.\")\n   350→  }\n   351→  if (lb >= ub) {\n   352→    stop(\"Lower bound must be strictly less than upper bound.\")\n   353→  }\n   354→}\n   355→\n   356→# --- Main ARS Function ---\n   357→\n   358→#' Adaptive Rejection Sampling\n   359→#'\n   360→#' @param f density function (possibly unnormalized), must be log-concave.\n   361→#'          Should accept a vector and return a vector of density values.\n   362→#' @param support optional 2-element vector c(lb, ub) for the domain\n   363→#' @param n number of samples to generate (default 100)\n   364→#' @param lb lower bound of the domain (default -Inf)\n   365→#' @param ub upper bound of the domain (default Inf)\n   366→#' @param ... additional arguments (currently unused)\n   367→#' @return vector of n samples from the target distribution\n   368→ars <- function(f, support = NULL, n = 100, lb = -Inf, ub = Inf, ...) {\n   369→  # Handle the support vector argument (alternative call pattern)\n   370→  if (!is.null(support) && is.numeric(support) && length(support) == 2) {\n   371→    lb <- support[1]\n   372→    ub <- support[2]\n   373→  }\n   374→\n   375→  # Validate inputs\n   376→  validate_inputs(f, n, lb, ub)\n   377→\n   378→  # Choose initial abscissae (at least 3)\n   379→  abscissae <- choose_initial_abscissae(lb, ub, f)\n   380→  h_vals <- eval_log_density(f, abscissae)\n   381→\n   382→  # Check initial log-concavity\n   383→  check_log_concavity(abscissae, h_vals)\n   384→\n   385→  # Main sampling loop\n   386→  samples <- numeric(n)\n   387→  count <- 0\n   388→  max_iter <- n * 200  # safety limit\n   389→  iter <- 0\n   390→\n   391→  while (count < n && iter < max_iter) {\n   392→    iter <- iter + 1\n   393→\n   394→    # Build upper hull\n   395→    hull <- compute_upper_hull(abscissae, h_vals, lb, ub)\n   396→\n   397→    # Sample a candidate from the upper hull\n   398→    x_star <- sample_upper_hull(1, hull)\n   399→\n   400→    # Squeeze and rejection test\n   401→    u <- runif(1)\n   402→    u_val <- eval_upper_hull(x_star, hull)\n   403→    l_val <- eval_lower_hull(x_star, abscissae, h_vals)\n   404→\n   405→    if (u <= exp(l_val - u_val)) {\n   406→      # Accept by squeeze test (no density evaluation needed)\n   407→      count <- count + 1\n   408→      samples[count] <- x_star\n   409→    } else {\n   410→      # Need to evaluate the true log-density\n   411→      h_star <- eval_log_density(f, x_star)\n   412→\n   413→      if (u <= exp(h_star - u_val)) {\n   414→        # Accept by rejection test\n   415→        count <- count + 1\n   416→        samples[count] <- x_star\n   417→      }\n   418→\n   419→      # Update abscissae with new point (whether accepted or not)\n   420→      insert_pos <- findInterval(x_star, abscissae) + 1\n   421→      abscissae <- append(abscissae, x_star, after = insert_pos - 1)\n   422→      h_vals <- append(h_vals, h_star, after = insert_pos - 1)\n   423→\n   424→      # Re-check log-concavity with new point\n   425→      check_log_concavity(abscissae, h_vals)\n   426→    }\n   427→  }\n   428→\n   429→  if (count < n) {\n   430→    warning(paste(\"Only generated\", count, \"out of\", n, \"requested samples.\"))\n   431→  }\n   432→\n   433→  return(samples)\n   434→}\n   435→\n   436→# --- Test Function ---\n   437→\n   438→#' Run formal tests of the ARS implementation\n   439→#' Compares samples against known distributions using statistical tests.\n   440→#' Output format: \"TEST_NAME: PASS\" or \"TEST_NAME: FAIL\"\n   441→test <- function() {\n   442→  cat(\"============================================================\\n\")\n   443→  cat(\"     Adaptive Rejection Sampling (ARS) - Test Suite\\n\")\n   444→  cat(\"============================================================\\n\\n\")\n   445→\n   446→  pass_count <- 0\n   447→  fail_count <- 0\n   448→\n   449→  run_test <- function(name, expr) {\n   450→    result <- tryCatch(expr, error = function(e) {\n   451→      cat(paste0(\"  Error: \", e$message, \"\\n\"))\n   452→      return(FALSE)\n   453→    })\n   454→    if (isTRUE(result)) {\n   455→      cat(paste0(name, \": PASS\\n\\n\"))\n   456→      pass_count <<- pass_count + 1\n   457→    } else {\n   458→      cat(paste0(name, \": FAIL\\n\\n\"))\n   459→      fail_count <<- fail_count + 1\n   460→    }\n   461→  }\n   462→\n   463→  # ---- Input Validation Tests ----\n   464→  cat(\"--- Input Validation Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n   465→\n   466→  run_test(\"test_negative_n\", {\n   467→    cat(\"  Testing that negative n is rejected...\\n\")\n   468→    tryCatch({ ars(dnorm, n = -5); FALSE },\n   469→             error = function(e) grepl(\"positive integer\", e$message))\n   470→  })\n   471→\n   472→  run_test(\"test_zero_n\", {\n   473→    cat(\"  Testing that n=0 is rejected...\\n\")\n   474→    tryCatch({ ars(dnorm, n = 0); FALSE },\n   475→             error = function(e) grepl(\"positive integer\", e$message))\n   476→  })\n   477→\n   478→  run_test(\"test_non_integer_n\", {\n   479→    cat(\"  Testing that non-integer n is rejected...\\n\")\n   480→    tryCatch({ ars(dnorm, n = 3.5); FALSE },\n   481→             error = function(e) grepl(\"positive integer\", e$message))\n   482→  })\n   483→\n   484→  run_test(\"test_invalid_bounds\", {\n   485→    cat(\"  Testing that lb >= ub is rejected...\\n\")\n   486→    tryCatch({ ars(dnorm, n = 10, lb = 5, ub = 2); FALSE },\n   487→             error = function(e) grepl(\"strictly less\", e$message))\n   488→  })\n   489→\n   490→  run_test(\"test_non_function_f\", {\n   491→    cat(\"  Testing that non-function f is rejected...\\n\")\n   492→    tryCatch({ ars(\"not_a_function\", n = 10); FALSE },\n   493→             error = function(e) grepl(\"must be a function\", e$message))\n   494→  })\n   495→\n   496→  # ---- Log-Concavity Tests ----\n   497→  cat(\"--- Log-Concavity Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n   498→\n   499→  run_test(\"test_bimodal_rejection\", {\n   500→    cat(\"  Testing rejection of bimodal (non-log-concave) density...\\n\")\n   501→    bimodal <- function(x) 0.5 * dnorm(x, -3, 0.5) + 0.5 * dnorm(x, 3, 0.5)\n   502→    tryCatch({ ars(bimodal, n = 1000, lb = -10, ub = 10); FALSE },\n   503→             error = function(e) grepl(\"[Ll]og-concav\", e$message))\n   504→  })\n   505→\n   506→  run_test(\"test_cauchy_rejection\", {\n   507→    cat(\"  Testing rejection of Cauchy (non-log-concave) density...\\n\")\n   508→    tryCatch({ ars(dcauchy, n = 1000, lb = -100, ub = 100); FALSE },\n   509→             error = function(e) grepl(\"[Ll]og-concav\", e$message))\n   510→  })\n   511→\n   512→  # ---- Module-Level Tests ----\n   513→  cat(\"--- Module-Level Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n   514→\n   515→  run_test(\"test_eval_log_density\", {\n   516→    cat(\"  Testing eval_log_density with dnorm...\\n\")\n   517→    result <- eval_log_density(dnorm, 0)\n   518→    expected <- log(dnorm(0))\n   519→    cat(paste0(\"  log(dnorm(0)) = \", round(result, 6),\n   520→               \" (expected \", round(expected, 6), \")\\n\"))\n   521→    abs(result - expected) < 1e-10\n   522→  })\n   523→\n   524→  run_test(\"test_check_log_concavity_valid\", {\n   525→    cat(\"  Testing log-concavity check on concave data...\\n\")\n   526→    x <- c(-2, -1, 0, 1, 2)\n   527→    h <- -x^2\n   528→    tryCatch({ check_log_concavity(x, h); TRUE }, error = function(e) FALSE)\n   529→  })\n   530→\n   531→  run_test(\"test_check_log_concavity_invalid\", {\n   532→    cat(\"  Testing log-concavity check on convex data...\\n\")\n   533→    x <- c(-2, -1, 0, 1, 2)\n   534→    h <- x^2  # convex, not concave\n   535→    tryCatch({ check_log_concavity(x, h); FALSE },\n   536→             error = function(e) grepl(\"[Ll]og-concav\", e$message))\n   537→  })\n   538→\n   539→  run_test(\"test_upper_hull_above_density\", {\n   540→    cat(\"  Testing that upper hull >= log-density at test points...\\n\")\n   541→    x_abs <- c(-3, -1, 0, 1, 3)\n   542→    h_abs <- eval_log_density(dnorm, x_abs)\n   543→    hull <- compute_upper_hull(x_abs, h_abs, -Inf, Inf)\n   544→    test_pts <- seq(-2.5, 2.5, by = 0.1)\n   545→    h_test <- eval_log_density(dnorm, test_pts)\n   546→    u_test <- eval_upper_hull(test_pts, hull)\n   547→    all_above <- all(u_test >= h_test - 1e-6)\n   548→    cat(paste0(\"  Upper hull >= log-density at all \", length(test_pts),\n   549→               \" test points: \", all_above, \"\\n\"))\n   550→    all_above\n   551→  })\n   552→\n   553→  run_test(\"test_lower_hull_below_density\", {\n   554→    cat(\"  Testing that lower hull <= log-density at test points...\\n\")\n   555→    x_abs <- c(-3, -1, 0, 1, 3)\n   556→    h_abs <- eval_log_density(dnorm, x_abs)\n   557→    test_pts <- seq(-2.5, 2.5, by = 0.1)\n   558→    h_test <- eval_log_density(dnorm, test_pts)\n   559→    l_test <- sapply(test_pts, function(x) eval_lower_hull(x, x_abs, h_abs))\n   560→    all_below <- all(l_test <= h_test + 1e-6)\n   561→    cat(paste0(\"  Lower hull <= log-density at all \", length(test_pts),\n   562→               \" test points: \", all_below, \"\\n\"))\n   563→    all_below\n   564→  })\n   565→\n   566→  # ---- Sampling Tests: Standard Normal ----\n   567→  cat(\"--- Standard Normal Distribution Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n   568→\n   569→  run_test(\"test_normal_unbounded\", {\n   570→    cat(\"  Sampling 5000 points from N(0,1) on (-Inf, Inf)...\\n\")\n   571→    set.seed(42)\n   572→    samples <- ars(n = 5000, f = dnorm, lb = -Inf, ub = Inf)\n   573→    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)\n   574→    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(m, 4), \" (expected ~0)\\n\"))\n   575→    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(s, 4), \" (expected ~1)\\n\"))\n   576→    writeLines(as.character(samples), \"/app/normal_samples.txt\")\n   577→    cat(\"  Saved samples to /app/normal_samples.txt\\n\")\n   578→    ks <- ks.test(samples, \"pnorm\")\n   579→    cat(paste0(\"  KS test p-value: \", round(ks$p.value, 4), \"\\n\"))\n   580→    abs(m) < 0.15 && abs(s - 1) < 0.15 && ks$p.value > 0.01\n   581→  })\n   582→\n   583→  run_test(\"test_normal_bounded\", {\n   584→    cat(\"  Sampling 2000 points from N(0,1) on [-5, 5]...\\n\")\n   585→    set.seed(123)\n   586→    samples <- ars(dnorm, c(-5, 5), n = 2000)\n   587→    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)\n   588→    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(m, 4), \"\\n\"))\n   589→    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(s, 4), \"\\n\"))\n   590→    cat(paste0(\"  Range: [\", round(min(samples), 4), \", \", round(max(samples), 4), \"]\\n\"))\n   591→    all(samples >= -5 & samples <= 5) && abs(m) < 0.2\n   592→  })\n   593→\n   594→  run_test(\"test_normal_shifted\", {\n   595→    cat(\"  Sampling 3000 points from N(5, 2^2) on (-Inf, Inf)...\\n\")\n   596→    set.seed(200)\n   597→    shifted_norm <- function(x) dnorm(x, mean = 5, sd = 2)\n   598→    samples <- ars(shifted_norm, n = 3000, lb = -Inf, ub = Inf)\n   599→    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)\n   600→    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(m, 4), \" (expected ~5)\\n\"))\n   601→    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(s, 4), \" (expected ~2)\\n\"))\n   602→    ks <- ks.test(samples, \"pnorm\", mean = 5, sd = 2)\n   603→    cat(paste0(\"  KS test p-value: \", round(ks$p.value, 4), \"\\n\"))\n   604→    abs(m - 5) < 0.3 && abs(s - 2) < 0.3 && ks$p.value > 0.01\n   605→  })\n   606→\n   607→  # ---- Sampling Tests: Exponential ----\n   608→  cat(\"--- Exponential Distribution Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n   609→\n   610→  run_test(\"test_exponential_sampling\", {\n   611→    cat(\"  Sampling 5000 points from Exp(1) on [0.001, 100]...\\n\")\n   612→    set.seed(99)\n   613→    samples <- ars(dexp, c(0.001, 100), n = 5000)\n   614→    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)\n   615→    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(m, 4), \" (expected ~1)\\n\"))\n   616→    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(s, 4), \" (expected ~1)\\n\"))\n   617→    writeLines(as.character(samples), \"/app/exponential_samples.txt\")\n   618→    cat(\"  Saved samples to /app/exponential_samples.txt\\n\")\n   619→    ks <- ks.test(samples, \"pexp\")\n   620→    cat(paste0(\"  KS test p-value: \", round(ks$p.value, 4), \"\\n\"))\n   621→    abs(m - 1) < 0.2 && ks$p.value > 0.01\n   622→  })\n   623→\n   624→  # ---- Sampling Tests: Gamma ----\n   625→  cat(\"--- Gamma Distribution Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n   626→\n   627→  run_test(\"test_gamma_sampling\", {\n   628→    cat(\"  Sampling 3000 points from Gamma(3, 1) on [0.01, 30]...\\n\")\n   629→    set.seed(77)\n   630→    gamma_dens <- function(x) dgamma(x, shape = 3, rate = 1)\n   631→    samples <- ars(gamma_dens, c(0.01, 30), n = 3000)\n   632→    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)\n   633→    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(m, 4), \" (expected ~3)\\n\"))\n   634→    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(s, 4), \" (expected ~1.73)\\n\"))\n   635→    ks <- ks.test(samples, \"pgamma\", shape = 3, rate = 1)\n   636→    cat(paste0(\"  KS test p-value: \", round(ks$p.value, 4), \"\\n\"))\n   637→    abs(m - 3) < 0.4 && ks$p.value > 0.01\n   638→  })\n   639→\n   640→  # ---- Sampling Tests: Beta ----\n   641→  cat(\"--- Beta Distribution Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n   642→\n   643→  run_test(\"test_beta_sampling\", {\n   644→    cat(\"  Sampling 3000 points from Beta(2, 5) on [0.01, 0.99]...\\n\")\n   645→    set.seed(55)\n   646→    beta_dens <- function(x) dbeta(x, shape1 = 2, shape2 = 5)\n   647→    samples <- ars(beta_dens, c(0.01, 0.99), n = 3000)\n   648→    expected_mean <- 2 / (2 + 5)\n   649→    expected_sd <- sqrt(2 * 5 / ((2 + 5)^2 * (2 + 5 + 1)))\n   650→    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)\n   651→    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(m, 4),\n   652→               \" (expected ~\", round(expected_mean, 4), \")\\n\"))\n   653→    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(s, 4),\n   654→               \" (expected ~\", round(expected_sd, 4), \")\\n\"))\n   655→    ks <- ks.test(samples, \"pbeta\", shape1 = 2, shape2 = 5)\n   656→    cat(paste0(\"  KS test p-value: \", round(ks$p.value, 4), \"\\n\"))\n   657→    abs(m - expected_mean) < 0.05 && ks$p.value > 0.01\n   658→  })\n   659→\n   660→  # ---- Sampling Tests: Unnormalized density ----\n   661→  cat(\"--- Unnormalized Density Test ---\\n\\n\")\n   662→\n   663→  run_test(\"test_unnormalized_density\", {\n   664→    cat(\"  Sampling from unnormalized Gaussian: f(x) = exp(-x^2/2)...\\n\")\n   665→    set.seed(33)\n   666→    unnorm_gauss <- function(x) exp(-x^2 / 2)\n   667→    samples <- ars(unnorm_gauss, n = 3000, lb = -Inf, ub = Inf)\n   668→    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)\n   669→    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(m, 4), \" (expected ~0)\\n\"))\n   670→    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(s, 4), \" (expected ~1)\\n\"))\n   671→    ks <- ks.test(samples, \"pnorm\")\n   672→    cat(paste0(\"  KS test p-value: \", round(ks$p.value, 4), \"\\n\"))\n   673→    abs(m) < 0.15 && abs(s - 1) < 0.15 && ks$p.value > 0.01\n   674→  })\n   675→\n   676→  # ---- Summary ----\n   677→  cat(\"============================================================\\n\")\n   678→  total <- pass_count + fail_count\n   679→  cat(paste0(\"  TOTAL: \", total, \" tests, \",\n   680→             pass_count, \" passed, \", fail_count, \" failed\\n\"))\n   681→  cat(\"============================================================\\n\")\n   682→\n   683→  invisible(list(passed = pass_count, failed = fail_count))\n   684→}\n   685→\n   686→# Run tests if this script is executed directly\n   687→if (sys.nframe() == 0) {\n   688→  test()\n   689→}\n   690→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"78903e02-0918-48fe-a339-296475e92493","uuid":"ae715a22-a242-43b9-b0af-9169183b9be0","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/app/ars.R","content":"###############################################################################\n# Adaptive Rejection Sampling (ARS)\n# Based on Gilks, W.R. & Wild, P. (1992)\n# \"Adaptive Rejection Sampling for Gibbs Sampling\"\n# Journal of the Royal Statistical Society: Series C, 41(2), 337-348.\n#\n# Derivative-free implementation using secant-based upper hull construction.\n###############################################################################\n\n# --- Auxiliary / Module Functions ---\n\n#' Evaluate the log of a density function safely\n#' @param f density function (possibly unnormalized)\n#' @param x point(s) at which to evaluate\n#' @return log(f(x)), with protection against f(x) <= 0\neval_log_density <- function(f, x) {\n  fx <- f(x)\n  if (any(is.na(fx) | is.nan(fx))) {\n    stop(\"Density function returned NA/NaN.\")\n  }\n  if (any(fx < 0)) {\n    stop(\"Density function returned negative value.\")\n  }\n  result <- rep(-Inf, length(fx))\n  pos <- fx > 0\n  result[pos] <- log(fx[pos])\n  return(result)\n}\n\n#' Check log-concavity at three consecutive abscissae\n#' @param abscissae sorted vector of x values\n#' @param h_vals corresponding log-density values h(x)\n#' @return TRUE if log-concave, else stops with error\ncheck_log_concavity <- function(abscissae, h_vals) {\n  n <- length(abscissae)\n  if (n < 3) return(TRUE)\n  for (i in 1:(n - 2)) {\n    x1 <- abscissae[i];   h1 <- h_vals[i]\n    x2 <- abscissae[i+1]; h2 <- h_vals[i+1]\n    x3 <- abscissae[i+2]; h3 <- h_vals[i+2]\n    slope_left  <- (h2 - h1) / (x2 - x1)\n    slope_right <- (h3 - h2) / (x3 - x2)\n    if (slope_right > slope_left + 1e-10) {\n      stop(paste0(\n        \"Log-concavity violated at x = \", x2,\n        \". Left slope = \", round(slope_left, 6),\n        \", Right slope = \", round(slope_right, 6),\n        \". The density may not be log-concave.\"\n      ))\n    }\n  }\n  return(TRUE)\n}\n\n#' Compute the upper hull for the ARS algorithm (derivative-free version)\n#'\n#' For k abscissae with log-density values, the upper hull is piecewise linear\n#' and lies above the log-density everywhere. It is constructed from the secant\n#' slopes between consecutive abscissae. By log-concavity, secant slopes\n#' decrease: s_1 > s_2 > ... > s_{k-1}.\n#'\n#' At each interior abscissa x_j, the upper hull has a \"tent\" with:\n#'   - left arm:  slope s_j (right secant), valid for x <= x_j\n#'   - right arm: slope s_{j-1} (left secant), valid for x >= x_j\n#'\n#' @param abscissae sorted x values (at least 3)\n#' @param h_vals corresponding h(x) = log(f(x)) values\n#' @param lb lower bound of domain\n#' @param ub upper bound of domain\n#' @return list with segment info for the piecewise linear upper hull\ncompute_upper_hull <- function(abscissae, h_vals, lb, ub) {\n  k <- length(abscissae)\n  if (k < 3) stop(\"Need at least 3 abscissae for upper hull construction.\")\n\n  # Secant slopes: s[j] = slope from x_j to x_{j+1}\n  s <- diff(h_vals) / diff(abscissae)\n\n  # Build the list of line segments for the upper hull.\n  # Each segment is defined by a point (px, ph) and slope m:\n  #   u(x) = ph + m * (x - px) = (ph - m*px) + m*x\n  #\n  # Segments from left to right:\n  # 1. x_1's left arm: slope s[1], through (x_1, h_1), for x from lb to z_1\n  # Then for each interior point j = 2,...,k-1:\n  #   left arm:  slope s[j], through (x_j, h_j)\n  #   right arm: slope s[j-1], through (x_j, h_j)\n  # Last: x_k's right arm: slope s[k-1], through (x_k, h_k), for x from z_{k-1} to ub\n\n  n_seg <- 2 * (k - 1)\n  seg_px <- numeric(n_seg)\n  seg_ph <- numeric(n_seg)\n  seg_m  <- numeric(n_seg)\n\n  idx <- 1\n  # First segment: x_1, slope s[1]\n  seg_px[idx] <- abscissae[1]; seg_ph[idx] <- h_vals[1]; seg_m[idx] <- s[1]\n  idx <- idx + 1\n\n  # Interior points\n  for (j in 2:(k-1)) {\n    # Left arm: slope s[j]\n    seg_px[idx] <- abscissae[j]; seg_ph[idx] <- h_vals[j]; seg_m[idx] <- s[j]\n    idx <- idx + 1\n    # Right arm: slope s[j-1]\n    seg_px[idx] <- abscissae[j]; seg_ph[idx] <- h_vals[j]; seg_m[idx] <- s[j-1]\n    idx <- idx + 1\n  }\n\n  # Last segment: x_k, slope s[k-1]\n  seg_px[idx] <- abscissae[k]; seg_ph[idx] <- h_vals[k]; seg_m[idx] <- s[k-1]\n\n  # Convert to intercept form: u(x) = intercept + slope * x\n  seg_int <- seg_ph - seg_m * seg_px\n\n  # Compute breakpoints between consecutive segments\n  bp <- numeric(n_seg - 1)\n  for (i in 1:(n_seg - 1)) {\n    if (seg_px[i] == seg_px[i+1] && seg_ph[i] == seg_ph[i+1]) {\n      # Same point (left/right arm of same tent): breakpoint is at that x\n      bp[i] <- seg_px[i]\n    } else {\n      # Intersection of two different lines\n      bp[i] <- (seg_int[i+1] - seg_int[i]) / (seg_m[i] - seg_m[i+1])\n    }\n  }\n\n  z <- c(lb, bp, ub)\n\n  return(list(\n    z = z,\n    seg_slopes = seg_m,\n    seg_intercepts = seg_int,\n    n_seg = n_seg\n  ))\n}\n\n#' Evaluate the upper hull at a point x\n#' @param x scalar or vector of points\n#' @param hull list from compute_upper_hull\n#' @return u(x), the upper hull value(s)\neval_upper_hull <- function(x, hull) {\n  z <- hull$z\n  m <- hull$seg_slopes\n  b <- hull$seg_intercepts\n  n_seg <- hull$n_seg\n\n  seg_idx <- findInterval(x, z, rightmost.closed = TRUE)\n  seg_idx <- pmax(1, pmin(seg_idx, n_seg))\n\n  return(b[seg_idx] + m[seg_idx] * x)\n}\n\n#' Evaluate the lower hull (piecewise linear through abscissae) at a point x\n#' @param x scalar point\n#' @param abscissae sorted x values\n#' @param h_vals corresponding h(x) values\n#' @return l(x), the lower hull value. -Inf if outside the range of abscissae.\neval_lower_hull <- function(x, abscissae, h_vals) {\n  k <- length(abscissae)\n  if (x <= abscissae[1] || x >= abscissae[k]) {\n    return(-Inf)\n  }\n  j <- findInterval(x, abscissae)\n  j <- pmax(1, pmin(j, k - 1))\n  t <- (x - abscissae[j]) / (abscissae[j+1] - abscissae[j])\n  return(h_vals[j] * (1 - t) + h_vals[j+1] * t)\n}\n\n#' Sample from the upper hull (piecewise-exponential distribution)\n#' @param n number of samples to draw\n#' @param hull list from compute_upper_hull\n#' @return vector of n samples\nsample_upper_hull <- function(n, hull) {\n  z <- hull$z\n  m <- hull$seg_slopes\n  b <- hull$seg_intercepts\n  n_seg <- hull$n_seg\n\n  # Compute area under each segment: integral of exp(b + m*x) from z[j] to z[j+1]\n  areas <- numeric(n_seg)\n  for (j in 1:n_seg) {\n    lo <- z[j]\n    hi <- z[j + 1]\n\n    # Handle infinite bounds: for the integral to converge, slope must be favorable\n    if (!is.finite(lo)) {\n      # lo = -Inf. Need m[j] > 0 for convergence\n      if (m[j] <= 0) {\n        areas[j] <- 0\n        next\n      }\n      # Integral of exp(b + m*x) from -Inf to hi = exp(b + m*hi) / m\n      areas[j] <- exp(b[j] + m[j] * hi) / m[j]\n      next\n    }\n    if (!is.finite(hi)) {\n      # hi = +Inf. Need m[j] < 0 for convergence\n      if (m[j] >= 0) {\n        areas[j] <- 0\n        next\n      }\n      # Integral of exp(b + m*x) from lo to Inf = -exp(b + m*lo) / m\n      areas[j] <- -exp(b[j] + m[j] * lo) / m[j]\n      next\n    }\n\n    if (abs(m[j]) < 1e-15) {\n      areas[j] <- exp(b[j]) * (hi - lo)\n    } else {\n      # Integral = (exp(b + m*hi) - exp(b + m*lo)) / m\n      # For numerical stability, factor out the larger value\n      log_hi <- b[j] + m[j] * hi\n      log_lo <- b[j] + m[j] * lo\n      log_max <- max(log_hi, log_lo)\n      areas[j] <- exp(log_max) * (exp(log_hi - log_max) - exp(log_lo - log_max)) / m[j]\n    }\n\n    if (is.na(areas[j]) || !is.finite(areas[j]) || areas[j] < 0) {\n      areas[j] <- 0\n    }\n  }\n\n  total_area <- sum(areas)\n  if (total_area <= 0 || !is.finite(total_area)) {\n    stop(\"Upper hull has zero or infinite area. Check domain bounds and initial abscissae.\")\n  }\n  probs <- areas / total_area\n\n  # Sample segments according to their areas\n  seg_choices <- sample.int(n_seg, size = n, replace = TRUE, prob = probs)\n\n  # For each chosen segment, sample from the truncated exponential via inverse CDF\n  samples <- numeric(n)\n  for (i in 1:n) {\n    j <- seg_choices[i]\n    lo <- z[j]\n    hi <- z[j + 1]\n    u <- runif(1)\n\n    if (abs(m[j]) < 1e-15) {\n      # Uniform on [lo, hi]\n      if (!is.finite(lo)) lo <- hi - 20\n      if (!is.finite(hi)) hi <- lo + 20\n      samples[i] <- lo + u * (hi - lo)\n    } else if (!is.finite(lo)) {\n      # lo = -Inf, m > 0: CDF(x) = exp(m*(x-hi)) for x <= hi\n      # Inverse: x = hi + log(u) / m\n      samples[i] <- hi + log(u) / m[j]\n    } else if (!is.finite(hi)) {\n      # hi = +Inf, m < 0: CDF(x) = 1 - exp(m*(x-lo)) for x >= lo\n      # Inverse: x = lo + log(1 - u) / m  ... but log(1-u) ~ log(u) in distribution\n      samples[i] <- lo + log(u) / m[j]  # since m < 0, this gives x > lo\n    } else {\n      # Both finite: inverse CDF of truncated exponential\n      log_lo <- m[j] * lo\n      log_hi <- m[j] * hi\n      # CDF: F(x) = (exp(m*x) - exp(m*lo)) / (exp(m*hi) - exp(m*lo))\n      # Inverse: x = log(exp(m*lo) + u*(exp(m*hi) - exp(m*lo))) / m\n      log_max <- max(log_lo, log_hi)\n      exp_lo <- exp(log_lo - log_max)\n      exp_hi <- exp(log_hi - log_max)\n      samples[i] <- (log_max + log(exp_lo + u * (exp_hi - exp_lo))) / m[j]\n    }\n  }\n  return(samples)\n}\n\n#' Choose initial abscissae for the ARS algorithm\n#' Ensures at least 3 points with positive density, and that\n#' boundary slopes are compatible with infinite domains.\n#' @param lb lower bound\n#' @param ub upper bound\n#' @param f density function\n#' @param n_init number of initial points (default 5, minimum 3)\n#' @return vector of initial abscissae\nchoose_initial_abscissae <- function(lb, ub, f, n_init = 20) {\n  n_init <- max(n_init, 5)\n\n  # Generate candidate points\n  if (is.finite(lb) && is.finite(ub)) {\n    margin <- (ub - lb) * 0.02\n    pts <- seq(lb + margin, ub - margin, length.out = n_init)\n  } else if (is.finite(lb) && !is.finite(ub)) {\n    pts <- lb + c(0.1, 0.5, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 12, 18, 25, 35, 50,\n                  0.2, 0.8, 1.5, 4, 7, 10, 15, 20)[1:n_init]\n  } else if (!is.finite(lb) && is.finite(ub)) {\n    pts <- ub - c(0.1, 0.5, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 12, 18, 25, 35, 50,\n                  0.2, 0.8, 1.5, 4, 7, 10, 15, 20)[1:n_init]\n  } else {\n    # Both infinite: center around 0\n    pts <- c(seq(-4, 4, length.out = n_init))\n  }\n\n  pts <- sort(unique(pts))\n\n  # Keep only points with positive density\n  vals <- f(pts)\n  good <- which(vals > 0 & !is.na(vals) & is.finite(vals))\n  if (length(good) < 3) {\n    stop(\"Cannot find at least 3 initial abscissae with positive density. Check domain bounds.\")\n  }\n  pts <- pts[good]\n\n  # Compute log-density\n  h <- log(vals[good])\n\n  # For infinite left bound: need first secant slope > 0\n  # For infinite right bound: need last secant slope < 0\n  # Trim from each end until this is satisfied\n  if (!is.finite(lb)) {\n    while (length(pts) > 3) {\n      s1 <- (h[2] - h[1]) / (pts[2] - pts[1])\n      if (s1 > 0) break\n      pts <- pts[-1]; h <- h[-1]\n    }\n  }\n  if (!is.finite(ub)) {\n    while (length(pts) > 3) {\n      k <- length(pts)\n      sk <- (h[k] - h[k-1]) / (pts[k] - pts[k-1])\n      if (sk < 0) break\n      pts <- pts[-k]; h <- h[-k]\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (length(pts) < 3) {\n    stop(\"Cannot find suitable initial abscissae. The density mode may be outside the initial search range.\")\n  }\n\n  return(pts)\n}\n\n#' Validate inputs to the ars function\n#' @param f density function\n#' @param n number of samples\n#' @param lb lower bound\n#' @param ub upper bound\nvalidate_inputs <- function(f, n, lb, ub) {\n  if (!is.function(f)) {\n    stop(\"'f' must be a function.\")\n  }\n  if (!is.numeric(n) || length(n) != 1 || n < 1 || n != as.integer(n)) {\n    stop(\"'n' must be a positive integer.\")\n  }\n  if (!is.numeric(lb) || length(lb) != 1) {\n    stop(\"'lb' must be a single numeric value.\")\n  }\n  if (!is.numeric(ub) || length(ub) != 1) {\n    stop(\"'ub' must be a single numeric value.\")\n  }\n  if (lb >= ub) {\n    stop(\"Lower bound must be strictly less than upper bound.\")\n  }\n}\n\n# --- Main ARS Function ---\n\n#' Adaptive Rejection Sampling\n#'\n#' @param f density function (possibly unnormalized), must be log-concave.\n#'          Should accept a vector and return a vector of density values.\n#' @param support optional 2-element vector c(lb, ub) for the domain\n#' @param n number of samples to generate (default 100)\n#' @param lb lower bound of the domain (default -Inf)\n#' @param ub upper bound of the domain (default Inf)\n#' @param ... additional arguments (currently unused)\n#' @return vector of n samples from the target distribution\nars <- function(f, support = NULL, n = 100, lb = -Inf, ub = Inf, ...) {\n  # Handle the support vector argument (alternative call pattern)\n  if (!is.null(support) && is.numeric(support) && length(support) == 2) {\n    lb <- support[1]\n    ub <- support[2]\n  }\n\n  # Validate inputs\n  validate_inputs(f, n, lb, ub)\n\n  # Choose initial abscissae (at least 3)\n  abscissae <- choose_initial_abscissae(lb, ub, f)\n  h_vals <- eval_log_density(f, abscissae)\n\n  # Check initial log-concavity\n  check_log_concavity(abscissae, h_vals)\n\n  # Main sampling loop\n  samples <- numeric(n)\n  count <- 0\n  max_iter <- n * 200  # safety limit\n  iter <- 0\n\n  while (count < n && iter < max_iter) {\n    iter <- iter + 1\n\n    # Build upper hull\n    hull <- compute_upper_hull(abscissae, h_vals, lb, ub)\n\n    # Sample a candidate from the upper hull\n    x_star <- sample_upper_hull(1, hull)\n\n    # Squeeze and rejection test\n    u <- runif(1)\n    u_val <- eval_upper_hull(x_star, hull)\n    l_val <- eval_lower_hull(x_star, abscissae, h_vals)\n\n    if (u <= exp(l_val - u_val)) {\n      # Accept by squeeze test (no density evaluation needed)\n      count <- count + 1\n      samples[count] <- x_star\n    } else {\n      # Need to evaluate the true log-density\n      h_star <- eval_log_density(f, x_star)\n\n      if (u <= exp(h_star - u_val)) {\n        # Accept by rejection test\n        count <- count + 1\n        samples[count] <- x_star\n      }\n\n      # Update abscissae with new point (whether accepted or not)\n      insert_pos <- findInterval(x_star, abscissae) + 1\n      abscissae <- append(abscissae, x_star, after = insert_pos - 1)\n      h_vals <- append(h_vals, h_star, after = insert_pos - 1)\n\n      # Re-check log-concavity with new point\n      check_log_concavity(abscissae, h_vals)\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (count < n) {\n    warning(paste(\"Only generated\", count, \"out of\", n, \"requested samples.\"))\n  }\n\n  return(samples)\n}\n\n# --- Test Function ---\n\n#' Run formal tests of the ARS implementation\n#' Compares samples against known distributions using statistical tests.\n#' Output format: \"TEST_NAME: PASS\" or \"TEST_NAME: FAIL\"\ntest <- function() {\n  cat(\"============================================================\\n\")\n  cat(\"     Adaptive Rejection Sampling (ARS) - Test Suite\\n\")\n  cat(\"============================================================\\n\\n\")\n\n  pass_count <- 0\n  fail_count <- 0\n\n  run_test <- function(name, expr) {\n    result <- tryCatch(expr, error = function(e) {\n      cat(paste0(\"  Error: \", e$message, \"\\n\"))\n      return(FALSE)\n    })\n    if (isTRUE(result)) {\n      cat(paste0(name, \": PASS\\n\\n\"))\n      pass_count <<- pass_count + 1\n    } else {\n      cat(paste0(name, \": FAIL\\n\\n\"))\n      fail_count <<- fail_count + 1\n    }\n  }\n\n  # ---- Input Validation Tests ----\n  cat(\"--- Input Validation Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_negative_n\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that negative n is rejected...\\n\")\n    tryCatch({ ars(dnorm, n = -5); FALSE },\n             error = function(e) grepl(\"positive integer\", e$message))\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_zero_n\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that n=0 is rejected...\\n\")\n    tryCatch({ ars(dnorm, n = 0); FALSE },\n             error = function(e) grepl(\"positive integer\", e$message))\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_non_integer_n\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that non-integer n is rejected...\\n\")\n    tryCatch({ ars(dnorm, n = 3.5); FALSE },\n             error = function(e) grepl(\"positive integer\", e$message))\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_invalid_bounds\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that lb >= ub is rejected...\\n\")\n    tryCatch({ ars(dnorm, n = 10, lb = 5, ub = 2); FALSE },\n             error = function(e) grepl(\"strictly less\", e$message))\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_non_function_f\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that non-function f is rejected...\\n\")\n    tryCatch({ ars(\"not_a_function\", n = 10); FALSE },\n             error = function(e) grepl(\"must be a function\", e$message))\n  })\n\n  # ---- Log-Concavity Tests ----\n  cat(\"--- Log-Concavity Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_bimodal_rejection\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing rejection of bimodal (non-log-concave) density...\\n\")\n    bimodal <- function(x) 0.5 * dnorm(x, -3, 0.5) + 0.5 * dnorm(x, 3, 0.5)\n    tryCatch({ ars(bimodal, n = 1000, lb = -10, ub = 10); FALSE },\n             error = function(e) grepl(\"[Ll]og-concav\", e$message))\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_cauchy_rejection\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing rejection of Cauchy (non-log-concave) density...\\n\")\n    tryCatch({ ars(dcauchy, n = 1000, lb = -100, ub = 100); FALSE },\n             error = function(e) grepl(\"[Ll]og-concav\", e$message))\n  })\n\n  # ---- Module-Level Tests ----\n  cat(\"--- Module-Level Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_eval_log_density\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing eval_log_density with dnorm...\\n\")\n    result <- eval_log_density(dnorm, 0)\n    expected <- log(dnorm(0))\n    cat(paste0(\"  log(dnorm(0)) = \", round(result, 6),\n               \" (expected \", round(expected, 6), \")\\n\"))\n    abs(result - expected) < 1e-10\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_check_log_concavity_valid\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing log-concavity check on concave data...\\n\")\n    x <- c(-2, -1, 0, 1, 2)\n    h <- -x^2\n    tryCatch({ check_log_concavity(x, h); TRUE }, error = function(e) FALSE)\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_check_log_concavity_invalid\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing log-concavity check on convex data...\\n\")\n    x <- c(-2, -1, 0, 1, 2)\n    h <- x^2  # convex, not concave\n    tryCatch({ check_log_concavity(x, h); FALSE },\n             error = function(e) grepl(\"[Ll]og-concav\", e$message))\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_upper_hull_above_density\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that upper hull >= log-density at test points...\\n\")\n    x_abs <- c(-3, -1, 0, 1, 3)\n    h_abs <- eval_log_density(dnorm, x_abs)\n    hull <- compute_upper_hull(x_abs, h_abs, -Inf, Inf)\n    test_pts <- seq(-2.5, 2.5, by = 0.1)\n    h_test <- eval_log_density(dnorm, test_pts)\n    u_test <- eval_upper_hull(test_pts, hull)\n    all_above <- all(u_test >= h_test - 1e-6)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Upper hull >= log-density at all \", length(test_pts),\n               \" test points: \", all_above, \"\\n\"))\n    all_above\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_lower_hull_below_density\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that lower hull <= log-density at test points...\\n\")\n    x_abs <- c(-3, -1, 0, 1, 3)\n    h_abs <- eval_log_density(dnorm, x_abs)\n    test_pts <- seq(-2.5, 2.5, by = 0.1)\n    h_test <- eval_log_density(dnorm, test_pts)\n    l_test <- sapply(test_pts, function(x) eval_lower_hull(x, x_abs, h_abs))\n    all_below <- all(l_test <= h_test + 1e-6)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Lower hull <= log-density at all \", length(test_pts),\n               \" test points: \", all_below, \"\\n\"))\n    all_below\n  })\n\n  # ---- Sampling Tests: Standard Normal ----\n  cat(\"--- Standard Normal Distribution Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_normal_unbounded\", {\n    cat(\"  Sampling 5000 points from N(0,1) on (-Inf, Inf)...\\n\")\n    set.seed(42)\n    samples <- ars(n = 5000, f = dnorm, lb = -Inf, ub = Inf)\n    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(m, 4), \" (expected ~0)\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(s, 4), \" (expected ~1)\\n\"))\n    writeLines(as.character(samples), \"/app/normal_samples.txt\")\n    cat(\"  Saved samples to /app/normal_samples.txt\\n\")\n    ks <- ks.test(samples, \"pnorm\")\n    cat(paste0(\"  KS test p-value: \", round(ks$p.value, 4), \"\\n\"))\n    abs(m) < 0.15 && abs(s - 1) < 0.15 && ks$p.value > 0.01\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_normal_bounded\", {\n    cat(\"  Sampling 2000 points from N(0,1) on [-5, 5]...\\n\")\n    set.seed(123)\n    samples <- ars(dnorm, c(-5, 5), n = 2000)\n    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(m, 4), \"\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(s, 4), \"\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Range: [\", round(min(samples), 4), \", \", round(max(samples), 4), \"]\\n\"))\n    all(samples >= -5 & samples <= 5) && abs(m) < 0.2\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_normal_shifted\", {\n    cat(\"  Sampling 3000 points from N(5, 2^2) on (-Inf, Inf)...\\n\")\n    set.seed(200)\n    shifted_norm <- function(x) dnorm(x, mean = 5, sd = 2)\n    samples <- ars(shifted_norm, n = 3000, lb = -Inf, ub = Inf)\n    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(m, 4), \" (expected ~5)\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(s, 4), \" (expected ~2)\\n\"))\n    ks <- ks.test(samples, \"pnorm\", mean = 5, sd = 2)\n    cat(paste0(\"  KS test p-value: \", round(ks$p.value, 4), \"\\n\"))\n    abs(m - 5) < 0.3 && abs(s - 2) < 0.3 && ks$p.value > 0.01\n  })\n\n  # ---- Sampling Tests: Exponential ----\n  cat(\"--- Exponential Distribution Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_exponential_sampling\", {\n    cat(\"  Sampling 5000 points from Exp(1) on [0.001, 100]...\\n\")\n    set.seed(99)\n    samples <- ars(dexp, c(0.001, 100), n = 5000)\n    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(m, 4), \" (expected ~1)\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(s, 4), \" (expected ~1)\\n\"))\n    writeLines(as.character(samples), \"/app/exponential_samples.txt\")\n    cat(\"  Saved samples to /app/exponential_samples.txt\\n\")\n    ks <- ks.test(samples, \"pexp\")\n    cat(paste0(\"  KS test p-value: \", round(ks$p.value, 4), \"\\n\"))\n    abs(m - 1) < 0.2 && ks$p.value > 0.01\n  })\n\n  # ---- Sampling Tests: Gamma ----\n  cat(\"--- Gamma Distribution Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_gamma_sampling\", {\n    cat(\"  Sampling 3000 points from Gamma(3, 1) on [0.01, 30]...\\n\")\n    set.seed(77)\n    gamma_dens <- function(x) dgamma(x, shape = 3, rate = 1)\n    samples <- ars(gamma_dens, c(0.01, 30), n = 3000)\n    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(m, 4), \" (expected ~3)\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(s, 4), \" (expected ~1.73)\\n\"))\n    ks <- ks.test(samples, \"pgamma\", shape = 3, rate = 1)\n    cat(paste0(\"  KS test p-value: \", round(ks$p.value, 4), \"\\n\"))\n    abs(m - 3) < 0.4 && ks$p.value > 0.01\n  })\n\n  # ---- Sampling Tests: Beta ----\n  cat(\"--- Beta Distribution Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_beta_sampling\", {\n    cat(\"  Sampling 3000 points from Beta(2, 5) on [0.01, 0.99]...\\n\")\n    set.seed(55)\n    beta_dens <- function(x) dbeta(x, shape1 = 2, shape2 = 5)\n    samples <- ars(beta_dens, c(0.01, 0.99), n = 3000)\n    expected_mean <- 2 / (2 + 5)\n    expected_sd <- sqrt(2 * 5 / ((2 + 5)^2 * (2 + 5 + 1)))\n    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(m, 4),\n               \" (expected ~\", round(expected_mean, 4), \")\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(s, 4),\n               \" (expected ~\", round(expected_sd, 4), \")\\n\"))\n    ks <- ks.test(samples, \"pbeta\", shape1 = 2, shape2 = 5)\n    cat(paste0(\"  KS test p-value: \", round(ks$p.value, 4), \"\\n\"))\n    abs(m - expected_mean) < 0.05 && ks$p.value > 0.01\n  })\n\n  # ---- Sampling Tests: Unnormalized density ----\n  cat(\"--- Unnormalized Density Test ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_unnormalized_density\", {\n    cat(\"  Sampling from unnormalized Gaussian: f(x) = exp(-x^2/2)...\\n\")\n    set.seed(33)\n    unnorm_gauss <- function(x) exp(-x^2 / 2)\n    samples <- ars(unnorm_gauss, n = 3000, lb = -Inf, ub = Inf)\n    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(m, 4), \" (expected ~0)\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(s, 4), \" (expected ~1)\\n\"))\n    ks <- ks.test(samples, \"pnorm\")\n    cat(paste0(\"  KS test p-value: \", round(ks$p.value, 4), \"\\n\"))\n    abs(m) < 0.15 && abs(s - 1) < 0.15 && ks$p.value > 0.01\n  })\n\n  # ---- Summary ----\n  cat(\"============================================================\\n\")\n  total <- pass_count + fail_count\n  cat(paste0(\"  TOTAL: \", total, \" tests, \",\n             pass_count, \" passed, \", fail_count, \" failed\\n\"))\n  cat(\"============================================================\\n\")\n\n  invisible(list(passed = pass_count, failed = fail_count))\n}\n\n# Run tests if this script is executed directly\nif (sys.nframe() == 0) {\n  test()\n}\n","numLines":690,"startLine":1,"totalLines":690}}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01DQrTF5PXjr6uq8dfsPxQek","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01CqiMbmw1kUEYLWv7vsaMKY","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/app/ars.R","content":"###############################################################################\n# Adaptive Rejection Sampling (ARS)\n# Based on Gilks, W.R. & Wild, P. (1992)\n# \"Adaptive Rejection Sampling for Gibbs Sampling\"\n# Journal of the Royal Statistical Society: Series C, 41(2), 337-348.\n#\n# Derivative-free implementation using secant-based upper hull construction.\n###############################################################################\n\n# --- Auxiliary / Module Functions ---\n\n#' Evaluate the log of a density function safely\n#' @param f density function (possibly unnormalized)\n#' @param x point(s) at which to evaluate\n#' @return log(f(x)), with protection against f(x) <= 0\neval_log_density <- function(f, x) {\n  fx <- f(x)\n  if (any(is.na(fx) | is.nan(fx))) {\n    stop(\"Density function returned NA/NaN.\")\n  }\n  if (any(fx < 0)) {\n    stop(\"Density function returned negative value.\")\n  }\n  result <- rep(-Inf, length(fx))\n  pos <- fx > 0\n  result[pos] <- log(fx[pos])\n  return(result)\n}\n\n#' Check log-concavity at three consecutive abscissae\n#' @param abscissae sorted vector of x values\n#' @param h_vals corresponding log-density values h(x)\n#' @return TRUE if log-concave, else stops with error\ncheck_log_concavity <- function(abscissae, h_vals) {\n  n <- length(abscissae)\n  if (n < 3) return(TRUE)\n  for (i in 1:(n - 2)) {\n    x1 <- abscissae[i];   h1 <- h_vals[i]\n    x2 <- abscissae[i+1]; h2 <- h_vals[i+1]\n    x3 <- abscissae[i+2]; h3 <- h_vals[i+2]\n    slope_left  <- (h2 - h1) / (x2 - x1)\n    slope_right <- (h3 - h2) / (x3 - x2)\n    if (slope_right > slope_left + 1e-10) {\n      stop(paste0(\n        \"Log-concavity violated at x = \", x2,\n        \". Left slope = \", round(slope_left, 6),\n        \", Right slope = \", round(slope_right, 6),\n        \". The density may not be log-concave.\"\n      ))\n    }\n  }\n  return(TRUE)\n}\n\n#' Compute the upper hull for the ARS algorithm (derivative-free version)\n#'\n#' Constructs a piecewise-linear upper envelope of the log-density using\n#' the secant slopes between consecutive abscissae.\n#'\n#' At each interior abscissa x_j (j = 2,...,k-1), the upper hull forms a\n#' \"tent\" with left arm slope s_j and right arm slope s_{j-1}, where\n#' s_j = (h_{j+1} - h_j) / (x_{j+1} - x_j).\n#'\n#' @param abscissae sorted x values (at least 3)\n#' @param h_vals corresponding h(x) = log(f(x)) values\n#' @param lb lower bound of domain\n#' @param ub upper bound of domain\n#' @return list with z (breakpoints), seg_slopes, seg_intercepts, n_seg\ncompute_upper_hull <- function(abscissae, h_vals, lb, ub) {\n  k <- length(abscissae)\n  if (k < 3) stop(\"Need at least 3 abscissae for upper hull construction.\")\n\n  # Secant slopes\n  s <- diff(h_vals) / diff(abscissae)\n\n  # Build segments: each defined by intercept and slope in form u(x) = b + m*x\n  # Segments from left to right:\n  #   1. x_1: slope s[1] (only arm, extends left)\n  #   For j = 2,...,k-1: left arm slope s[j], right arm slope s[j-1]\n  #   Last: x_k: slope s[k-1] (only arm, extends right)\n\n  seg_px <- numeric(0)  # reference x\n  seg_ph <- numeric(0)  # reference h\n  seg_m  <- numeric(0)  # slope\n\n  # First segment\n  seg_px <- c(seg_px, abscissae[1])\n  seg_ph <- c(seg_ph, h_vals[1])\n  seg_m  <- c(seg_m, s[1])\n\n  # Interior points\n  for (j in 2:(k-1)) {\n    # Left arm\n    seg_px <- c(seg_px, abscissae[j])\n    seg_ph <- c(seg_ph, h_vals[j])\n    seg_m  <- c(seg_m, s[j])\n    # Right arm\n    seg_px <- c(seg_px, abscissae[j])\n    seg_ph <- c(seg_ph, h_vals[j])\n    seg_m  <- c(seg_m, s[j-1])\n  }\n\n  # Last segment\n  seg_px <- c(seg_px, abscissae[k])\n  seg_ph <- c(seg_ph, h_vals[k])\n  seg_m  <- c(seg_m, s[k-1])\n\n  # Convert to intercept form: u(x) = seg_int + seg_m * x\n  seg_int <- seg_ph - seg_m * seg_px\n\n  # Merge consecutive segments that are effectively the same line\n  # (happens when log-density is linear, e.g. exponential distribution)\n  keep <- rep(TRUE, length(seg_m))\n  for (i in 2:length(seg_m)) {\n    if (abs(seg_m[i] - seg_m[i-1]) < 1e-12 && abs(seg_int[i] - seg_int[i-1]) < 1e-6) {\n      keep[i] <- FALSE\n    }\n  }\n  seg_px <- seg_px[keep]\n  seg_ph <- seg_ph[keep]\n  seg_m  <- seg_m[keep]\n  seg_int <- seg_int[keep]\n  n_seg <- length(seg_m)\n\n  # Compute breakpoints between consecutive segments\n  if (n_seg == 1) {\n    z <- c(lb, ub)\n  } else {\n    bp <- numeric(n_seg - 1)\n    for (i in 1:(n_seg - 1)) {\n      if (seg_px[i] == seg_px[i+1]) {\n        # Same reference point (left/right arms of a tent)\n        bp[i] <- seg_px[i]\n      } else if (abs(seg_m[i] - seg_m[i+1]) < 1e-12) {\n        # Parallel lines: use midpoint of reference points\n        bp[i] <- (seg_px[i] + seg_px[i+1]) / 2\n      } else {\n        bp[i] <- (seg_int[i+1] - seg_int[i]) / (seg_m[i] - seg_m[i+1])\n      }\n    }\n    # Ensure breakpoints are valid\n    bp[is.na(bp) | is.nan(bp)] <- 0\n    z <- c(lb, bp, ub)\n  }\n\n  return(list(\n    z = z,\n    seg_slopes = seg_m,\n    seg_intercepts = seg_int,\n    n_seg = n_seg\n  ))\n}\n\n#' Evaluate the upper hull at a point x\n#' @param x scalar or vector of points\n#' @param hull list from compute_upper_hull\n#' @return u(x), the upper hull value(s)\neval_upper_hull <- function(x, hull) {\n  z <- hull$z\n  m <- hull$seg_slopes\n  b <- hull$seg_intercepts\n  n_seg <- hull$n_seg\n\n  seg_idx <- findInterval(x, z, rightmost.closed = TRUE)\n  seg_idx <- pmax(1, pmin(seg_idx, n_seg))\n\n  return(b[seg_idx] + m[seg_idx] * x)\n}\n\n#' Evaluate the lower hull (piecewise linear interpolation) at a point x\n#' @param x scalar point\n#' @param abscissae sorted x values\n#' @param h_vals corresponding h(x) values\n#' @return l(x). Returns -Inf if outside the range of abscissae.\neval_lower_hull <- function(x, abscissae, h_vals) {\n  k <- length(abscissae)\n  if (x <= abscissae[1] || x >= abscissae[k]) {\n    return(-Inf)\n  }\n  j <- findInterval(x, abscissae)\n  j <- pmax(1, pmin(j, k - 1))\n  t <- (x - abscissae[j]) / (abscissae[j+1] - abscissae[j])\n  return(h_vals[j] * (1 - t) + h_vals[j+1] * t)\n}\n\n#' Sample from the upper hull (piecewise-exponential distribution)\n#' @param n number of samples to draw\n#' @param hull list from compute_upper_hull\n#' @return vector of n samples\nsample_upper_hull <- function(n, hull) {\n  z <- hull$z\n  m <- hull$seg_slopes\n  b <- hull$seg_intercepts\n  n_seg <- hull$n_seg\n\n  # Compute area under each segment: integral of exp(b + m*x) from z[j] to z[j+1]\n  areas <- numeric(n_seg)\n  for (j in 1:n_seg) {\n    lo <- z[j]\n    hi <- z[j + 1]\n\n    if (!is.finite(lo)) {\n      if (m[j] <= 0) { areas[j] <- 0; next }\n      areas[j] <- exp(b[j] + m[j] * hi) / m[j]\n      next\n    }\n    if (!is.finite(hi)) {\n      if (m[j] >= 0) { areas[j] <- 0; next }\n      areas[j] <- -exp(b[j] + m[j] * lo) / m[j]\n      next\n    }\n\n    if (abs(m[j]) < 1e-15) {\n      areas[j] <- exp(b[j]) * (hi - lo)\n    } else {\n      log_hi <- b[j] + m[j] * hi\n      log_lo <- b[j] + m[j] * lo\n      log_max <- max(log_hi, log_lo)\n      areas[j] <- exp(log_max) * (exp(log_hi - log_max) - exp(log_lo - log_max)) / m[j]\n    }\n\n    if (is.na(areas[j]) || !is.finite(areas[j]) || areas[j] < 0) {\n      areas[j] <- 0\n    }\n  }\n\n  total_area <- sum(areas)\n  if (total_area <= 0 || !is.finite(total_area)) {\n    stop(\"Upper hull has zero or infinite area. Check domain bounds and initial abscissae.\")\n  }\n  probs <- areas / total_area\n\n  # Sample segments according to their areas\n  seg_choices <- sample.int(n_seg, size = n, replace = TRUE, prob = probs)\n\n  # For each chosen segment, sample from truncated exponential via inverse CDF\n  samples <- numeric(n)\n  for (i in 1:n) {\n    j <- seg_choices[i]\n    lo <- z[j]\n    hi <- z[j + 1]\n    u <- runif(1)\n\n    if (abs(m[j]) < 1e-15) {\n      if (!is.finite(lo)) lo <- hi - 20\n      if (!is.finite(hi)) hi <- lo + 20\n      samples[i] <- lo + u * (hi - lo)\n    } else if (!is.finite(lo)) {\n      # lo = -Inf, m > 0: inverse CDF gives x = hi + log(u) / m\n      samples[i] <- hi + log(u) / m[j]\n    } else if (!is.finite(hi)) {\n      # hi = +Inf, m < 0: inverse CDF gives x = lo + log(u) / m (m < 0)\n      samples[i] <- lo + log(u) / m[j]\n    } else {\n      log_lo <- m[j] * lo\n      log_hi <- m[j] * hi\n      log_max <- max(log_lo, log_hi)\n      exp_lo <- exp(log_lo - log_max)\n      exp_hi <- exp(log_hi - log_max)\n      val <- exp_lo + u * (exp_hi - exp_lo)\n      if (val <= 0) val <- 1e-300\n      samples[i] <- (log_max + log(val)) / m[j]\n    }\n  }\n  return(samples)\n}\n\n#' Choose initial abscissae for the ARS algorithm\n#' @param lb lower bound\n#' @param ub upper bound\n#' @param f density function\n#' @return vector of initial abscissae (at least 3, with required slope conditions)\nchoose_initial_abscissae <- function(lb, ub, f) {\n  # Generate candidate points\n  if (is.finite(lb) && is.finite(ub)) {\n    margin <- (ub - lb) * 0.02\n    pts <- seq(lb + margin, ub - margin, length.out = 20)\n  } else if (is.finite(lb) && !is.finite(ub)) {\n    pts <- lb + c(0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.2, 0.5, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 12, 18, 25, 40, 60)\n  } else if (!is.finite(lb) && is.finite(ub)) {\n    pts <- ub - c(0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.2, 0.5, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 12, 18, 25, 40, 60)\n    pts <- sort(pts)\n  } else {\n    pts <- seq(-5, 5, length.out = 21)\n  }\n\n  pts <- sort(unique(pts))\n\n  # Keep only points with positive density\n  vals <- f(pts)\n  good <- which(vals > 0 & !is.na(vals) & is.finite(vals))\n  if (length(good) < 3) {\n    stop(\"Cannot find at least 3 initial abscissae with positive density. Check domain bounds.\")\n  }\n  pts <- pts[good]\n  h <- log(vals[good])\n\n  # For infinite left bound: trim until first secant slope > 0\n  if (!is.finite(lb)) {\n    while (length(pts) > 3) {\n      s1 <- (h[2] - h[1]) / (pts[2] - pts[1])\n      if (s1 > 1e-12) break\n      pts <- pts[-1]; h <- h[-1]\n    }\n    if ((h[2] - h[1]) / (pts[2] - pts[1]) <= 0) {\n      stop(\"Cannot find initial points where log-density is increasing from the left. Check domain.\")\n    }\n  }\n\n  # For infinite right bound: trim until last secant slope < 0\n  if (!is.finite(ub)) {\n    while (length(pts) > 3) {\n      k <- length(pts)\n      sk <- (h[k] - h[k-1]) / (pts[k] - pts[k-1])\n      if (sk < -1e-12) break\n      pts <- pts[-k]; h <- h[-k]\n    }\n    k <- length(pts)\n    if ((h[k] - h[k-1]) / (pts[k] - pts[k-1]) >= 0) {\n      stop(\"Cannot find initial points where log-density is decreasing on the right. Check domain.\")\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (length(pts) < 3) {\n    stop(\"Cannot find suitable initial abscissae.\")\n  }\n\n  # Subsample to avoid too many initial points (keep ~5-8)\n  if (length(pts) > 8) {\n    idx <- round(seq(1, length(pts), length.out = 8))\n    # But always keep first and last\n    idx <- sort(unique(c(1, idx, length(pts))))\n    pts <- pts[idx]\n  }\n\n  return(pts)\n}\n\n#' Validate inputs to the ars function\nvalidate_inputs <- function(f, n, lb, ub) {\n  if (!is.function(f)) {\n    stop(\"'f' must be a function.\")\n  }\n  if (!is.numeric(n) || length(n) != 1 || n < 1 || n != as.integer(n)) {\n    stop(\"'n' must be a positive integer.\")\n  }\n  if (!is.numeric(lb) || length(lb) != 1) {\n    stop(\"'lb' must be a single numeric value.\")\n  }\n  if (!is.numeric(ub) || length(ub) != 1) {\n    stop(\"'ub' must be a single numeric value.\")\n  }\n  if (lb >= ub) {\n    stop(\"Lower bound must be strictly less than upper bound.\")\n  }\n}\n\n# --- Main ARS Function ---\n\n#' Adaptive Rejection Sampling\n#'\n#' @param f density function (possibly unnormalized), must be log-concave.\n#' @param support optional 2-element vector c(lb, ub) for the domain\n#' @param n number of samples to generate (default 100)\n#' @param lb lower bound of the domain (default -Inf)\n#' @param ub upper bound of the domain (default Inf)\n#' @param ... additional arguments (currently unused)\n#' @return vector of n samples from the target distribution\nars <- function(f, support = NULL, n = 100, lb = -Inf, ub = Inf, ...) {\n  # Handle the support vector argument\n  if (!is.null(support) && is.numeric(support) && length(support) == 2) {\n    lb <- support[1]\n    ub <- support[2]\n  }\n\n  validate_inputs(f, n, lb, ub)\n\n  abscissae <- choose_initial_abscissae(lb, ub, f)\n  h_vals <- eval_log_density(f, abscissae)\n  check_log_concavity(abscissae, h_vals)\n\n  samples <- numeric(n)\n  count <- 0\n  max_iter <- n * 200\n  iter <- 0\n\n  while (count < n && iter < max_iter) {\n    iter <- iter + 1\n\n    hull <- compute_upper_hull(abscissae, h_vals, lb, ub)\n    x_star <- sample_upper_hull(1, hull)\n\n    u <- runif(1)\n    u_val <- eval_upper_hull(x_star, hull)\n    l_val <- eval_lower_hull(x_star, abscissae, h_vals)\n\n    if (u <= exp(l_val - u_val)) {\n      count <- count + 1\n      samples[count] <- x_star\n    } else {\n      h_star <- eval_log_density(f, x_star)\n\n      if (u <= exp(h_star - u_val)) {\n        count <- count + 1\n        samples[count] <- x_star\n      }\n\n      # Add new point to abscissae\n      insert_pos <- findInterval(x_star, abscissae) + 1\n      abscissae <- append(abscissae, x_star, after = insert_pos - 1)\n      h_vals <- append(h_vals, h_star, after = insert_pos - 1)\n\n      check_log_concavity(abscissae, h_vals)\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (count < n) {\n    warning(paste(\"Only generated\", count, \"out of\", n, \"requested samples.\"))\n  }\n\n  return(samples)\n}\n\n# --- Test Function ---\n\n#' Run formal tests of the ARS implementation\ntest <- function() {\n  cat(\"============================================================\\n\")\n  cat(\"     Adaptive Rejection Sampling (ARS) - Test Suite\\n\")\n  cat(\"============================================================\\n\\n\")\n\n  pass_count <- 0\n  fail_count <- 0\n\n  run_test <- function(name, expr) {\n    result <- tryCatch(expr, error = function(e) {\n      cat(paste0(\"  Error: \", e$message, \"\\n\"))\n      return(FALSE)\n    })\n    if (isTRUE(result)) {\n      cat(paste0(name, \": PASS\\n\\n\"))\n      pass_count <<- pass_count + 1\n    } else {\n      cat(paste0(name, \": FAIL\\n\\n\"))\n      fail_count <<- fail_count + 1\n    }\n  }\n\n  # ---- Input Validation Tests ----\n  cat(\"--- Input Validation Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_negative_n\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that negative n is rejected...\\n\")\n    tryCatch({ ars(dnorm, n = -5); FALSE },\n             error = function(e) grepl(\"positive integer\", e$message))\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_zero_n\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that n=0 is rejected...\\n\")\n    tryCatch({ ars(dnorm, n = 0); FALSE },\n             error = function(e) grepl(\"positive integer\", e$message))\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_non_integer_n\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that non-integer n is rejected...\\n\")\n    tryCatch({ ars(dnorm, n = 3.5); FALSE },\n             error = function(e) grepl(\"positive integer\", e$message))\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_invalid_bounds\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that lb >= ub is rejected...\\n\")\n    tryCatch({ ars(dnorm, n = 10, lb = 5, ub = 2); FALSE },\n             error = function(e) grepl(\"strictly less\", e$message))\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_non_function_f\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that non-function f is rejected...\\n\")\n    tryCatch({ ars(\"not_a_function\", n = 10); FALSE },\n             error = function(e) grepl(\"must be a function\", e$message))\n  })\n\n  # ---- Log-Concavity Tests ----\n  cat(\"--- Log-Concavity Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_bimodal_rejection\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing rejection of bimodal (non-log-concave) density...\\n\")\n    bimodal <- function(x) 0.5 * dnorm(x, -3, 0.5) + 0.5 * dnorm(x, 3, 0.5)\n    tryCatch({ ars(bimodal, n = 1000, lb = -10, ub = 10); FALSE },\n             error = function(e) grepl(\"[Ll]og-concav\", e$message))\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_cauchy_rejection\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing rejection of Cauchy (non-log-concave) density...\\n\")\n    tryCatch({ ars(dcauchy, n = 1000, lb = -100, ub = 100); FALSE },\n             error = function(e) grepl(\"[Ll]og-concav\", e$message))\n  })\n\n  # ---- Module-Level Tests ----\n  cat(\"--- Module-Level Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_eval_log_density\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing eval_log_density with dnorm...\\n\")\n    result <- eval_log_density(dnorm, 0)\n    expected <- log(dnorm(0))\n    cat(paste0(\"  log(dnorm(0)) = \", round(result, 6),\n               \" (expected \", round(expected, 6), \")\\n\"))\n    abs(result - expected) < 1e-10\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_check_log_concavity_valid\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing log-concavity check on concave data...\\n\")\n    x <- c(-2, -1, 0, 1, 2)\n    h <- -x^2\n    tryCatch({ check_log_concavity(x, h); TRUE }, error = function(e) FALSE)\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_check_log_concavity_invalid\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing log-concavity check on convex data...\\n\")\n    x <- c(-2, -1, 0, 1, 2)\n    h <- x^2\n    tryCatch({ check_log_concavity(x, h); FALSE },\n             error = function(e) grepl(\"[Ll]og-concav\", e$message))\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_upper_hull_above_density\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that upper hull >= log-density at test points...\\n\")\n    x_abs <- c(-3, -1, 0, 1, 3)\n    h_abs <- eval_log_density(dnorm, x_abs)\n    hull <- compute_upper_hull(x_abs, h_abs, -Inf, Inf)\n    test_pts <- seq(-2.5, 2.5, by = 0.1)\n    h_test <- eval_log_density(dnorm, test_pts)\n    u_test <- eval_upper_hull(test_pts, hull)\n    all_above <- all(u_test >= h_test - 1e-6)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Upper hull >= log-density at all \", length(test_pts),\n               \" test points: \", all_above, \"\\n\"))\n    all_above\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_lower_hull_below_density\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that lower hull <= log-density at test points...\\n\")\n    x_abs <- c(-3, -1, 0, 1, 3)\n    h_abs <- eval_log_density(dnorm, x_abs)\n    test_pts <- seq(-2.5, 2.5, by = 0.1)\n    h_test <- eval_log_density(dnorm, test_pts)\n    l_test <- sapply(test_pts, function(x) eval_lower_hull(x, x_abs, h_abs))\n    all_below <- all(l_test <= h_test + 1e-6)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Lower hull <= log-density at all \", length(test_pts),\n               \" test points: \", all_below, \"\\n\"))\n    all_below\n  })\n\n  # ---- Sampling Tests: Standard Normal ----\n  cat(\"--- Standard Normal Distribution Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_normal_unbounded\", {\n    cat(\"  Sampling 5000 points from N(0,1) on (-Inf, Inf)...\\n\")\n    set.seed(42)\n    samples <- ars(n = 5000, f = dnorm, lb = -Inf, ub = Inf)\n    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(m, 4), \" (expected ~0)\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(s, 4), \" (expected ~1)\\n\"))\n    writeLines(as.character(samples), \"/app/normal_samples.txt\")\n    cat(\"  Saved samples to /app/normal_samples.txt\\n\")\n    ks <- ks.test(samples, \"pnorm\")\n    cat(paste0(\"  KS test p-value: \", round(ks$p.value, 4), \"\\n\"))\n    abs(m) < 0.15 && abs(s - 1) < 0.15 && ks$p.value > 0.01\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_normal_bounded\", {\n    cat(\"  Sampling 2000 points from N(0,1) on [-5, 5]...\\n\")\n    set.seed(123)\n    samples <- ars(dnorm, c(-5, 5), n = 2000)\n    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(m, 4), \"\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(s, 4), \"\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Range: [\", round(min(samples), 4), \", \", round(max(samples), 4), \"]\\n\"))\n    all(samples >= -5 & samples <= 5) && abs(m) < 0.2\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_normal_shifted\", {\n    cat(\"  Sampling 3000 points from N(5, 2^2) on (-Inf, Inf)...\\n\")\n    set.seed(200)\n    shifted_norm <- function(x) dnorm(x, mean = 5, sd = 2)\n    samples <- ars(shifted_norm, n = 3000, lb = -Inf, ub = Inf)\n    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(m, 4), \" (expected ~5)\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(s, 4), \" (expected ~2)\\n\"))\n    ks <- ks.test(samples, \"pnorm\", mean = 5, sd = 2)\n    cat(paste0(\"  KS test p-value: \", round(ks$p.value, 4), \"\\n\"))\n    abs(m - 5) < 0.3 && abs(s - 2) < 0.3 && ks$p.value > 0.01\n  })\n\n  # ---- Sampling Tests: Exponential ----\n  cat(\"--- Exponential Distribution Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_exponential_sampling\", {\n    cat(\"  Sampling 5000 points from Exp(1) on [0.001, 50]...\\n\")\n    set.seed(99)\n    samples <- ars(dexp, c(0.001, 50), n = 5000)\n    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(m, 4), \" (expected ~1)\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(s, 4), \" (expected ~1)\\n\"))\n    writeLines(as.character(samples), \"/app/exponential_samples.txt\")\n    cat(\"  Saved samples to /app/exponential_samples.txt\\n\")\n    ks <- ks.test(samples, \"pexp\")\n    cat(paste0(\"  KS test p-value: \", round(ks$p.value, 4), \"\\n\"))\n    abs(m - 1) < 0.2 && ks$p.value > 0.01\n  })\n\n  # ---- Sampling Tests: Gamma ----\n  cat(\"--- Gamma Distribution Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_gamma_sampling\", {\n    cat(\"  Sampling 3000 points from Gamma(3, 1) on [0.01, 30]...\\n\")\n    set.seed(77)\n    gamma_dens <- function(x) dgamma(x, shape = 3, rate = 1)\n    samples <- ars(gamma_dens, c(0.01, 30), n = 3000)\n    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(m, 4), \" (expected ~3)\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(s, 4), \" (expected ~1.73)\\n\"))\n    ks <- ks.test(samples, \"pgamma\", shape = 3, rate = 1)\n    cat(paste0(\"  KS test p-value: \", round(ks$p.value, 4), \"\\n\"))\n    abs(m - 3) < 0.4 && ks$p.value > 0.01\n  })\n\n  # ---- Sampling Tests: Beta ----\n  cat(\"--- Beta Distribution Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_beta_sampling\", {\n    cat(\"  Sampling 3000 points from Beta(2, 5) on [0.01, 0.99]...\\n\")\n    set.seed(55)\n    beta_dens <- function(x) dbeta(x, shape1 = 2, shape2 = 5)\n    samples <- ars(beta_dens, c(0.01, 0.99), n = 3000)\n    expected_mean <- 2 / (2 + 5)\n    expected_sd <- sqrt(2 * 5 / ((2 + 5)^2 * (2 + 5 + 1)))\n    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(m, 4),\n               \" (expected ~\", round(expected_mean, 4), \")\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(s, 4),\n               \" (expected ~\", round(expected_sd, 4), \")\\n\"))\n    ks <- ks.test(samples, \"pbeta\", shape1 = 2, shape2 = 5)\n    cat(paste0(\"  KS test p-value: \", round(ks$p.value, 4), \"\\n\"))\n    abs(m - expected_mean) < 0.05 && ks$p.value > 0.01\n  })\n\n  # ---- Sampling Tests: Unnormalized density ----\n  cat(\"--- Unnormalized Density Test ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_unnormalized_density\", {\n    cat(\"  Sampling from unnormalized Gaussian: f(x) = exp(-x^2/2)...\\n\")\n    set.seed(33)\n    unnorm_gauss <- function(x) exp(-x^2 / 2)\n    samples <- ars(unnorm_gauss, n = 3000, lb = -Inf, ub = Inf)\n    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(m, 4), \" (expected ~0)\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(s, 4), \" (expected ~1)\\n\"))\n    ks <- ks.test(samples, \"pnorm\")\n    cat(paste0(\"  KS test p-value: \", round(ks$p.value, 4), \"\\n\"))\n    abs(m) < 0.15 && abs(s - 1) < 0.15 && ks$p.value > 0.01\n  })\n\n  # ---- Summary ----\n  cat(\"============================================================\\n\")\n  total <- pass_count + fail_count\n  cat(paste0(\"  TOTAL: \", total, \" tests, \",\n             pass_count, \" passed, \", fail_count, \" failed\\n\"))\n  cat(\"============================================================\\n\")\n\n  invisible(list(passed = pass_count, failed = fail_count))\n}\n\n# Run tests if this script test()\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":14253,"cache_read_input_tokens":67393,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":14253,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":8,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"78903e02-0918-48fe-a339-296475e92493","uuid":"5aa61022-e13a-454f-b80b-bd6d6f371101"}
