(1992)\n# \"Adaptive Rejection Sampling for Gibbs Sampling\"\n# Journal of the Royal Statistical Society: Series C, 41(2), 337-348.\n#\n# Derivative-free implementation using secant-based upper hull construction.\n###############################################################################\n\n# --- Auxiliary / Module Functions ---\n\n#' Evaluate the log of a density function safely\n#' @param f density function (possibly unnormalized)\n#' @param x point(s) at which to evaluate\n#' @return log(f(x)), with protection against f(x) <= 0\neval_log_density <- function(f, x) {\n  fx <- f(x)\n  if (any(is.na(fx) | is.nan(fx))) {\n    stop(\"Density function returned NA/NaN.\")\n  }\n  if (any(fx < 0)) {\n    stop(\"Density function returned negative value.\")\n  }\n  result <- rep(-Inf, length(fx))\n  pos <- fx > 0\n  result[pos] <- log(fx[pos])\n  return(result)\n}\n\n#' Check log-concavity at three consecutive abscissae\n#' @param abscissae sorted vector of x values\n#' @param h_vals corresponding log-density values h(x)\n#' @return TRUE if log-concave, else stops with error\ncheck_log_concavity <- function(abscissae, h_vals) {\n  n <- length(abscissae)\n  if (n < 3) return(TRUE)\n  for (i in 1:(n - 2)) {\n    x1 <- abscissae[i];   h1 <- h_vals[i]\n    x2 <- abscissae[i+1]; h2 <- h_vals[i+1]\n    x3 <- abscissae[i+2]; h3 <- h_vals[i+2]\n    slope_left  <- (h2 - h1) / (x2 - x1)\n    slope_right <- (h3 - h2) / (x3 - x2)\n    if (slope_right > slope_left + 1e-10) {\n      stop(paste0(\n        \"Log-concavity violated at x = \", x2,\n        \". Left slope = \", round(slope_left, 6),\n        \", Right slope = \", round(slope_right, 6),\n        \". The density may not be log-concave.\"\n      ))\n    }\n  }\n  return(TRUE)\n}\n\n#' Compute the upper hull for the ARS algorithm (derivative-free version)\n#'\n#' Constructs a piecewise-linear upper envelope of the log-density using\n#' the secant slopes between consecutive abscissae.\n#'\n#' At each interior abscissa x_j (j = 2,...,k-1), the upper hull forms a\n#' \"tent\" with left arm slope s_j and right arm slope s_{j-1}, where\n#' s_j = (h_{j+1} - h_j) / (x_{j+1} - x_j).\n#'\n#' @param abscissae sorted x values (at least 3)\n#' @param h_vals corresponding h(x) = log(f(x)) values\n#' @param lb lower bound of domain\n#' @param ub upper bound of domain\n#' @return list with z (breakpoints), seg_slopes, seg_intercepts, n_seg\ncompute_upper_hull <- function(abscissae, h_vals, lb, ub) {\n  k <- length(abscissae)\n  if (k < 3) stop(\"Need at least 3 abscissae for upper hull construction.\")\n\n  # Secant slopes\n  s <- diff(h_vals) / diff(abscissae)\n\n  # Build segments: each defined by intercept and slope in form u(x) = b + m*x\n  # Segments from left to right:\n  #   1. x_1: slope s[1] (only arm, extends left)\n  #   For j = 2,...,k-1: left arm slope s[j], right arm slope s[j-1]\n  #   Last: x_k: slope s[k-1] (only arm, extends right)\n\n  seg_px <- numeric(0)  # reference x\n  seg_ph <- numeric(0)  # reference h\n  seg_m  <- numeric(0)  # slope\n\n  # First segment\n  seg_px <- c(seg_px, abscissae[1])\n  seg_ph <- c(seg_ph, h_vals[1])\n  seg_m  <- c(seg_m, s[1])\n\n  # Interior points\n  for (j in 2:(k-1)) {\n    # Left arm\n    seg_px <- c(seg_px, abscissae[j])\n    seg_ph <- c(seg_ph, h_vals[j])\n    seg_m  <- c(seg_m, s[j])\n    # Right arm\n    seg_px <- c(seg_px, abscissae[j])\n    seg_ph <- c(seg_ph, h_vals[j])\n    seg_m  <- c(seg_m, s[j-1])\n  }\n\n  # Last segment\n  seg_px <- c(seg_px, abscissae[k])\n  seg_ph <- c(seg_ph, h_vals[k])\n  seg_m  <- c(seg_m, s[k-1])\n\n  # Convert to intercept form: u(x) = seg_int + seg_m * x\n  seg_int <- seg_ph - seg_m * seg_px\n\n  # Merge consecutive segments that are effectively the same line\n  # (happens when log-density is linear, e.g. exponential distribution)\n  keep <- rep(TRUE, length(seg_m))\n  for (i in 2:length(seg_m)) {\n    if (abs(seg_m[i] - seg_m[i-1]) < 1e-12 && abs(seg_int[i] - seg_int[i-1]) < 1e-6) {\n      keep[i] <- FALSE\n    }\n  }\n  seg_px <- seg_px[keep]\n  seg_ph <- seg_ph[keep]\n  seg_m  <- seg_m[keep]\n  seg_int <- seg_int[keep]\n  n_seg <- length(seg_m)\n\n  # Compute breakpoints between consecutive segments\n  if (n_seg == 1) {\n    z <- c(lb, ub)\n  } else {\n    bp <- numeric(n_seg - 1)\n    for (i in 1:(n_seg - 1)) {\n      if (seg_px[i] == seg_px[i+1]) {\n        # Same reference point (left/right arms of a tent)\n        bp[i] <- seg_px[i]\n      } else if (abs(seg_m[i] - seg_m[i+1]) < 1e-12) {\n        # Parallel lines: use midpoint of reference points\n        bp[i] <- (seg_px[i] + seg_px[i+1]) / 2\n      } else {\n        bp[i] <- (seg_int[i+1] - seg_int[i]) / (seg_m[i] - seg_m[i+1])\n      }\n    }\n    # Ensure breakpoints are valid\n    bp[is.na(bp) | is.nan(bp)] <- 0\n    z <- c(lb, bp, ub)\n  }\n\n  return(list(\n    z = z,\n    seg_slopes = seg_m,\n    seg_intercepts = seg_int,\n    n_seg = n_seg\n  ))\n}\n\n#' Evaluate the upper hull at a point x\n#' @param x scalar or vector of points\n#' @param hull list from compute_upper_hull\n#' @return u(x), the upper hull value(s)\neval_upper_hull <- function(x, hull) {\n  z <- hull$z\n  m <- hull$seg_slopes\n  b <- hull$seg_intercepts\n  n_seg <- hull$n_seg\n\n  seg_idx <- findInterval(x, z, rightmost.closed = TRUE)\n  seg_idx <- pmax(1, pmin(seg_idx, n_seg))\n\n  return(b[seg_idx] + m[seg_idx] * x)\n}\n\n#' Evaluate the lower hull (piecewise linear interpolation) at a point x\n#' @param x scalar point\n#' @param abscissae sorted x values\n#' @param h_vals corresponding h(x) values\n#' @return l(x). Returns -Inf if outside the range of abscissae.\neval_lower_hull <- function(x, abscissae, h_vals) {\n  k <- length(abscissae)\n  if (x <= abscissae[1] || x >= abscissae[k]) {\n    return(-Inf)\n  }\n  j <- findInterval(x, abscissae)\n  j <- pmax(1, pmin(j, k - 1))\n  t <- (x - abscissae[j]) / (abscissae[j+1] - abscissae[j])\n  return(h_vals[j] * (1 - t) + h_vals[j+1] * t)\n}\n\n#' Sample from the upper hull (piecewise-exponential distribution)\n#' @param n number of samples to draw\n#' @param hull list from compute_upper_hull\n#' @return vector of n samples\nsample_upper_hull <- function(n, hull) {\n  z <- hull$z\n  m <- hull$seg_slopes\n  b <- hull$seg_intercepts\n  n_seg <- hull$n_seg\n\n  # Compute area under each segment: integral of exp(b + m*x) from z[j] to z[j+1]\n  areas <- numeric(n_seg)\n  for (j in 1:n_seg) {\n    lo <- z[j]\n    hi <- z[j + 1]\n\n    if (!is.finite(lo)) {\n      if (m[j] <= 0) { areas[j] <- 0; next }\n      areas[j] <- exp(b[j] + m[j] * hi) / m[j]\n      next\n    }\n    if (!is.finite(hi)) {\n      if (m[j] >= 0) { areas[j] <- 0; next }\n      areas[j] <- -exp(b[j] + m[j] * lo) / m[j]\n      next\n    }\n\n    if (abs(m[j]) < 1e-15) {\n      areas[j] <- exp(b[j]) * (hi - lo)\n    } else {\n      log_hi <- b[j] + m[j] * hi\n      log_lo <- b[j] + m[j] * lo\n      log_max <- max(log_hi, log_lo)\n      areas[j] <- exp(log_max) * (exp(log_hi - log_max) - exp(log_lo - log_max)) / m[j]\n    }\n\n    if (is.na(areas[j]) || !is.finite(areas[j]) || areas[j] < 0) {\n      areas[j] <- 0\n    }\n  }\n\n  total_area <- sum(areas)\n  if (total_area <= 0 || !is.finite(total_area)) {\n    stop(\"Upper hull has zero or infinite area. Check domain bounds and initial abscissae.\")\n  }\n  probs <- areas / total_area\n\n  # Sample segments according to their areas\n  seg_choices <- sample.int(n_seg, size = n, replace = TRUE, prob = probs)\n\n  # For each chosen segment, sample from truncated exponential via inverse CDF\n  samples <- numeric(n)\n  for (i in 1:n) {\n    j <- seg_choices[i]\n    lo <- z[j]\n    hi <- z[j + 1]\n    u <- runif(1)\n\n    if (abs(m[j]) < 1e-15) {\n      if (!is.finite(lo)) lo <- hi - 20\n      if (!is.finite(hi)) hi <- lo + 20\n      samples[i] <- lo + u * (hi - lo)\n    } else if (!is.finite(lo)) {\n      # lo = -Inf, m > 0: inverse CDF gives x = hi + log(u) / m\n      samples[i] <- hi + log(u) / m[j]\n    } else if (!is.finite(hi)) {\n      # hi = +Inf, m < 0: inverse CDF gives x = lo + log(u) / m (m < 0)\n      samples[i] <- lo + log(u) / m[j]\n    } else {\n      log_lo <- m[j] * lo\n      log_hi <- m[j] * hi\n      log_max <- max(log_lo, log_hi)\n      exp_lo <- exp(log_lo - log_max)\n      exp_hi <- exp(log_hi - log_max)\n      val <- exp_lo + u * (exp_hi - exp_lo)\n      if (val <= 0) val <- 1e-300\n      samples[i] <- (log_max + log(val)) / m[j]\n    }\n  }\n  return(samples)\n}\n\n#' Choose initial abscissae for the ARS algorithm\n#' @param lb lower bound\n#' @param ub upper bound\n#' @param f density function\n#' @return vector of initial abscissae (at least 3, with required slope conditions)\nchoose_initial_abscissae <- function(lb, ub, f) {\n  # Generate candidate points\n  if (is.finite(lb) && is.finite(ub)) {\n    margin <- (ub - lb) * 0.02\n    pts <- seq(lb + margin, ub - margin, length.out = 20)\n  } else if (is.finite(lb) && !is.finite(ub)) {\n    pts <- lb + c(0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.2, 0.5, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 12, 18, 25, 40, 60)\n  } else if (!is.finite(lb) && is.finite(ub)) {\n    pts <- ub - c(0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.2, 0.5, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 12, 18, 25, 40, 60)\n    pts <- sort(pts)\n  } else {\n    pts <- seq(-5, 5, length.out = 21)\n  }\n\n  pts <- sort(unique(pts))\n\n  # Keep only points with positive density\n  vals <- f(pts)\n  good <- which(vals > 0 & !is.na(vals) & is.finite(vals))\n  if (length(good) < 3) {\n    stop(\"Cannot find at least 3 initial abscissae with positive density. Check domain bounds.\")\n  }\n  pts <- pts[good]\n  h <- log(vals[good])\n\n  # For infinite left bound: trim until first secant slope > 0\n  if (!is.finite(lb)) {\n    while (length(pts) > 3) {\n      s1 <- (h[2] - h[1]) / (pts[2] - pts[1])\n      if (s1 > 1e-12) break\n      pts <- pts[-1]; h <- h[-1]\n    }\n    if ((h[2] - h[1]) / (pts[2] - pts[1]) <= 0) {\n      stop(\"Cannot find initial points where log-density is increasing from the left. Check domain.\")\n    }\n  }\n\n  # For infinite right bound: trim until last secant slope < 0\n  if (!is.finite(ub)) {\n    while (length(pts) > 3) {\n      k <- length(pts)\n      sk <- (h[k] - h[k-1]) / (pts[k] - pts[k-1])\n      if (sk < -1e-12) break\n      pts <- pts[-k]; h <- h[-k]\n    }\n    k <- length(pts)\n    if ((h[k] - h[k-1]) / (pts[k] - pts[k-1]) >= 0) {\n      stop(\"Cannot find initial points where log-density is decreasing on the right. Check domain.\")\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (length(pts) < 3) {\n    stop(\"Cannot find suitable initial abscissae.\")\n  }\n\n  # Subsample to avoid too many initial points (keep ~5-8)\n  if (length(pts) > 8) {\n    idx <- round(seq(1, length(pts), length.out = 8))\n    # But always keep first and last\n    idx <- sort(unique(c(1, idx, length(pts))))\n    pts <- pts[idx]\n  }\n\n  return(pts)\n}\n\n#' Validate inputs to the ars function\nvalidate_inputs <- function(f, n, lb, ub) {\n  if (!is.function(f)) {\n    stop(\"'f' must be a function.\")\n  }\n  if (!is.numeric(n) || length(n) != 1 || n < 1 || n != as.integer(n)) {\n    stop(\"'n' must be a positive integer.\")\n  }\n  if (!is.numeric(lb) || length(lb) != 1) {\n    stop(\"'lb' must be a single numeric value.\")\n  }\n  if (!is.numeric(ub) || length(ub) != 1) {\n    stop(\"'ub' must be a single numeric value.\")\n  }\n  if (lb >= ub) {\n    stop(\"Lower bound must be strictly less than upper bound.\")\n  }\n}\n\n# --- Main ARS Function ---\n\n#' Adaptive Rejection Sampling\n#'\n#' @param f density function (possibly unnormalized), must be log-concave.\n#' @param support optional 2-element vector c(lb, ub) for the domain\n#' @param n number of samples to generate (default 100)\n#' @param lb lower bound of the domain (default -Inf)\n#' @param ub upper bound of the domain (default Inf)\n#' @param ... additional arguments (currently unused)\n#' @return vector of n samples from the target distribution\nars <- function(f, support = NULL, n = 100, lb = -Inf, ub = Inf, ...) {\n  # Handle the support vector argument\n  if (!is.null(support) && is.numeric(support) && length(support) == 2) {\n    lb <- support[1]\n    ub <- support[2]\n  }\n\n  validate_inputs(f, n, lb, ub)\n\n  abscissae <- choose_initial_abscissae(lb, ub, f)\n  h_vals <- eval_log_density(f, abscissae)\n  check_log_concavity(abscissae, h_vals)\n\n  samples <- numeric(n)\n  count <- 0\n  max_iter <- n * 200\n  iter <- 0\n\n  while (count < n && iter < max_iter) {\n    iter <- iter + 1\n\n    hull <- compute_upper_hull(abscissae, h_vals, lb, ub)\n    x_star <- sample_upper_hull(1, hull)\n\n    u <- runif(1)\n    u_val <- eval_upper_hull(x_star, hull)\n    l_val <- eval_lower_hull(x_star, abscissae, h_vals)\n\n    if (u <= exp(l_val - u_val)) {\n      count <- count + 1\n      samples[count] <- x_star\n    } else {\n      h_star <- eval_log_density(f, x_star)\n\n      if (u <= exp(h_star - u_val)) {\n        count <- count + 1\n        samples[count] <- x_star\n      }\n\n      # Add new point to abscissae\n      insert_pos <- findInterval(x_star, abscissae) + 1\n      abscissae <- append(abscissae, x_star, after = insert_pos - 1)\n      h_vals <- append(h_vals, h_star, after = insert_pos - 1)\n\n      check_log_concavity(abscissae, h_vals)\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (count < n) {\n    warning(paste(\"Only generated\", count, \"out of\", n, \"requested samples.\"))\n  }\n\n  return(samples)\n}\n\n# --- Test Function ---\n\n#' Run formal tests of the ARS implementation\ntest <- function() {\n  cat(\"============================================================\\n\")\n  cat(\"     Adaptive Rejection Sampling (ARS) - Test Suite\\n\")\n  cat(\"============================================================\\n\\n\")\n\n  pass_count <- 0\n  fail_count <- 0\n\n  run_test <- function(name, expr) {\n    result <- tryCatch(expr, error = function(e) {\n      cat(paste0(\"  Error: \", e$message, \"\\n\"))\n      return(FALSE)\n    })\n    if (isTRUE(result)) {\n      cat(paste0(name, \": PASS\\n\\n\"))\n      pass_count <<- pass_count + 1\n    } else {\n      cat(paste0(name, \": FAIL\\n\\n\"))\n      fail_count <<- fail_count + 1\n    }\n  }\n\n  # ---- Input Validation Tests ----\n  cat(\"--- Input Validation Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_negative_n\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that negative n is rejected...\\n\")\n    tryCatch({ ars(dnorm, n = -5); FALSE },\n             error = function(e) grepl(\"positive integer\", e$message))\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_zero_n\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that n=0 is rejected...\\n\")\n    tryCatch({ ars(dnorm, n = 0); FALSE },\n             error = function(e) grepl(\"positive integer\", e$message))\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_non_integer_n\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that non-integer n is rejected...\\n\")\n    tryCatch({ ars(dnorm, n = 3.5); FALSE },\n             error = function(e) grepl(\"positive integer\", e$message))\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_invalid_bounds\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that lb >= ub is rejected...\\n\")\n    tryCatch({ ars(dnorm, n = 10, lb = 5, ub = 2); FALSE },\n             error = function(e) grepl(\"strictly less\", e$message))\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_non_function_f\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that non-function f is rejected...\\n\")\n    tryCatch({ ars(\"not_a_function\", n = 10); FALSE },\n             error = function(e) grepl(\"must be a function\", e$message))\n  })\n\n  # ---- Log-Concavity Tests ----\n  cat(\"--- Log-Concavity Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_bimodal_rejection\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing rejection of bimodal (non-log-concave) density...\\n\")\n    bimodal <- function(x) 0.5 * dnorm(x, -3, 0.5) + 0.5 * dnorm(x, 3, 0.5)\n    tryCatch({ ars(bimodal, n = 1000, lb = -10, ub = 10); FALSE },\n             error = function(e) grepl(\"[Ll]og-concav\", e$message))\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_cauchy_rejection\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing rejection of Cauchy (non-log-concave) density...\\n\")\n    tryCatch({ ars(dcauchy, n = 1000, lb = -100, ub = 100); FALSE },\n             error = function(e) grepl(\"[Ll]og-concav\", e$message))\n  })\n\n  # ---- Module-Level Tests ----\n  cat(\"--- Module-Level Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_eval_log_density\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing eval_log_density with dnorm...\\n\")\n    result <- eval_log_density(dnorm, 0)\n    expected <- log(dnorm(0))\n    cat(paste0(\"  log(dnorm(0)) = \", round(result, 6),\n               \" (expected \", round(expected, 6), \")\\n\"))\n    abs(result - expected) < 1e-10\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_check_log_concavity_valid\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing log-concavity check on concave data...\\n\")\n    x <- c(-2, -1, 0, 1, 2)\n    h <- -x^2\n    tryCatch({ check_log_concavity(x, h); TRUE }, error = function(e) FALSE)\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_check_log_concavity_invalid\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing log-concavity check on convex data...\\n\")\n    x <- c(-2, -1, 0, 1, 2)\n    h <- x^2\n    tryCatch({ check_log_concavity(x, h); FALSE },\n             error = function(e) grepl(\"[Ll]og-concav\", e$message))\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_upper_hull_above_density\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that upper hull >= log-density at test points...\\n\")\n    x_abs <- c(-3, -1, 0, 1, 3)\n    h_abs <- eval_log_density(dnorm, x_abs)\n    hull <- compute_upper_hull(x_abs, h_abs, -Inf, Inf)\n    test_pts <- seq(-2.5, 2.5, by = 0.1)\n    h_test <- eval_log_density(dnorm, test_pts)\n    u_test <- eval_upper_hull(test_pts, hull)\n    all_above <- all(u_test >= h_test - 1e-6)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Upper hull >= log-density at all \", length(test_pts),\n               \" test points: \", all_above, \"\\n\"))\n    all_above\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_lower_hull_below_density\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that lower hull <= log-density at test points...\\n\")\n    x_abs <- c(-3, -1, 0, 1, 3)\n    h_abs <- eval_log_density(dnorm, x_abs)\n    test_pts <- seq(-2.5, 2.5, by = 0.1)\n    h_test <- eval_log_density(dnorm, test_pts)\n    l_test <- sapply(test_pts, function(x) eval_lower_hull(x, x_abs, h_abs))\n    all_below <- all(l_test <= h_test + 1e-6)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Lower hull <= log-density at all \", length(test_pts),\n               \" test points: \", all_below, \"\\n\"))\n    all_below\n  })\n\n  # ---- Sampling Tests: Standard Normal ----\n  cat(\"--- Standard Normal Distribution Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_normal_unbounded\", {\n    cat(\"  Sampling 5000 points from N(0,1) on (-Inf, Inf)...\\n\")\n    set.seed(42)\n    samples <- ars(n = 5000, f = dnorm, lb = -Inf, ub = Inf)\n    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(m, 4), \" (expected ~0)\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(s, 4), \" (expected ~1)\\n\"))\n    writeLines(as.character(samples), \"/app/normal_samples.txt\")\n    cat(\"  Saved samples to /app/normal_samples.txt\\n\")\n    ks <- ks.test(samples, \"pnorm\")\n    cat(paste0(\"  KS test p-value: \", round(ks$p.value, 4), \"\\n\"))\n    abs(m) < 0.15 && abs(s - 1) < 0.15 && ks$p.value > 0.01\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_normal_bounded\", {\n    cat(\"  Sampling 2000 points from N(0,1) on [-5, 5]...\\n\")\n    set.seed(123)\n    samples <- ars(dnorm, c(-5, 5), n = 2000)\n    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(m, 4), \"\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(s, 4), \"\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Range: [\", round(min(samples), 4), \", \", round(max(samples), 4), \"]\\n\"))\n    all(samples >= -5 & samples <= 5) && abs(m) < 0.2\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_normal_shifted\", {\n    cat(\"  Sampling 3000 points from N(5, 2^2) on (-Inf, Inf)...\\n\")\n    set.seed(200)\n    shifted_norm <- function(x) dnorm(x, mean = 5, sd = 2)\n    samples <- ars(shifted_norm, n = 3000, lb = -Inf, ub = Inf)\n    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(m, 4), \" (expected ~5)\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(s, 4), \" (expected ~2)\\n\"))\n    ks <- ks.test(samples, \"pnorm\", mean = 5, sd = 2)\n    cat(paste0(\"  KS test p-value: \", Accept by rejection test","         count <- count + 1","         samples[count] <- x_star","       }"," ","-      # Update abscissae with new point (whether accepted or not)","+      # Add new point to abscissae","       insert_pos <- findInterval(x_star, abscissae) + 1","       abscissae <- append(abscissae, x_star, after = insert_pos - 1)","       h_vals <- append(h_vals, h_star, after = insert_pos - 1)"," ","-      # Re-check log-concavity with new point","       check_log_concavity(abscissae, h_vals)","     }","   }"]},{"oldStart":436,"oldLines":8,"newStart":424,"newLines":6,"lines":[" # --- Test Function ---"," "," #' Run formal tests of the ARS implementation","-#' Compares samples against known distributions using statistical tests.","-#' Output format: \"TEST_NAME: PASS\" or \"TEST_NAME: FAIL\""," test <- function() {","   cat(\"============================================================\\n\")","   cat(\"     Adaptive Rejection Sampling (ARS) - Test Suite\\n\")"]},{"oldStart":531,"oldLines":7,"newStart":517,"newLines":7,"lines":["   run_test(\"test_check_log_concavity_invalid\", {","     cat(\"  Testing log-concavity check on convex data...\\n\")","     x <- c(-2, -1, 0, 1, 2)","-    h <- x^2  # convex, not concave","+    h <- x^2","     tryCatch({ check_log_concavity(x, h); FALSE },","              error = function(e) grepl(\"[Ll]og-concav\", e$message))","   })"]},{"oldStart":608,"oldLines":9,"newStart":594,"newLines":9,"lines":["   cat(\"--- Exponential Distribution Tests ---\\n\\n\")"," ","   run_test(\"test_exponential_sampling\", {","-    cat(\"  Sampling 5000 points from Exp(1) on [0.001, 100]...\\n\")","+    cat(\"  Sampling 5000 points from Exp(1) on [0.001, 50]...\\n\")","     set.seed(99)","-    samples <- ars(dexp, c(0.001, 100), n = 5000)","+    samples <- ars(dexp, c(0.001, 50), n = 5000)","     m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)","     cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(m, 4), \" (expected ~1)\\n\"))","     cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(s, 4), \" (expected ~1)\\n\"))"]}],"originalFile":"###############################################################################\n# Adaptive Rejection Sampling (ARS)\n# Based on Gilks, W.R. & Wild, P. (1992)\n# \"Adaptive Rejection Sampling for Gibbs Sampling\"\n# Journal of the Royal Statistical Society: Series C, 41(2), 337-348.\n#\n# Derivative-free implementation using secant-based upper hull construction.\n###############################################################################\n\n# --- Auxiliary / Module Functions ---\n\n#' Evaluate the log of a density function safely\n#' @param f density function (possibly unnormalized)\n#' @param x point(s) at which to evaluate\n#' @return log(f(x)), with protection against f(x) <= 0\neval_log_density <- function(f, x) {\n  fx <- f(x)\n  if (any(is.na(fx) | is.nan(fx))) {\n    stop(\"Density function returned NA/NaN.\")\n  }\n  if (any(fx < 0)) {\n    stop(\"Density function returned negative value.\")\n  }\n  result <- rep(-Inf, length(fx))\n  pos <- fx > 0\n  result[pos] <- log(fx[pos])\n  return(result)\n}\n\n#' Check log-concavity at three consecutive abscissae\n#' @param abscissae sorted vector of x values\n#' @param h_vals corresponding log-density values h(x)\n#' @return TRUE if log-concave, else stops with error\ncheck_log_concavity <- function(abscissae, h_vals) {\n  n <- length(abscissae)\n  if (n < 3) return(TRUE)\n  for (i in 1:(n - 2)) {\n    x1 <- abscissae[i];   h1 <- h_vals[i]\n    x2 <- abscissae[i+1]; h2 <- h_vals[i+1]\n    x3 <- abscissae[i+2]; h3 <- h_vals[i+2]\n    slope_left  <- (h2 - h1) / (x2 - x1)\n    slope_right <- (h3 - h2) / (x3 - x2)\n    if (slope_right > slope_left + 1e-10) {\n      stop(paste0(\n        \"Log-concavity violated at x = \", x2,\n        \". Left slope = \", round(slope_left, 6),\n        \", Right slope = \", round(slope_right, 6),\n        \". The density may not be log-concave.\"\n      ))\n    }\n  }\n  return(TRUE)\n}\n\n#' Compute the upper hull for the ARS algorithm (derivative-free version)\n#'\n#' For k abscissae with log-density values, the upper hull is piecewise linear\n#' and lies above the log-density everywhere. It is constructed from the secant\n#' slopes between consecutive abscissae. By log-concavity, secant slopes\n#' decrease: s_1 > s_2 > ... > s_{k-1}.\n#'\n#' At each interior abscissa x_j, the upper hull has a \"tent\" with:\n#'   - left arm:  slope s_j (right secant), valid for x <= x_j\n#'   - right arm: slope s_{j-1} (left secant), valid for x >= x_j\n#'\n#' @param abscissae sorted x values (at least 3)\n#' @param h_vals corresponding h(x) = log(f(x)) values\n#' @param lb lower bound of domain\n#' @param ub upper bound of domain\n#' @return list with segment info for the piecewise linear upper hull\ncompute_upper_hull <- function(abscissae, h_vals, lb, ub) {\n  k <- length(abscissae)\n  if (k < 3) stop(\"Need at least 3 abscissae for upper hull construction.\")\n\n  # Secant slopes: s[j] = slope from x_j to x_{j+1}\n  s <- diff(h_vals) / diff(abscissae)\n\n  # Build the list of line segments for the upper hull.\n  # Each segment is defined by a point (px, ph) and slope m:\n  #   u(x) = ph + m * (x - px) = (ph - m*px) + m*x\n  #\n  # Segments from left to right:\n  # 1. x_1's left arm: slope s[1], through (x_1, h_1), for x from lb to z_1\n  # Then for each interior point j = 2,...,k-1:\n  #   left arm:  slope s[j], through (x_j, h_j)\n  #   right arm: slope s[j-1], through (x_j, h_j)\n  # Last: x_k's right arm: slope s[k-1], through (x_k, h_k), for x from z_{k-1} to ub\n\n  n_seg <- 2 * (k - 1)\n  seg_px <- numeric(n_seg)\n  seg_ph <- numeric(n_seg)\n  seg_m  <- numeric(n_seg)\n\n  idx <- 1\n  # First segment: x_1, slope s[1]\n  seg_px[idx] <- abscissae[1]; seg_ph[idx] <- h_vals[1]; seg_m[idx] <- s[1]\n  idx <- idx + 1\n\n  # Interior points\n  for (j in 2:(k-1)) {\n    # Left arm: slope s[j]\n    seg_px[idx] <- abscissae[j]; seg_ph[idx] <- h_vals[j]; seg_m[idx] <- s[j]\n    idx <- idx + 1\n    # Right arm: slope s[j-1]\n    seg_px[idx] <- abscissae[j]; seg_ph[idx] <- h_vals[j]; seg_m[idx] <- s[j-1]\n    idx <- idx + 1\n  }\n\n  # Last segment: x_k, slope s[k-1]\n  seg_px[idx] <- abscissae[k]; seg_ph[idx] <- h_vals[k]; seg_m[idx] <- s[k-1]\n\n  # Convert to intercept form: u(x) = intercept + slope * x\n  seg_int <- seg_ph - seg_m * seg_px\n\n  # Compute breakpoints between consecutive segments\n  bp <- numeric(n_seg - 1)\n  for (i in 1:(n_seg - 1)) {\n    if (seg_px[i] == seg_px[i+1] && seg_ph[i] == seg_ph[i+1]) {\n      # Same point (left/right arm of same tent): breakpoint is at that x\n      bp[i] <- seg_px[i]\n    } else {\n      # Intersection of two different lines\n      bp[i] <- (seg_int[i+1] - seg_int[i]) / (seg_m[i] - seg_m[i+1])\n    }\n  }\n\n  z <- c(lb, bp, ub)\n\n  return(list(\n    z = z,\n    seg_slopes = seg_m,\n    seg_intercepts = seg_int,\n    n_seg = n_seg\n  ))\n}\n\n#' Evaluate the upper hull at a point x\n#' @param x scalar or vector of points\n#' @param hull list from compute_upper_hull\n#' @return u(x), the upper hull value(s)\neval_upper_hull <- function(x, hull) {\n  z <- hull$z\n  m <- hull$seg_slopes\n  b <- hull$seg_intercepts\n  n_seg <- hull$n_seg\n\n  seg_idx <- findInterval(x, z, rightmost.closed = TRUE)\n  seg_idx <- pmax(1, pmin(seg_idx, n_seg))\n\n  return(b[seg_idx] + m[seg_idx] * x)\n}\n\n#' Evaluate the lower hull (piecewise linear through abscissae) at a point x\n#' @param x scalar point\n#' @param abscissae sorted x values\n#' @param h_vals corresponding h(x) values\n#' @return l(x), the lower hull value. -Inf if outside the range of abscissae.\neval_lower_hull <- function(x, abscissae, h_vals) {\n  k <- length(abscissae)\n  if (x <= abscissae[1] || x >= abscissae[k]) {\n    return(-Inf)\n  }\n  j <- findInterval(x, abscissae)\n  j <- pmax(1, pmin(j, k - 1))\n  t <- (x - abscissae[j]) / (abscissae[j+1] - abscissae[j])\n  return(h_vals[j] * (1 - t) + h_vals[j+1] * t)\n}\n\n#' Sample from the upper hull (piecewise-exponential distribution)\n#' @param n number of samples to draw\n#' @param hull list from compute_upper_hull\n#' @return vector of n samples\nsample_upper_hull <- function(n, hull) {\n  z <- hull$z\n  m <- hull$seg_slopes\n  b <- hull$seg_intercepts\n  n_seg <- hull$n_seg\n\n  # Compute area under each segment: integral of exp(b + m*x) from z[j] to z[j+1]\n  areas <- numeric(n_seg)\n  for (j in 1:n_seg) {\n    lo <- z[j]\n    hi <- z[j + 1]\n\n    # Handle infinite bounds: for the integral to converge, slope must be favorable\n    if (!is.finite(lo)) {\n      # lo = -Inf. Need m[j] > 0 for convergence\n      if (m[j] <= 0) {\n        areas[j] <- 0\n        next\n      }\n      # Integral of exp(b + m*x) from -Inf to hi = exp(b + m*hi) / m\n      areas[j] <- exp(b[j] + m[j] * hi) / m[j]\n      next\n    }\n    if (!is.finite(hi)) {\n      # hi = +Inf. Need m[j] < 0 for convergence\n      if (m[j] >= 0) {\n        areas[j] <- 0\n        next\n      }\n      # Integral of exp(b + m*x) from lo to Inf = -exp(b + m*lo) / m\n      areas[j] <- -exp(b[j] + m[j] * lo) / m[j]\n      next\n    }\n\n    if (abs(m[j]) < 1e-15) {\n      areas[j] <- exp(b[j]) * (hi - lo)\n    } else {\n      # Integral = (exp(b + m*hi) - exp(b + m*lo)) / m\n      # For numerical stability, factor out the larger value\n      log_hi <- b[j] + m[j] * hi\n      log_lo <- b[j] + m[j] * lo\n      log_max <- max(log_hi, log_lo)\n      areas[j] <- exp(log_max) * (exp(log_hi - log_max) - exp(log_lo - log_max)) / m[j]\n    }\n\n    if (is.na(areas[j]) || !is.finite(areas[j]) || areas[j] < 0) {\n      areas[j] <- 0\n    }\n  }\n\n  total_area <- sum(areas)\n  if (total_area <= 0 || !is.finite(total_area)) {\n    stop(\"Upper hull has zero or infinite area. Check domain bounds and initial abscissae.\")\n  }\n  probs <- areas / total_area\n\n  # Sample segments according to their areas\n  seg_choices <- sample.int(n_seg, size = n, replace = TRUE, prob = probs)\n\n  # For each chosen segment, sample from the truncated exponential via inverse CDF\n  samples <- numeric(n)\n  for (i in 1:n) {\n    j <- seg_choices[i]\n    lo <- z[j]\n    hi <- z[j + 1]\n    u <- runif(1)\n\n    if (abs(m[j]) < 1e-15) {\n      # Uniform on [lo, hi]\n      if (!is.finite(lo)) lo <- hi - 20\n      if (!is.finite(hi)) hi <- lo + 20\n      samples[i] <- lo + u * (hi - lo)\n    } else if (!is.finite(lo)) {\n      # lo = -Inf, m > 0: CDF(x) = exp(m*(x-hi)) for x <= hi\n      # Inverse: x = hi + log(u) / m\n      samples[i] <- hi + log(u) / m[j]\n    } else if (!is.finite(hi)) {\n      # hi = +Inf, m < 0: CDF(x) = 1 - exp(m*(x-lo)) for x >= lo\n      # Inverse: x = lo + log(1 - u) / m  ... but log(1-u) ~ log(u) in distribution\n      samples[i] <- lo + log(u) / m[j]  # since m < 0, this gives x > lo\n    } else {\n      # Both finite: inverse CDF of truncated exponential\n      log_lo <- m[j] * lo\n      log_hi <- m[j] * hi\n      # CDF: F(x) = (exp(m*x) - exp(m*lo)) / (exp(m*hi) - exp(m*lo))\n      # Inverse: x = log(exp(m*lo) + u*(exp(m*hi) - exp(m*lo))) / m\n      log_max <- max(log_lo, log_hi)\n      exp_lo <- exp(log_lo - log_max)\n      exp_hi <- exp(log_hi - log_max)\n      samples[i] <- (log_max + log(exp_lo + u * (exp_hi - exp_lo))) / m[j]\n    }\n  }\n  return(samples)\n}\n\n#' Choose initial abscissae for the ARS algorithm\n#' Ensures at least 3 points with positive density, and that\n#' boundary slopes are compatible with infinite domains.\n#' @param lb lower bound\n#' @param ub upper bound\n#' @param f density function\n#' @param n_init number of initial points (default 5, minimum 3)\n#' @return vector of initial abscissae\nchoose_initial_abscissae <- function(lb, ub, f, n_init = 20) {\n  n_init <- max(n_init, 5)\n\n  # Generate candidate points\n  if (is.finite(lb) && is.finite(ub)) {\n    margin <- (ub - lb) * 0.02\n    pts <- seq(lb + margin, ub - margin, length.out = n_init)\n  } else if (is.finite(lb) && !is.finite(ub)) {\n    pts <- lb + c(0.1, 0.5, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 12, 18, 25, 35, 50,\n                  0.2, 0.8, 1.5, 4, 7, 10, 15, 20)[1:n_init]\n  } else if (!is.finite(lb) && is.finite(ub)) {\n    pts <- ub - c(0.1, 0.5, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 12, 18, 25, 35, 50,\n                  0.2, 0.8, 1.5, 4, 7, 10, 15, 20)[1:n_init]\n  } else {\n    # Both infinite: center around 0\n    pts <- c(seq(-4, 4, length.out = n_init))\n  }\n\n  pts <- sort(unique(pts))\n\n  # Keep only points with positive density\n  vals <- f(pts)\n  good <- which(vals > 0 & !is.na(vals) & is.finite(vals))\n  if (length(good) < 3) {\n    stop(\"Cannot find at least 3 initial abscissae with positive density. Check domain bounds.\")\n  }\n  pts <- pts[good]\n\n  # Compute log-density\n  h <- log(vals[good])\n\n  # For infinite left bound: need first secant slope > 0\n  # For infinite right bound: need last secant slope < 0\n  # Trim from each end until this is satisfied\n  if (!is.finite(lb)) {\n    while (length(pts) > 3) {\n      s1 <- (h[2] - h[1]) / (pts[2] - pts[1])\n      if (s1 > 0) break\n      pts <- pts[-1]; h <- h[-1]\n    }\n  }\n  if (!is.finite(ub)) {\n    while (length(pts) > 3) {\n      k <- length(pts)\n      sk <- (h[k] - h[k-1]) / (pts[k] - pts[k-1])\n      if (sk < 0) break\n      pts <- pts[-k]; h <- h[-k]\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (length(pts) < 3) {\n    stop(\"Cannot find suitable initial abscissae. The density mode may be outside the initial search range.\")\n  }\n\n  return(pts)\n}\n\n#' Validate inputs to the ars function\n#' @param f density function\n#' @param n number of samples\n#' @param lb lower bound\n#' @param ub upper bound\nvalidate_inputs <- function(f, n, lb, ub) {\n  if (!is.function(f)) {\n    stop(\"'f' must be a function.\")\n  }\n  if (!is.numeric(n) || length(n) != 1 || n < 1 || n != as.integer(n)) {\n    stop(\"'n' must be a positive integer.\")\n  }\n  if (!is.numeric(lb) || length(lb) != 1) {\n    stop(\"'lb' must be a single numeric value.\")\n  }\n  if (!is.numeric(ub) || length(ub) != 1) {\n    stop(\"'ub' must be a single numeric value.\")\n  }\n  if (lb >= ub) {\n    stop(\"Lower bound must be strictly less than upper bound.\")\n  }\n}\n\n# --- Main ARS Function ---\n\n#' Adaptive Rejection Sampling\n#'\n#' @param f density function (possibly unnormalized), must be log-concave.\n#'          Should accept a vector and return a vector of density values.\n#' @param support optional 2-element vector c(lb, ub) for the domain\n#' @param n number of samples to generate (default 100)\n#' @param lb lower bound of the domain (default -Inf)\n#' @param ub upper bound of the domain (default Inf)\n#' @param ... additional arguments (currently unused)\n#' @return vector of n samples from the target distribution\nars <- function(f, support = NULL, n = 100, lb = -Inf, ub = Inf, ...) {\n  # Handle the support vector argument (alternative call pattern)\n  if (!is.null(support) && is.numeric(support) && length(support) == 2) {\n    lb <- support[1]\n    ub <- support[2]\n  }\n\n  # Validate inputs\n  validate_inputs(f, n, lb, ub)\n\n  # Choose initial abscissae (at least 3)\n  abscissae <- choose_initial_abscissae(lb, ub, f)\n  h_vals <- eval_log_density(f, abscissae)\n\n  # Check initial log-concavity\n  check_log_concavity(abscissae, h_vals)\n\n  # Main sampling loop\n  samples <- numeric(n)\n  count <- 0\n  max_iter <- n * 200  # safety limit\n  iter <- 0\n\n  while (count < n && iter < max_iter) {\n    iter <- iter + 1\n\n    # Build upper hull\n    hull <- compute_upper_hull(abscissae, h_vals, lb, ub)\n\n    # Sample a candidate from the upper hull\n    x_star <- sample_upper_hull(1, hull)\n\n    # Squeeze and rejection test\n    u <- runif(1)\n    u_val <- eval_upper_hull(x_star, hull)\n    l_val <- eval_lower_hull(x_star, abscissae, h_vals)\n\n    if (u <= exp(l_val - u_val)) {\n      # Accept by squeeze test (no density evaluation needed)\n      count <- count + 1\n      samples[count] <- x_star\n    } else {\n      # Need to evaluate the true log-density\n      h_star <- eval_log_density(f, x_star)\n\n      if (u <= exp(h_star - u_val)) {\n        # Accept by rejection test\n        count <- count + 1\n        samples[count] <- x_star\n      }\n\n      # Update abscissae with new point (whether accepted or not)\n      insert_pos <- findInterval(x_star, abscissae) + 1\n      abscissae <- append(abscissae, x_star, after = insert_pos - 1)\n      h_vals <- append(h_vals, h_star, after = insert_pos - 1)\n\n      # Re-check log-concavity with new point\n      check_log_concavity(abscissae, h_vals)\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (count < n) {\n    warning(paste(\"Only generated\", count, \"out of\", n, \"requested samples.\"))\n  }\n\n  return(samples)\n}\n\n# --- Test Function ---\n\n#' Run formal tests of the ARS implementation\n#' Compares samples against known distributions using statistical tests.\n#' Output format: \"TEST_NAME: PASS\" or \"TEST_NAME: FAIL\"\ntest <- function() {\n  cat(\"============================================================\\n\")\n  cat(\"     Adaptive Rejection Sampling (ARS) - Test Suite\\n\")\n  cat(\"============================================================\\n\\n\")\n\n  pass_count <- 0\n  fail_count <- 0\n\n  run_test <- function(name, expr) {\n    result <- tryCatch(expr, error = function(e) {\n      cat(paste0(\"  Error: \", e$message, \"\\n\"))\n      return(FALSE)\n    })\n    if (isTRUE(result)) {\n      cat(paste0(name, \": PASS\\n\\n\"))\n      pass_count <<- pass_count + 1\n    } else {\n      cat(paste0(name, \": FAIL\\n\\n\"))\n      fail_count <<- fail_count + 1\n    }\n  }\n\n  # ---- Input Validation Tests ----\n  cat(\"--- Input Validation Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_negative_n\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that negative n is rejected...\\n\")\n    tryCatch({ ars(dnorm, n = -5); FALSE },\n             error = function(e) grepl(\"positive integer\", e$message))\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_zero_n\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that n=0 is rejected...\\n\")\n    tryCatch({ ars(dnorm, n = 0); FALSE },\n             error = function(e) grepl(\"positive integer\", e$message))\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_non_integer_n\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that non-integer n is rejected...\\n\")\n    tryCatch({ ars(dnorm, n = 3.5); FALSE },\n             error = function(e) grepl(\"positive integer\", e$message))\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_invalid_bounds\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that lb >= ub is rejected...\\n\")\n    tryCatch({ ars(dnorm, n = 10, lb = 5, ub = 2); FALSE },\n             error = function(e) grepl(\"strictly less\", e$message))\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_non_function_f\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that non-function f is rejected...\\n\")\n    tryCatch({ ars(\"not_a_function\", n = 10); FALSE },\n             error = function(e) grepl(\"must be a function\", e$message))\n  })\n\n  # ---- Log-Concavity Tests ----\n  cat(\"--- Log-Concavity Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_bimodal_rejection\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing rejection of bimodal (non-log-concave) density...\\n\")\n    bimodal <- function(x) 0.5 * dnorm(x, -3, 0.5) + 0.5 * dnorm(x, 3, 0.5)\n    tryCatch({ ars(bimodal, n = 1000, lb = -10, ub = 10); FALSE },\n             error = function(e) grepl(\"[Ll]og-concav\", e$message))\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_cauchy_rejection\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing rejection of Cauchy (non-log-concave) density...\\n\")\n    tryCatch({ ars(dcauchy, n = 1000, lb = -100, ub = 100); FALSE },\n             error = function(e) grepl(\"[Ll]og-concav\", e$message))\n  })\n\n  # ---- Module-Level Tests ----\n  cat(\"--- Module-Level Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_eval_log_density\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing eval_log_density with dnorm...\\n\")\n    result <- eval_log_density(dnorm, 0)\n    expected <- log(dnorm(0))\n    cat(paste0(\"  log(dnorm(0)) = \", round(result, 6),\n               \" (expected \", round(expected, 6), \")\\n\"))\n    abs(result - expected) < 1e-10\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_check_log_concavity_valid\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing log-concavity check on concave data...\\n\")\n    x <- c(-2, -1, 0, 1, 2)\n    h <- -x^2\n    tryCatch({ check_log_concavity(x, h); TRUE }, error = function(e) FALSE)\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_check_log_concavity_invalid\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing log-concavity check on convex data...\\n\")\n    x <- c(-2, -1, 0, 1, 2)\n    h <- x^2  # convex, not concave\n    tryCatch({ check_log_concavity(x, h); FALSE },\n             error = function(e) grepl(\"[Ll]og-concav\", e$message))\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_upper_hull_above_density\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that upper hull >= log-density at test points...\\n\")\n    x_abs <- c(-3, -1, 0, 1, 3)\n    h_abs <- eval_log_density(dnorm, x_abs)\n    hull <- compute_upper_hull(x_abs, h_abs, -Inf, Inf)\n    test_pts <- seq(-2.5, 2.5, by = 0.1)\n    h_test <- eval_log_density(dnorm, test_pts)\n    u_test <- eval_upper_hull(test_pts, hull)\n    all_above <- all(u_test >= h_test - 1e-6)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Upper hull >= log-density at all \", length(test_pts),\n               \" test points: \", all_above, \"\\n\"))\n    all_above\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_lower_hull_below_density\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that lower hull <= log-density at test points...\\n\")\n    x_abs <- c(-3, -1, 0, 1, 3)\n    h_abs <- eval_log_density(dnorm, x_abs)\n    test_pts <- seq(-2.5, 2.5, by = 0.1)\n    h_test <- eval_log_density(dnorm, test_pts)\n    l_test <- sapply(test_pts, function(x) eval_lower_hull(x, x_abs, h_abs))\n    all_below <- all(l_test <= h_test + 1e-6)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Lower hull <= log-density at all \", length(test_pts),\n               \" test points: \", all_below, \"\\n\"))\n    all_below\n  })\n\n  # ---- Sampling Tests: Standard Normal ----\n  cat(\"--- Standard Normal Distribution Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_normal_unbounded\", {\n    cat(\"  Sampling 5000 points from N(0,1) on (-Inf, Inf)...\\n\")\n    set.seed(42)\n    samples <- ars(n = 5000, f = dnorm, lb = -Inf, ub = Inf)\n    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(m, 4), \" (expected ~0)\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(s, 4), \" (expected ~1)\\n\"))\n    writeLines(as.character(samples), \"/app/normal_samples.txt\")\n    cat(\"  Saved samples to /app/normal_samples.txt\\n\")\n    ks <- ks.test(samples, \"pnorm\")\n    cat(paste0(\"  KS test p-value: \", round(ks$p.value, 4), \"\\n\"))\n    abs(m) < 0.15 && abs(s - 1) < 0.15 && ks$p.value > 0.01\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_normal_bounded\", {\n    cat(\"  Sampling 2000 points from N(0,1) on [-5, 5]...\\n\")\n    set.seed(123)\n    samples <- ars(dnorm, c(-5, 5), n = 2000)\n    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(m, 4), \"\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(s, 4), \"\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Range: [\", round(min(samples), 4), \", \", round(max(samples), 4), \"]\\n\"))\n    all(samples >= -5 & samples <= 5) && abs(m) < 0.2\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_normal_shifted\", {\n    cat(\"  Sampling 3000 points from N(5, 2^2) on (-Inf, Inf)...\\n\")\n    set.seed(200)\n    shifted_norm <- function(x) dnorm(x, mean = 5, sd = 2)\n    samples <- ars(shifted_norm, n = 3000, lb = -Inf, ub = Inf)\n    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(m, 4), \" (expected ~5)\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(s, 4), \" (expected ~2)\\n\"))\n    ks <- ks.test(samples, \"pnorm\", mean = 5, sd = 2)\n    cat(paste0(\"  KS test p-value: \", round(ks$p.value, 4), \"\\n\"))\n    abs(m - 5) < 0.3 && abs(s - 2) < 0.3 && ks$p.value > 0.01\n  })\n\n  # ---- Sampling Tests: Exponential ----\n  cat(\"--- Exponential Distribution Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_exponential_sampling\", {\n    cat(\"  Sampling 5000 points from Exp(1) on [0.001, 100]...\\n\")\n    set.seed(99)\n    samples <- ars(dexp, c(0.001, 100), n = 5000)\n    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(m, 4), \" (expected ~1)\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(s, 4), \" (expected ~1)\\n\"))\n    writeLines(as.character(samples), \"/app/exponential_samples.txt\")\n    cat(\"  Saved samples to /app/exponential_samples.txt\\n\")\n    ks <- ks.test(samples, \"pexp\")\n    cat(paste0(\"  KS test p-value: \", round(ks$p.value, 4), \"\\n\"))\n    abs(m - 1) < 0.2 && ks$p.value > 0.01\n  })\n\n  # ---- Sampling Tests: Gamma ----\n  cat(\"--- Gamma Distribution Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_gamma_sampling\", {\n    cat(\"  Sampling 3000 points from Gamma(3, 1) on [0.01, 30]...\\n\")\n    set.seed(77)\n    gamma_dens <- function(x) dgamma(x, shape = 3, rate = 1)\n    samples <- ars(gamma_dens, c(0.01, 30), n = 3000)\n    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(m, 4), \" (expected ~3)\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(s, 4), \" (expected ~1.73)\\n\"))\n    ks <- ks.test(samples, \"pgamma\", shape = 3, rate = 1)\n    cat(paste0(\"  KS test p-value: \", round(ks$p.value, 4), \"\\n\"))\n    abs(m - 3) < 0.4 && ks$p.value > 0.01\n  })\n\n  # ---- Sampling Tests: Beta ----\n  cat(\"--- Beta Distribution Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_beta_sampling\", {\n    cat(\"  Sampling 3000 points from Beta(2, 5) on [0.01, 0.99]...\\n\")\n    set.seed(55)\n    beta_dens <- function(x) dbeta(x, shape1 = 2, shape2 = 5)\n    samples <- ars(beta_dens, c(0.01, 0.99), n = 3000)\n    expected_mean <- 2 / (2 + 5)\n    expected_sd <- sqrt(2 * 5 / ((2 + 5)^2 * (2 + 5 + 1)))\n    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(m, 4),\n               \" (expected ~\", round(expected_mean, 4), \")\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(s, 4),\n               \" (expected ~\", round(expected_sd, 4), \")\\n\"))\n    ks <- ks.test(samples, \"pbeta\", shape1 = 2, shape2 = 5)\n    cat(paste0(\"  KS test p-value: \", round(ks$p.value, 4), \"\\n\"))\n    abs(m - expected_mean) < 0.05 && ks$p.value > 0.01\n  })\n\n  # ---- Sampling Tests: Unnormalized density ----\n  cat(\"--- Unnormalized Density Test ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_unnormalized_density\", {\n    cat(\"  Sampling from unnormalized Gaussian: f(x) = exp(-x^2/2)...\\n\")\n    set.seed(33)\n    unnorm_gauss <- function(x) exp(-x^2 / 2)\n    samples <- ars(unnorm_gauss, n = 3000, lb = -Inf, ub = Inf)\n    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(m, 4), \" (expected ~0)\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(s, 4), \" (expected ~1)\\n\"))\n    ks <- ks.test(samples, \"pnorm\")\n    cat(paste0(\"  KS test p-value: \", round(ks$p.value, 4), \"\\n\"))\n    abs(m) < 0.15 && abs(s - 1) < 0.15 && ks$p.value > 0.01\n  })\n\n  # ---- Summary ----\n  cat(\"============================================================\\n\")\n  total <- pass_count + fail_count\n  cat(paste0(\"  TOTAL: \", total, \" tests, \",\n             pass_count, \" passed, \", fail_count, \" failed\\n\"))\n  cat(\"============================================================\\n\")\n\n  invisible(list(passed = pass_count, failed = fail_count))\n}\n\n# Run tests if this script is executed directly\nif (sys.nframe() == 0) {\n  test()\n}\n"}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Mb5LhzWyNXnMwmWBr3WFbu","type":"tool_result","content":"The file /app/ars.R has been updated successfully."}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"78903e02-0918-48fe-a339-296475e92493","uuid":"3adad503-0f0a-4d13-8481-663c218f7c12","tool_use_result":{"filePath":"/app/ars.R","oldString":"    # Ensure breakpoints are valid\n    bp[is.na(bp) | is.nan(bp)] <- 0\n    z <- c(lb, bp, ub)\n  }","newString":"    # Ensure breakpoints are valid\n    bp[is.na(bp) | is.nan(bp)] <- 0\n    z <- c(lb, bp, ub)\n    # Sort to handle floating-point precision issues\n    z <- sort(z)\n  }","originalFile":"###############################################################################\n# Adaptive Rejection Sampling (ARS)\n# Based on Gilks, W.R. & Wild, P. (1992)\n# \"Adaptive Rejection Sampling for Gibbs Sampling\"\n# Journal of the Royal Statistical Society: Series C, 41(2), 337-348.\n#\n# Derivative-free implementation using secant-based upper hull construction.\n###############################################################################\n\n# --- Auxiliary / Module Functions ---\n\n#' Evaluate the log of a density function safely\n#' @param f density function (possibly unnormalized)\n#' @param x point(s) at which to evaluate\n#' @return log(f(x)), with protection against f(x) <= 0\neval_log_density <- function(f, x) {\n  fx <- f(x)\n  if (any(is.na(fx) | is.nan(fx))) {\n    stop(\"Density function returned NA/NaN.\")\n  }\n  if (any(fx < 0)) {\n    stop(\"Density function returned negative value.\")\n  }\n  result <- rep(-Inf, length(fx))\n  pos <- fx > 0\n  result[pos] <- log(fx[pos])\n  return(result)\n}\n\n#' Check log-concavity at three consecutive abscissae\n#' @param abscissae sorted vector of x values\n#' @param h_vals corresponding log-density values h(x)\n#' @return TRUE if log-concave, else stops with error\ncheck_log_concavity <- function(abscissae, h_vals) {\n  n <- length(abscissae)\n  if (n < 3) return(TRUE)\n  for (i in 1:(n - 2)) {\n    x1 <- abscissae[i];   h1 <- h_vals[i]\n    x2 <- abscissae[i+1]; h2 <- h_vals[i+1]\n    x3 <- abscissae[i+2]; h3 <- h_vals[i+2]\n    slope_left  <- (h2 - h1) / (x2 - x1)\n    slope_right <- (h3 - h2) / (x3 - x2)\n    if (slope_right > slope_left + 1e-10) {\n      stop(paste0(\n        \"Log-concavity violated at x = \", x2,\n        \". Left slope = \", round(slope_left, 6),\n        \", Right slope = \", round(slope_right, 6),\n        \". The density may not be log-concave.\"\n      ))\n    }\n  }\n  return(TRUE)\n}\n\n#' Compute the upper hull for the ARS algorithm (derivative-free version)\n#'\n#' Constructs a piecewise-linear upper envelope of the log-density using\n#' the secant slopes between consecutive abscissae.\n#'\n#' At each interior abscissa x_j (j = 2,...,k-1), the upper hull forms a\n#' \"tent\" with left arm slope s_j and right arm slope s_{j-1}, where\n#' s_j = (h_{j+1} - h_j) / (x_{j+1} - x_j).\n#'\n#' @param abscissae sorted x values (at least 3)\n#' @param h_vals corresponding h(x) = log(f(x)) values\n#' @param lb lower bound of domain\n#' @param ub upper bound of domain\n#' @return list with z (breakpoints), seg_slopes, seg_intercepts, n_seg\ncompute_upper_hull <- function(abscissae, h_vals, lb, ub) {\n  k <- length(abscissae)\n  if (k < 3) stop(\"Need at least 3 abscissae for upper hull construction.\")\n\n  # Secant slopes\n  s <- diff(h_vals) / diff(abscissae)\n\n  # Build segments: each defined by intercept and slope in form u(x) = b + m*x\n  # Segments from left to right:\n  #   1. x_1: slope s[1] (only arm, extends left)\n  #   For j = 2,...,k-1: left arm slope s[j], right arm slope s[j-1]\n  #   Last: x_k: slope s[k-1] (only arm, extends right)\n\n  seg_px <- numeric(0)  # reference x\n  seg_ph <- numeric(0)  # reference h\n  seg_m  <- numeric(0)  # slope\n\n  # First segment\n  seg_px <- c(seg_px, abscissae[1])\n  seg_ph <- c(seg_ph, h_vals[1])\n  seg_m  <- c(seg_m, s[1])\n\n  # Interior points\n  for (j in 2:(k-1)) {\n    # Left arm\n    seg_px <- c(seg_px, abscissae[j])\n    seg_ph <- c(seg_ph, h_vals[j])\n    seg_m  <- c(seg_m, s[j])\n    # Right arm\n    seg_px <- c(seg_px, abscissae[j])\n    seg_ph <- c(seg_ph, h_vals[j])\n    seg_m  <- c(seg_m, s[j-1])\n  }\n\n  # Last segment\n  seg_px <- c(seg_px, abscissae[k])\n  seg_ph <- c(seg_ph, h_vals[k])\n  seg_m  <- c(seg_m, s[k-1])\n\n  # Convert to intercept form: u(x) = seg_int + seg_m * x\n  seg_int <- seg_ph - seg_m * seg_px\n\n  # Merge consecutive segments that are effectively the same line\n  # (happens when log-density is linear, e.g. exponential distribution)\n  keep <- rep(TRUE, length(seg_m))\n  for (i in 2:length(seg_m)) {\n    if (abs(seg_m[i] - seg_m[i-1]) < 1e-12 && abs(seg_int[i] - seg_int[i-1]) < 1e-6) {\n      keep[i] <- FALSE\n    }\n  }\n  seg_px <- seg_px[keep]\n  seg_ph <- seg_ph[keep]\n  seg_m  <- seg_m[keep]\n  seg_int <- seg_int[keep]\n  n_seg <- length(seg_m)\n\n  # Compute breakpoints between consecutive segments\n  if (n_seg == 1) {\n    z <- c(lb, ub)\n  } else {\n    bp <- numeric(n_seg - 1)\n    for (i in 1:(n_seg - 1)) {\n      if (seg_px[i] == seg_px[i+1]) {\n        # Same reference point (left/right arms of a tent)\n        bp[i] <- seg_px[i]\n      } else if (abs(seg_m[i] - seg_m[i+1]) < 1e-12) {\n        # Parallel lines: use midpoint of reference points\n        bp[i] <- (seg_px[i] + seg_px[i+1]) / 2\n      } else {\n        bp[i] <- (seg_int[i+1] - seg_int[i]) / (seg_m[i] - seg_m[i+1])\n      }\n    }\n    # Ensure breakpoints are valid\n    bp[is.na(bp) | is.nan(bp)] <- 0\n    z <- c(lb, bp, ub)\n  }\n\n  return(list(\n    z = z,\n    seg_slopes = seg_m,\n    seg_intercepts = seg_int,\n    n_seg = n_seg\n  ))\n}\n\n#' Evaluate the upper hull at a point x\n#' @param x scalar or vector of points\n#' @param hull list from compute_upper_hull\n#' @return u(x), the upper hull value(s)\neval_upper_hull <- function(x, hull) {\n  z <- hull$z\n  m <- hull$seg_slopes\n  b <- hull$seg_intercepts\n  n_seg <- hull$n_seg\n\n  seg_idx <- findInterval(x, z, rightmost.closed = TRUE)\n  seg_idx <- pmax(1, pmin(seg_idx, n_seg))\n\n  return(b[seg_idx] + m[seg_idx] * x)\n}\n\n#' Evaluate the lower hull (piecewise linear interpolation) at a point x\n#' @param x scalar point\n#' @param abscissae sorted x values\n#' @param h_vals corresponding h(x) values\n#' @return l(x). Returns -Inf if outside the range of abscissae.\neval_lower_hull <- function(x, abscissae, h_vals) {\n  k <- length(abscissae)\n  if (x <= abscissae[1] || x >= abscissae[k]) {\n    return(-Inf)\n  }\n  j <- findInterval(x, abscissae)\n  j <- pmax(1, pmin(j, k - 1))\n  t <- (x - abscissae[j]) / (abscissae[j+1] - abscissae[j])\n  return(h_vals[j] * (1 - t) + h_vals[j+1] * t)\n}\n\n#' Sample from the upper hull (piecewise-exponential distribution)\n#' @param n number of samples to draw\n#' @param hull list from compute_upper_hull\n#' @return vector of n samples\nsample_upper_hull <- function(n, hull) {\n  z <- hull$z\n  m <- hull$seg_slopes\n  b <- hull$seg_intercepts\n  n_seg <- hull$n_seg\n\n  # Compute area under each segment: integral of exp(b + m*x) from z[j] to z[j+1]\n  areas <- numeric(n_seg)\n  for (j in 1:n_seg) {\n    lo <- z[j]\n    hi <- z[j + 1]\n\n    if (!is.finite(lo)) {\n      if (m[j] <= 0) { areas[j] <- 0; next }\n      areas[j] <- exp(b[j] + m[j] * hi) / m[j]\n      next\n    }\n    if (!is.finite(hi)) {\n      if (m[j] >= 0) { areas[j] <- 0; next }\n      areas[j] <- -exp(b[j] + m[j] * lo) / m[j]\n      next\n    }\n\n    if (abs(m[j]) < 1e-15) {\n      areas[j] <- exp(b[j]) * (hi - lo)\n    } else {\n      log_hi <- b[j] + m[j] * hi\n      log_lo <- b[j] + m[j] * lo\n      log_max <- max(log_hi, log_lo)\n      areas[j] <- exp(log_max) * (exp(log_hi - log_max) - exp(log_lo - log_max)) / m[j]\n    }\n\n    if (is.na(areas[j]) || !is.finite(areas[j]) || areas[j] < 0) {\n      areas[j] <- 0\n    }\n  }\n\n  total_area <- sum(areas)\n  if (total_area <= 0 || !is.finite(total_area)) {\n    stop(\"Upper hull has zero or infinite area. Check domain bounds and initial abscissae.\")\n  }\n  probs <- areas / total_area\n\n  # Sample segments according to their areas\n  seg_choices <- sample.int(n_seg, size = n, replace = TRUE, prob = probs)\n\n  # For each chosen segment, sample from truncated exponential via inverse CDF\n  samples <- numeric(n)\n  for (i in 1:n) {\n    j <- seg_choices[i]\n    lo <- z[j]\n    hi <- z[j + 1]\n    u <- runif(1)\n\n    if (abs(m[j]) < 1e-15) {\n      if (!is.finite(lo)) lo <- hi - 20\n      if (!is.finite(hi)) hi <- lo + 20\n      samples[i] <- lo + u * (hi - lo)\n    } else if (!is.finite(lo)) {\n      # lo = -Inf, m > 0: inverse CDF gives x = hi + log(u) / m\n      samples[i] <- hi + log(u) / m[j]\n    } else if (!is.finite(hi)) {\n      # hi = +Inf, m < 0: inverse CDF gives x = lo + log(u) / m (m < 0)\n      samples[i] <- lo + log(u) / m[j]\n    } else {\n      log_lo <- m[j] * lo\n      log_hi <- m[j] * hi\n      log_max <- max(log_lo, log_hi)\n      exp_lo <- exp(log_lo - log_max)\n      exp_hi <- exp(log_hi - log_max)\n      val <- exp_lo + u * (exp_hi - exp_lo)\n      if (val <= 0) val <- 1e-300\n      samples[i] <- (log_max + log(val)) / m[j]\n    }\n  }\n  return(samples)\n}\n\n#' Choose initial abscissae for the ARS algorithm\n#' @param lb lower bound\n#' @param ub upper bound\n#' @param f density function\n#' @return vector of initial abscissae (at least 3, with required slope conditions)\nchoose_initial_abscissae <- function(lb, ub, f) {\n  # Generate candidate points\n  if (is.finite(lb) && is.finite(ub)) {\n    margin <- (ub - lb) * 0.02\n    pts <- seq(lb + margin, ub - margin, length.out = 20)\n  } else if (is.finite(lb) && !is.finite(ub)) {\n    pts <- lb + c(0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.2, 0.5, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 12, 18, 25, 40, 60)\n  } else if (!is.finite(lb) && is.finite(ub)) {\n    pts <- ub - c(0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.2, 0.5, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 12, 18, 25, 40, 60)\n    pts <- sort(pts)\n  } else {\n    pts <- seq(-5, 5, length.out = 21)\n  }\n\n  pts <- sort(unique(pts))\n\n  # Keep only points with positive density\n  vals <- f(pts)\n  good <- which(vals > 0 & !is.na(vals) & is.finite(vals))\n  if (length(good) < 3) {\n    stop(\"Cannot find at least 3 initial abscissae with positive density. Check domain bounds.\")\n  }\n  pts <- pts[good]\n  h <- log(vals[good])\n\n  # For infinite left bound: trim until first secant slope > 0\n  if (!is.finite(lb)) {\n    while (length(pts) > 3) {\n      s1 <- (h[2] - h[1]) / (pts[2] - pts[1])\n      if (s1 > 1e-12) break\n      pts <- pts[-1]; h <- h[-1]\n    }\n    if ((h[2] - h[1]) / (pts[2] - pts[1]) <= 0) {\n      stop(\"Cannot find initial points where log-density is increasing from the left. Check domain.\")\n    }\n  }\n\n  # For infinite right bound: trim until last secant slope < 0\n  if (!is.finite(ub)) {\n    while (length(pts) > 3) {\n      k <- length(pts)\n      sk <- (h[k] - h[k-1]) / (pts[k] - pts[k-1])\n      if (sk < -1e-12) break\n      pts <- pts[-k]; h <- h[-k]\n    }\n    k <- length(pts)\n    if ((h[k] - h[k-1]) / (pts[k] - pts[k-1]) >= 0) {\n      stop(\"Cannot find initial points where log-density is decreasing on the right. Check domain.\")\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (length(pts) < 3) {\n    stop(\"Cannot find suitable initial abscissae.\")\n  }\n\n  # Subsample to avoid too many initial points (keep ~5-8)\n  if (length(pts) > 8) {\n    idx <- round(seq(1, length(pts), length.out = 8))\n    # But always keep first and last\n    idx <- sort(unique(c(1, idx, length(pts))))\n    pts <- pts[idx]\n  }\n\n  return(pts)\n}\n\n#' Validate inputs to the ars function\nvalidate_inputs <- function(f, n, lb, ub) {\n  if (!is.function(f)) {\n    stop(\"'f' must be a function.\")\n  }\n  if (!is.numeric(n) || length(n) != 1 || n < 1 || n != as.integer(n)) {\n    stop(\"'n' must be a positive integer.\")\n  }\n  if (!is.numeric(lb) || length(lb) != 1) {\n    stop(\"'lb' must be a single numeric value.\")\n  }\n  if (!is.numeric(ub) || length(ub) != 1) {\n    stop(\"'ub' must be a single numeric value.\")\n  }\n  if (lb >= ub) {\n    stop(\"Lower bound must be strictly less than upper bound.\")\n  }\n}\n\n# --- Main ARS Function ---\n\n#' Adaptive Rejection Sampling\n#'\n#' @param f density function (possibly unnormalized), must be log-concave.\n#' @param support optional 2-element vector c(lb, ub) for the arguments (currently unused)\n#' @return vector of n samples from the target distribution\nars <- function(f, support = NULL, n = 100, lb = -Inf, ub = Inf, ...) {\n  # Handle the support vector choose_initial_abscissae(lb, ub, f)\n  h_vals <- eval_log_density(f, abscissae)\n  check_log_concavity(abscissae, h_vals)\n\n  samples <- numeric(n)\n  count <- 0\n  max_iter <- n * 200\n  iter <- 0\n\n  while (count < n && iter < max_iter) {\n    iter <- iter + 1\n\n    hull <- compute_upper_hull(abscissae, h_vals, lb, ub)\n    x_star <- sample_upper_hull(1, hull)\n\n    u <- runif(1)\n    u_val <- eval_upper_hull(x_star, hull)\n    l_val <- eval_lower_hull(x_star, abscissae, h_vals)\n\n    if (u <= exp(l_val - u_val)) {\n      count <- count + 1\n      samples[count] <- x_star\n    } else {\n      h_star <- eval_log_density(f, x_star)\n\n      if (u <= exp(h_star - u_val)) {\n        count <- count + 1\n        samples[count] <- x_star\n      }\n\n      # Add new point to abscissae\n      insert_pos <- findInterval(x_star, abscissae) + 1\n      abscissae <- append(abscissae, x_star, after = insert_pos - 1)\n      h_vals <- append(h_vals, h_star, after = insert_pos - 1)\n\n      check_log_concavity(abscissae, h_vals)\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (count < n) {\n    warning(paste(\"Only generated\", count, \"out of\", n, \"requested samples.\"))\n  }\n\n  return(samples)\n}\n\n# --- Test Function ---\n\n#' Run formal tests of the ARS implementation\ntest <- function() {\n  cat(\"============================================================\\n\")\n
